prompt.instructions: Extract employer and plan information fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.description: Annual auto-escalation increment. Expressed as decimal.
identifiers: Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return the numeric decimal value for the annual auto-escalation increment, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Convert percentages to decimals.

Label discovery:
- Find the Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase subsection under ACA/EACA/QACA automatic enrollment.
- This subsection can appear under item 10 ACA and EACA after the plan-level automatic enrollment election.
- Do not use QACA safe harbor contribution limits, ACP/ADP safe harbor limits, or "six-percent of Compensation" contribution-limit text for this field.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected increase option and read the filled percent blank in that selected option.
- A selected Yes/increase row with blanks for annual percent and maximum percent is the controlling source for both the annual increase and the maximum cap.
- Filled-in numbers printed in that selected increase row are the values for the blanks. Do not treat them as placeholders and do not return null.
- If the page image shows numbers printed in the blanks of the selected increase sentence, use those numbers even when OCR splits them away from the sentence.
- The first filled percent in the selected increase sentence is the annual increase. The second filled percent is the maximum cap.
- Do not return null merely because the OCR separates the filled number from the sentence. Use the image to connect the number to the selected increase row.
- OCR may put the filled numbers on lines immediately before the selected increase sentence. If the page image shows those numbers fill that selected row, use the first number as the annual increase percent.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text omits checkbox symbols. Do not return null when the image shows the increase row selected and the option text contains the filled percent.
- Ignore unselected increase percentages.

Value mapping:
- Convert the selected annual increase percent to a decimal by dividing by 100.
- Return null when auto-escalation is not selected, automatic enrollment is not applicable, or the current page/request lacks the increase subsection.

Source evidence:
- Do not use the default automatic deferral percentage or maximum cap as the annual increase.

prompt.description: Automatic enrollment as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Applicable", "QACA Automatic Enrollment", "EACA Automatic Enrollment"].

Label discovery:
- Find item 9(a), Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferrals. It asks whether the automatic Elective Deferral enrollment provisions apply.
- Treat item 9(a) as the plan-level automatic enrollment election.
- Later ACA/EACA subsections describe which employees, amounts, increases, and timing apply after item 9(a) has already selected ACA/EACA/QACA.

Selection evidence:
- Use only the selected checkbox or marked option in item 9(a) to decide this field.
- If item 9(a) is visible and one ACA, EACA, QACA, or No row has a checked/filled square, return that selected row. Do not return null from that page.
- The controlling source is item "Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferrals", especially the question "Will the automatic Elective Deferral enrollment provisions apply".
- If item 9(a) selects the Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement (EACA) row, return EACA even if a QACA subsection appears later on the following pages.
- Treat later QACA details as active only when the plan-level automatic-enrollment election selected the Qualified Automatic Contribution Arrangement (QACA) row.
- In reconcile or QA, preserve a candidate whose raw text cites item 9(a) and a selected ACA/EACA/QACA row over a later candidate that only cites QACA explanation text.
- Do not infer QACA from references to Plan Section 3.01(F), safe harbor formulas, or QACA requirements unless the QACA row in item 9(a) is visibly selected.
- Do not use item 10 Current Employees, New Employees, default percent, increase percent, or timing options to decide whether automatic enrollment applies at the plan level.
- A selected "No" inside item 10 Current Employees means current employees are not covered by that subsection. It does not override item 9(a) when item 9(a) selected ACA, EACA, or QACA.
- Ignore nearby unselected EACA/QACA labels and safe harbor contribution text.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option says Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement or EACA, return value "EACA Automatic Enrollment".
- If the selected option says Qualified Automatic Contribution Arrangement or QACA, return value "QACA Automatic Enrollment".
- If the selected option says No automatic enrollment or automatic elective deferrals do not apply, return value "Not Applicable".

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected option label and automatic-enrollment wording in _raw_text.
- Do not use an EACA election as evidence of a QACA safe harbor contribution.
- Do not use safe harbor contribution language as evidence for this field.

prompt.description: Default deferral percentage for automatically enrolled employees. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.03 = 3%).
prompt.instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when automatic_enrollment_type.value does not equal "Not Applicable". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Default enrollment percentage; Automatic deferral percentage.

prompt.description: Automatic enrollment eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["All Employees", "Newly Eligible", "Participants not contributing", "Affirmative Election of at least Automatic Deferral amount", "No existing Affirmative Election", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the ACA/EACA/QACA subsection that asks which employees are subject to automatic enrollment.
- Relevant labels may say New Employees, employees who meet eligibility requirements on or after the Effective Date, existing participants, participants not contributing, or affirmative election.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkbox or marked option in the automatic-enrollment eligible-employees option group.
- Ignore general eligibility text outside the automatic-enrollment section unless the selected option points to it.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option applies to employees who meet eligibility requirements on or after the Effective Date, return value "Newly Eligible".
- If the selected option applies to all eligible employees, return value "All Employees".
- If the selected option applies to participants not contributing, return value "Participants not contributing".
- If the selected wording is not captured by one enum, return value "Other" and preserve the full selected wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected option and governed employee population text in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when eligible_employees.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Eligible Employees Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

prompt.description: Escalation timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["First day of each succeeding plan year", "Anniversary date of when participant's automatic enrollment was implemented", "First day of the second plan year after automatic deferrals were first implemented", "Every January", "Every February", "Every March", "Every April", "Every May", "Every June", "Every July", "Every August", "Every September", "Every October", "Every November", "Every December", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Only populate this field when automatic_enrollment_type.value does not equal "Not Applicable". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Escalation timing; Automatic increase timing.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when escalation_timing resolves to 'Other'.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when escalation_timing.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Escalation Timing Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

prompt.description: Maximum deferral percentage under auto-escalation. Expressed as decimal.
identifiers: Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return the numeric decimal value for the maximum automatic enrollment or auto-escalation cap, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Convert percentages to decimals.

Label discovery:
- Find the Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase subsection under ACA/EACA/QACA automatic enrollment.
- This subsection can appear under item 10 ACA and EACA after the plan-level automatic enrollment election.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected increase option and read the maximum percent in that selected option.
- A selected Yes/increase row with blanks for annual percent and maximum percent is the controlling source for both the annual increase and the maximum cap.
- Filled-in numbers printed in that selected increase row are the values for the blanks. Do not treat them as placeholders and do not return null.
- If the page image shows numbers printed in the blanks of the selected increase sentence, use those numbers even when OCR splits them away from the sentence.
- The second filled percent in the selected increase sentence is the maximum cap. The first filled percent is the annual increase.
- Do not return null merely because the OCR separates the filled number from the sentence. Use the image to connect the number to the selected increase row.
- OCR may put the filled numbers on lines immediately before the selected increase sentence. If the page image shows those numbers fill that selected row, use the second number as the maximum percent.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text omits checkbox symbols. Do not return null when the image shows the increase row selected and the option text contains the filled maximum percent.
- Ignore maximum values in unselected options.

Value mapping:
- Convert the selected maximum percent to a decimal by dividing by 100.
- Return null when auto-escalation is not selected, automatic enrollment is not applicable, or the current page/request lacks the increase subsection.

Source evidence:
- Do not use the annual increase percent as the maximum cap.
- Do not use the six-percent QACA/safe-harbor contribution cap as the maximum automatic enrollment cap.

prompt.description: Window within which a newly auto-enrolled participant may withdraw their automatic contributions..
identifiers: Permissible Withdrawals of EACA or QACA Elective Deferrals
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not permitted", "Permitted within 30 days of participant's automatic enrollment", "Permitted within 60 days of participant's automatic enrollment", "Permitted within 90 days of participant's automatic enrollment"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Permissible Withdrawals of EACA or QACA Elective Deferrals section.
- Use the Authorization of Permissible Withdrawals subsection only to decide whether withdrawals are permitted.
- Use the Permissible Withdrawal Period subsection to choose the number of days.
- The deciding sentence often begins "A Participant's election to make a permissible withdrawal must be made within"; use the selected period row governed by that sentence.

Selection evidence:
- Treat authorization and period as two separate option groups, even when both reuse the same numbering or row labels.
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected row inside the Permissible Withdrawal Period subsection.
- A checkmark, filled box, or X immediately before or after a period option label selects that period row when the page image confirms the row alignment.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected period option label; treat the following option row as selected when it is visually aligned with that mark.
- When the standalone mark appears between two period option labels, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- The selected mark may appear on the option-label line while the day-count text continues on the next line; read the whole selected row.
- Ignore unselected period rows, Form of Distribution options, and default notes when any period row is visibly selected.
- Use a default period only when every row in the Permissible Withdrawal Period subsection is visibly unselected. Do not choose 30 days from the default note when a later period row has a visible selected mark.

Value mapping:
- If the selected authorization option says No, return value "Not permitted".
- If the selected period is 30, 60, or 90 days following the first automatic contribution, return the matching enum.
- Return null when automatic enrollment is not applicable or the current page/request does not contain the permissible-withdrawal section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected authorization and period option text in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Effective date of QACA or EACA automatic enrollment. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return the date as MM/DD/YYYY, or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the automatic-enrollment election and determine whether ACA, EACA, or QACA is selected.
- Also find Section One, Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date, especially "The Initial Plan Document was effective on".
- Look for a specific ACA/EACA/QACA effective date. If no separate date exists, use the Initial Plan Document effective date when the automatic-enrollment provisions apply from the plan start.
- Do not use the amendment/restatement effective date when the Initial Plan Document effective date is visible and there is no separate automatic-enrollment effective date.

Selection evidence:
- Automatic enrollment must be selected somewhere in the document before the final value can be populated.
- In page-level extraction, do not reject the Initial Plan Document effective date just because the automatic-enrollment election is on another page.
- During final reconcile or QA, combine the selected ACA/EACA/QACA election with the Initial Plan Document effective date even when those facts came from different page-level candidates.
- A plan date can be used only when no separate automatic-enrollment effective date is printed.
- If the request includes full document page images, use them to connect the selected EACA/ACA/QACA election to the Initial Plan Document effective date.

Value mapping:
- If a specific EACA/QACA effective date is printed, return that date.
- If selected ACA/EACA/QACA applies from the plan start and no separate date is printed, return the Initial Plan Document effective date.
- Return null when the current page/request lacks both automatic-enrollment evidence and source-date evidence.

Source evidence:
- Do not infer a date from signature dates, restatement dates, payroll timing, or unrelated contribution timing.

prompt.description: Compensation definition as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["W2 Compensation", "415 Compensation", "3401(a) Compensation", "W2 excluding pre-tax contributions", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the definition of compensation section or the contribution-source compensation table.
- Relevant labels include W-2 Wages, 415 Compensation, 3401(a) Wages, and contribution-source columns.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected checkboxes, marks, or filled cells in the compensation table.
- If the table has contribution-source columns, read the selected row and the applicable contribution-source columns together.

Value mapping:
- Map W-2, W2, Form W-2 wages, or a selected W-2 Wages row to value "W2 Compensation".
- Map selected 415 compensation language to value "415 Compensation".
- Map selected 3401(a) wages language to value "3401(a) Compensation".
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the compensation definition label but no selected definition is visible. If the compensation definition section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected compensation definition row and relevant contribution-source marks in _raw_text.
- Do not use exclusion-from-compensation rows as the base compensation definition.

prompt.description: Compensation components excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON array string whose entries are objects with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Expense Allowances and Fringe Benefits", "Bonus", "Commissions", "Bonus and Commissions", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the exclusion from compensation / general exclusions section.
- Relevant rows list categories excluded from compensation for all contribution types.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected rows and selected contribution-source columns together.
- If the row says Not applicable for all relevant contribution sources, treat that as no general exclusions.

Value mapping:
- If no compensation exclusions apply for all contribution types, return an array containing value "N/A".
- If bonus, commissions, expense allowances, or fringe benefits are selected as excluded for all contribution types, return the matching enum values.
- If selected exclusions are different, return value "Other" and preserve the selected wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected exclusion row labels and relevant column marks in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Compensation components excluded for Elective Deferral purposes only.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON array string whose entries are objects with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Same as All Contributions", "Expense Allowances and Fringe Benefits", "Bonus", "Commissions", "Bonus and Commissions", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Exclusion from Compensation / General Exclusions table.
- Read the Elective Deferrals column, not the Matching Contributions or Employer Profit Sharing Contributions columns.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected row/column intersections in the Elective Deferrals column.
- An "X" in row "a. Not applicable." under the Elective Deferrals column means Not applicable is selected for Elective Deferrals.
- ADP compensation-exclusion tables may use an "X" in the table cell instead of a checkbox. Treat the X as the selected mark for that row and column.
- Do not use unselected row labels or selected values in other contribution-source columns.

Value mapping:
- If Elective Deferrals has Not applicable selected, return a single entry with value "N/A".
- If the same exclusion rows are selected across all contribution columns, return a single entry with value "Same as All Contributions".
- Map selected bonus, commission, and expense/fringe-benefit rows to the matching enum entries.
- If a selected Elective Deferrals exclusion is not covered by the enum, return value "Other" with the exact source row in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the compensation exclusion table.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected row labels and Elective Deferrals column evidence in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Compensation for initial year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Compensation from entry date", "Compensation for full plan year", "Same as Deferral", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the pre-entry date compensation or initial-year compensation section.
- Relevant labels ask whether compensation before the employee's entry date is included or excluded.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected checkboxes, marked rows, or filled contribution-source columns in the pre-entry date compensation table.
- Read the selected row together with the relevant contribution-source columns.

Value mapping:
- If the selected row says Compensation from Entry Date, return value "Compensation from entry date".
- If the selected row says compensation for the full plan year or includes pre-entry compensation, return value "Compensation for full plan year".
- If the document explicitly says the initial-year rule is the same as deferral, return value "Same as Deferral".
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve the wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected row label and relevant column marks in _raw_text.

prompt.instructions:

- Use source labels such as: Compensation For Initial Year Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

Extract compensation fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.description: Canonical Nondeductible Employee Contributions flag carried from the Eligibility Requirements / plan setup surface. This is not Roth elective deferrals.
identifiers: May a Contributing Participant make Nondeductible Employee Contributions
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section. In this ADP document family, nondeductible employee contributions are the source label for after-tax voluntary employee contributions.
- Do not use rollover, Roth elective deferral, top-heavy, or generic "if applicable" references as after-tax contribution evidence.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected answer row in the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following option row.
- A selected No row controls this field and means nondeductible employee contributions are not allowed.
- A selected Yes or allowed row means nondeductible employee contributions are allowed when the row governs that same question.
- This field mirrors employee_contributions.after_tax_voluntary_contributions. During reconcile or QA, keep these two values aligned to the Nondeductible Employee Contributions election unless the source document clearly contradicts itself.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected Nondeductible Employee Contributions answer permits nondeductible employee contributions.
- Return "false" when the selected answer says No/not permitted, or when the section says the No/not-permitted answer applies by default when nothing else is selected.
- During reconcile or QA across multiple page candidates, keep "false" when the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section selected No/not permitted. Do not flip this field to "true" unless the same question has a selected answer permitting nondeductible employee contributions.
- A "true" candidate that does not cite the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question is lower priority than a "false" candidate that cites selected No/not permitted.
- If any candidate for this same source question extracted selected No/not permitted, treat later generic employee-contribution evidence as lower priority. A true value requires selected Yes/allowed evidence from the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- If the same page has Rollover Contributions from IRAs selected Yes and Nondeductible Employee Contributions selected No, return "false" for this field. Rollover evidence is not after-tax contribution evidence.
- Roth elective deferrals may be true while Nondeductible Employee Contributions are false. Do not copy a Roth true value into this field.
- If either mirrored after-tax field cites selected No/not permitted from the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question, both mirrored fields should resolve to "false" unless the same question selects Yes/allowed elsewhere.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section.

Source evidence:
- Use the exact selected option label and nearby question text as evidence.

Return "N/A" only when "no age requirement" is affirmatively selected for general eligibility, not merely because a contribution-specific N/A row appears.

prompt.description: Employee classes excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
identifiers: Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON array string whose entries are objects with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No exclusions", "Union Employees", "Nonresident Aliens", "Highly Compensated Employees", "Leased Employees", "Residents of Puerto Rico", "Interns", "Part-time Employees", "Temporary Employees", "Seasonal Employees", "Other"].
- Example shape: "[{\"value\":\"Union Employees\",\"_raw_text\":\"selected union employee class and selected contribution-source rows\"}]".

Label discovery:
- Find the Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees section, including rows that continue across pages.
- This field asks for classes excluded from all contribution types, not classes excluded only for one contribution source.

Selection evidence:
- For each excluded employee class, pair the selected class checkbox with the selected contribution-source checkboxes beneath it.
- ADP OCR may show a selected checkbox as "4", "x", a filled box, or a checked box before the class label or before each contribution-source line.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text is unclear. On ADP adoption agreements, the selected mark often appears immediately before the class label and immediately before each contribution-source line.
- If the OCR moves selected marks into a stack at the end of the page, use the page image row alignment instead of OCR order.
- OCR may show selected marks as repeated standalone lines before or beside the contribution-source labels. Use the page image to align those marks with the source rows instead of treating the standalone marks as missing labels.
- Treat a selected class plus selected contribution-source rows as real evidence even when the source text wraps across multiple lines or pages.
- When the page shows a selected excluded class followed by selected marks on every printed contribution-source row for that class, return that class even if OCR separates the marks from the labels.
- Do not require one continuous OCR sentence. Use the visual grouping: class label first, then the contribution-source rows governed by that class.
- A selected contribution-source row can be written as a continuation line under the class. If the selected rows collectively cover all listed contribution sources, include that class.
- In this ADP section, "all contribution types" means the full set of contribution-source rows printed under the selected class in Part B. Do not require proof from later plan sections before including the selected class.
- Include a class in this field when the class row is selected and every listed contribution-source line under that class is also selected: Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals and Safe Harbor Contributions, Roth Elective Deferrals, Matching Contributions, Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, and Qualified Nonelective Contributions.
- The ADP label "Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals and Safe Harbor Contributions" counts as the pre-tax/safe-harbor contribution-source line for this field.
- Do not require the source to say "all contributions" in one sentence. The visual evidence is enough when the selected class row has all printed contribution-source rows selected beneath it.
- When several selected excluded classes each have all contribution-source rows selected, return one array containing every selected class. Do not drop the whole field because the evidence spans multiple repeated row groups.
- Return every selected employee class that applies to all listed contribution sources. Do not return null just because more than one class is selected or because one selected class maps to "Other".
- If one selected class maps to "Other", still return the standard selected classes in the same JSON array string.
- A selected merger, acquisition, asset acquisition, stock acquisition, or Code section 410(b)(6)(C) class maps to "Other"; this should not cause the whole field to be null when other selected classes are also present.
- Do not return null when the Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees section is present and the page image shows selected marks for excluded classes and all contribution-source lines.
- If at least one selected excluded class has all contribution-source rows selected, return the supported selected classes. Do not require every printed class in the section to be selected.
- Page-level extraction should emit the selected classes visible on the current page. Final reconcile can merge repeated class evidence from other pages.
- When the page image shows several selected class blocks and each selected block has all listed contribution-source rows checked, return a single JSON array string with one entry per selected class.

Value mapping:
- Map selected union-employee language to "Union Employees".
- Treat "Employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement", "unit of Employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement", and employee-representative bargaining language as union-employee language.
- Map selected nonresident-alien language to "Nonresident Aliens".
- Map selected Code section 410(b)(6)(C), asset acquisition, stock acquisition, merger, or similar transaction language to "Other" and preserve the exact source text in _raw_text unless a closer enum exists.
- Return "No exclusions" only when the section selects no exclusions apply for all contribution sources.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the excluded-employee section.

Source evidence:
- Put each selected excluded class and its selected contribution-source scope in _raw_text.

- If a selected service requirement is based only on months/elapsed time with no hours requirement, return value "N/A" unless the schema has a closer value.
- Never return a month value such as "3 Months" for hours_requirement. Month values belong to service_requirement, not this field.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the eligibility service requirement section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected service requirement option and contribution-source rows in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Whether prior service with a predecessor employer is recognized.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Recognized - specify employers", "Same as Deferral"].

Label discovery:
- Find Part H, Predecessor Employer Service, or a section asking whether service with a predecessor employer will be credited.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes or No answer in the predecessor-service question.
- Do not treat merger/acquisition exclusion text as predecessor service recognition.

Value mapping:
- If the selected answer says No, or if the section says the No answer applies by default when nothing else is selected, return value "N/A".
- If the selected answer says predecessor service is recognized using the same rule as elective deferrals, return value "Same as Deferral" only when the document says that.
- If the selected answer says predecessor service is recognized and predecessor employers are specified, return value "Recognized - specify employers" and preserve the employer names in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Predecessor Employer Service section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected predecessor-service option and any named predecessor employer text in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Rollover contributions as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.
- Return "true" when the document permits or accepts rollover contributions, even if rollover contributions have separate eligibility rules.
- If the rollover contribution section asks whether employees may make rollover contributions and the selected answer is Yes, return "true".
- Return "false" only when rollover contributions are explicitly not permitted.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Rollover contributions; Rollover contribution eligibility.

prompt.description: Does the plan have different eligibility requirements for employer contributions vs. elective deferrals?
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the eligibility section that compares age, service, and entry-date requirements across Elective Deferrals, Matching Contributions, Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, Safe Harbor/QACA Safe Harbor Contributions, and Qualified Nonelective Contributions.
- This field asks whether employer contribution eligibility is different from elective-deferral eligibility. It is not asking for the age, service, or entry-date value itself.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkboxes or marked contribution-source rows for age, service, and entry dates.
- Do not treat the presence of separate contribution-source rows as proof of different eligibility. Compare the selected values across sources.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" only when selected employer-contribution eligibility requirements differ from the selected elective-deferral requirements.
- Return "false" only when the relevant eligibility rows are visible and the selected requirements are the same across elective deferrals and employer contributions, or a direct separate-eligibility question is selected No.
- Return "false" when the visible eligibility language states one shared age, service, or entry-date rule for elective deferrals, matching contributions, employer profit sharing, safe harbor contributions, and qualified nonelective contributions, with no separate employer-contribution rule selected.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the eligibility comparison needed to decide.

Source evidence:
- Preserve the selected eligibility rows in _raw_text when the output format includes raw text.
- Do not infer from unselected options or from generic contribution-source labels.

prompt.description: Service requirement for general plan eligibility.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Immediate", "30 Days", "60 Days", "90 Days", "1 Month", "2 Months", "3 Months", "4 Months", "5 Months", "6 Months", "1 Year", "2 Years", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Use Section Two, Part A Eligibility Requirements for general plan eligibility.
- Look for the selected service option that applies across contribution sources, such as "After completing __ consecutive Months of Eligibility Service."
- Do not use later employer-contribution allocation conditions, matching/profit-sharing last-day rules, vesting service rules, or withdrawal-service rules for this general eligibility field.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the service option and read the filled number of days, months, or years.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text shows the number but not the checkbox mark.
- If "After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service" is the selected general eligibility service option, this field is 3 Months.
- Do not return N/A when the general eligibility section contains a selected months-of-service option.

Value mapping:
- Map selected consecutive-month requirements to the matching month enum, such as "3 Months".
- Map selected no-service-required language to "Immediate" or "N/A" only when that no-service option is selected.
- If the selected wording is not covered by the enum, return value "Other" and preserve the wording in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current request lacks the Section Two general eligibility service section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected service option text and filled number in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when service_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when service_requirement.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other service requirement; Other - specify service requirement; Service Requirement Other Specify.

prompt.description: SIMPLE 401(k) contributions as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find Part K, SIMPLE 401(k) Provisions, or a question asking whether SIMPLE 401(k) provisions of the Plan apply.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes or No answer in the SIMPLE 401(k) provisions question.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected answer says SIMPLE 401(k) provisions apply.
- Return "false" when the selected answer says No/not applicable, or when the section says the No/not-applicable answer applies by default when nothing else is selected.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the SIMPLE 401(k) provisions section.

Source evidence:
- Use the exact selected SIMPLE 401(k) option text as evidence.

prompt.description: Maximum percentage cap for after-tax (voluntary) contributions. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.10 = 10%).. Only populated when after_tax_contributions is true.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when after_tax_contributions equals "true". Otherwise return null.
- This field is only for voluntary after-tax contribution limits. Do not use the elective deferral percentage range for this field.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: After-tax contribution percentage; Voluntary after-tax contribution limit.

Extract eligibility requirements fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.description: Mirror of eligibility_requirements.after_tax_contributions kept in the Employee Contributions section because the workflow surfaces the concept in both places. Values should match unless the document itself is contradictory.
identifiers: May a Contributing Participant make Nondeductible Employee Contributions
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Use the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question as the source for after-tax voluntary employee contributions.
- Do not use rollover contributions, Roth elective deferrals, top-heavy contributions, or generic employee-contribution text as evidence for this field.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected answer row in the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following option row.
- When the standalone mark appears between Yes rows and the No row, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- A selected No row controls this field even when other employee-contribution features are selected elsewhere.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected Nondeductible Employee Contributions answer permits nondeductible employee contributions.
- Return "false" when the selected answer says No/not permitted, or when the section says the No/not-permitted answer applies by default when nothing else is selected.
- During reconcile or QA, keep a source-backed "false" from selected No/not permitted unless another candidate cites selected Yes/allowed evidence from the same Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- Roth elective deferrals are separate from nondeductible employee contributions; do not convert a selected Roth option into "true" here.
- The document can permit Roth elective deferrals or rollovers while prohibiting nondeductible employee contributions; that combination is still "false" for this field.
- If eligibility_requirements.after_tax_contributions is source-backed false from the same Nondeductible Employee Contributions question, this mirror field should also be false.
- If either mirrored after-tax field cites selected No/not permitted from the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question, both mirrored fields should resolve to "false" unless the same question selects Yes/allowed elsewhere.
- Do not leave this mirror field blank when the current page/request contains a visible selected Nondeductible Employee Contributions option.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section.

Source evidence:
- Use the exact selected option label and nearby question text as evidence.

prompt.description: Maximum deferral limit expressed as percentage of compensation.
prompt.instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["1% to 100% of compensation subject to IRS limits", "1% to 92% of compensation subject to IRS limits", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- If the document's exact contribution range is not one of the listed enum ranges, return value "Other" and put the exact source range in _raw_text and contribution_limit_other_specify.
- Do not coerce a 0% minimum into a 1% minimum.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Elective deferral limit; Maximum deferral limit; Salary deferral limit; Contribution Limit.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when contribution_limit resolves to 'Other'.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when contribution_limit.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- When contribution_limit.value is "Other", preserve the exact source range, including the minimum and maximum percentages.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Contribution Limit Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

prompt.description: Method(s) by which employees may elect deferrals.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["% of Compensation Only", "Flat Dollar Only", "% of Compensation and Flat Dollar", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the elective deferral authorization or salary reduction agreement section that describes the form of employee deferral elections.
- Do not use contribution-limit text, catch-up contribution text, or dollar limits as the contribution method unless the selected option is explicitly about the election form.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected options that say the participant may elect a percentage of compensation, a flat dollar amount, or both.
- In Limits on Elective Deferrals, a selected row that says the deferral amount is a percentage of the participant's compensation means the contribution method is percentage of compensation only.
- Ignore unselected dollar-amount options in the same list.

Value mapping:
- Return "% of Compensation Only" when only a compensation percentage election is selected.
- Return "Flat Dollar Only" when only a dollar election is selected.
- Return "% of Compensation and Flat Dollar" when both are selected or the selected option allows either a dollar amount or percentage.
- Return "Not Indicated" when elective deferrals are allowed but the selected source only states limits or plan-administrator rules and does not state the election method.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain elective-deferral election-method evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected election-method wording in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Deferral change frequency as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Indicated", "Daily", "Every Pay Period", "Weekly", "Monthly", "Quarterly", "Semi-Annual", "Annual", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Changing Elective Deferral Amounts section. Do not use Ceasing Elective Deferrals or Return as a Contributing Participant unless the field specifically asks about those actions.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected option under Changing Elective Deferral Amounts.
- Do not use nearby options that are unselected.

Value mapping:
- Map selected Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annually, or Annually options to the matching enum.
- If the selected option says changes occur at times established by the Plan Administrator in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, return value "Not Indicated" because no fixed frequency is given.
- Use "Other" only when a selected option gives a specific frequency/date rule that is not one of the enum choices.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Changing Elective Deferral Amounts section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected option text in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when deferral_change_frequency resolves to 'Other'.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when deferral_change_frequency.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Deferral Change Frequency Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

prompt.description: Are Roth (after-tax) elective deferrals permitted?.
prompt.instructions:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Roth elective deferrals; Roth contributions.

Extract employee contributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.description: Conditions employees must meet to receive the employer match.
identifiers: Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["None - All eligible participants receive Match", "Must complete 500 hours of service", "Must complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 1000 hours of service"].

Label discovery:
- Find the additional conditions for receiving matching contributions section.
- Relevant rows include last day requirement, hours of service requirement, and all-eligible/no additional condition options.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected conditions under matching contributions.
- Treat a selected additional-conditions framework plus a selected Last Day Requirement row as an actual last-day condition.
- Do not return no additional conditions merely because the No Additional Conditions row is unselected or absent.
- Read selected last-day and hours rows together, including whether the condition says AND or OR.
- Do not treat the Service Requirement row as selected unless its own checkbox/mark is selected.
- Parenthetical text such as "(not more than 1,000) Hours of Service" or "(not more than 12) months" is a limit on the blank, not a filled-in service requirement value.
- Blank underscores are not values. Do not convert a blank line plus a maximum-hours parenthetical into a service-hours requirement.
- A selected additional-conditions framework means some additional condition applies; it does not by itself select every subcondition printed underneath.
- Ignore matching formula text that explains allocation amount rather than eligibility conditions.

Value mapping:
- If only Last Day Requirement is selected, return value "Must be employed on last day of plan year".
- If last-day and hours requirements are both selected, choose the matching AND/OR enum based on the source wording.
- If only an hours requirement is selected, return the matching hours enum.
- If no additional matching allocation condition is selected, return value "None - All eligible participants receive Match".

Source evidence:
- Put the selected matching allocation condition rows in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Match eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
identifiers: Contributing Participants Eligible for Matching Contributions
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement", "Allocated to NHCEs only", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Participants or Contributing Participants Eligible for Matching Contributions section for regular employer matching contributions.
- Also use the regular Matching Contribution Formula and Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions when they define who receives the regular match.
- Do not use Qualified Matching Contributions or ACP/QACA safe harbor matching sections for this regular employer-match field.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the option that says Non-Highly Compensated Employee Participants or All Contributing Participants.
- If there is no separate selected regular-match participant-class row, use selected regular-match conditions that define which contributing participants receive the match.
- If the regular match applies to Qualifying Contributing Participants subject to a selected Last Day Requirement, use that regular-match evidence instead of later Qualified Matching Contribution eligibility rows.
- If Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions selects an additional-condition framework and the Last Day Requirement row is selected, the answer is not the plain all-eligible enum. Return "Other" because the selected rule adds a last-day condition.
- If the same page shows "Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions" with a selected Last Day Requirement, return "Other" even when another regular-match field extracted "Must be employed on last day of plan year".
- Do not choose "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement" when any selected additional condition changes who receives the regular match.
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA selects No, ignore the nearby Part C Safe Harbor CODA row that mentions non-Highly Compensated Employees. It is not the regular employer-match eligible-employee rule.
- Do not use "Participants Entitled to Receive Safe Harbor CODA Contributions" or "ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions" rows to answer regular employer-match eligible employees. Those rows only apply to safe harbor contributions.
- Ignore Qualified Matching Contributions / Qualified Nonelective Contributions eligibility rows when the field asks about regular employer matching contributions.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option limits matching contributions to non-Highly Compensated Employees, return value "Allocated to NHCEs only".
- If the selected option applies to all contributing/eligible participants, return value "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement".
- Return "Other" when the selected regular matching eligible-employee wording is not covered by the enum, including when eligibility depends on being a Qualifying Contributing Participant and satisfying a selected Last Day Requirement.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the regular matching eligible-employee section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected eligible-employee option text in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when employer_match_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
identifiers: Employer Match Eligible Employees Other Specify
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when employer_match_eligible_employees.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer Match Eligible Employees Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

prompt.description: First tier of employer match formula. Free text as stated in plan document.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer match formula; Matching contribution formula; First tier match formula; Employer Match Formula 1.

prompt.description: Second tier of employer match formula, if applicable.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Second tier match formula; Additional matching contribution formula; Employer Match Formula 2.

prompt.description: Employer match timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Matching Contribution Computation Period section as the controlling source.
- Do not use compensation inclusion, contribution formula, or payroll-period language outside that section as timing evidence.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the row inside the Matching Contribution Computation Period option group.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected period option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- When the standalone mark appears between two computation-period labels, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Ignore blank squares and unselected rows, even when they appear first or contain "payroll period".
- Ignore marks in compensation tables above this section; those table cells are not timing elections.
- Use a default only when every row in the Matching Contribution Computation Period option group is visibly unselected. Do not choose payroll period from the first row when a later computation-period row has a visible selected mark.

Value mapping:
- If the selected row says the employer payroll period, return value "Every Pay Period".
- If the selected row says annually or annual, return value "Annually".
- If monthly, quarterly, or semi-annual timing is selected, return the closest enum; semi-annual should be "Other" only if the enum cannot express it.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the Matching Contribution Computation Period section is visible but no option/default can be determined.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Matching Contribution Computation Period section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected computation-period option in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Is there an annual true-up of employer matching contributions?
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the Matching Contribution Computation Period and any matching-contribution recalculation or true-up language.
- This field asks about an annual true-up for employer matching contributions, not the general timing label alone.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected matching contribution computation-period options together with selected matching contribution formula timing.
- Do not infer true-up from boilerplate notes that start with "If Matching Contributions are made more frequently..." unless the document also selects or states that matching contributions are actually made more frequently than the computation period.
- If the computation period is Annually and there is no separate selected option saying contributions are made more frequently than annually, do not emit a true-up value from the conditional note alone.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected matching contribution terms require annual or computation-period recalculation after more frequent contributions.
- Return "false" only when the source directly says no true-up or no recalculation.
- Return null when the source only contains conditional recalculation boilerplate, when matching contributions are not applicable, or when the current page/request lacks true-up/recalculation evidence.

Source evidence:
- Preserve the computation-period and recalculation wording used for the decision.

prompt.description: Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No Match", "Fixed Match", "Discretionary Match", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the matching contribution formula section.
- This field is for employer matching contributions, separate from safe harbor contributions and profit sharing contributions.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected option in the matching contribution formula section.
- Ignore eligibility entry dates, safe harbor contribution formulas, and profit sharing formulas.

Selection from OCR text (page image may be unavailable):
- A selected option is shown by a filled/checked box or by its label appearing without the preceding empty-box glyph.
- A discretionary match is elected by selecting the discretionary-matching option; it has NO numeric formula filled in. Do not return "No Match" merely because no fixed percentage/formula numbers appear.

Value mapping:
- If the document establishes a discretionary matching contribution (e.g., the Employer will determine a discretionary Matching Contribution formula), return value "Discretionary Match".
- If the selected formula gives a fixed match formula (specific percentages/tiers), return value "Fixed Match".
- Return value "No Match" only when matching contributions are explicitly not made / the no-match option is selected.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the matching contribution formula label but no selected formula or explicit no-match election is visible. If the matching contribution formula is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected matching contribution formula option in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Conditions employees must meet to receive profit sharing allocation.
identifiers: Additional Conditions for Receiving Employer Profit Sharing Contributions
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["None - All eligible participants receive NEC", "Must complete 500 hours of service", "Must complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 1000 hours of service"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Additional Conditions for Receiving Employer Profit Sharing Contributions section.
- Relevant rows include Last Day Requirement, Service Requirement, and No additional conditions.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected conditions under Employer Profit Sharing Contributions.
- Treat a selected additional-conditions framework plus a selected Last Day Requirement row as an actual last-day condition.
- A standalone selected mark immediately before the additional-conditions framework selects that framework; it does not select the Service Requirement subrow by itself.
- Do not return no additional conditions merely because the No Additional Conditions row is unselected or absent.
- Read selected last-day and hours rows together, including whether the condition says AND or OR.
- Do not treat the Service Requirement row as selected unless its own checkbox/mark is selected or the hours/months blank is filled in.
- Parenthetical text such as "(not more than 1,000) Hours of Service" or "(not more than 12) months" is a limit on the blank, not a filled-in service requirement value.
- Blank underscores are not values. Do not convert a blank line plus "(not more than 1,000)" into "complete 1000 hours of service."
- Blank hours text or the default note about zero hours is not selected evidence.
- A selected additional-conditions framework means some additional condition applies; it does not by itself select every subcondition printed underneath.
- If the only selected subcondition under that framework is Last Day Requirement, return only the last-day enum.
- Ignore explanatory waiver rows and allocation formula rows; they do not define the base allocation condition.

Value mapping:
- If only Last Day Requirement is selected, return value "Must be employed on last day of plan year".
- If last-day and hours requirements are both selected, choose the matching AND/OR enum based on the source wording.
- If only an hours requirement is selected, return the matching hours enum.
- If the selected row says No additional conditions, return value "None - All eligible participants receive NEC".
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain this profit sharing allocation-condition section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing allocation condition rows in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Profit sharing allocation method as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No Profit Sharing", "Pro-Rata", "Social Security Integrated", "New Comparability - each participant in separate classification", "New Comparability - Enter groups", "Age-Weighted", "Other", "Prevailing Wage Contribution"].

Label discovery:
- Find the employer profit sharing or nonelective contribution allocation formula section.
- Relevant labels describe how employer profit sharing contributions are allocated.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected allocation formula option for employer profit sharing contributions.
- Do not infer no profit sharing from an unselected safe harbor section or from unrelated matching language.

Value mapping:
- If Pro Rata Formula is selected, return value "Pro-Rata".
- If social security integration, new comparability, age-weighted, or prevailing wage is selected, return the matching enum value.
- Return value "No Profit Sharing" only when the current page section contains the employer profit sharing/nonelective contribution label and explicitly disallows employer profit sharing/nonelective contributions. If the employer profit sharing allocation section is not on the current page section, return null.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing allocation formula in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Profit sharing eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
identifiers: Participants Eligible for Employer Profit Sharing Contributions
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement", "Allocated to NHCEs only"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Employer Profit Sharing Contributions allocation/eligible participants section, not Qualified Nonelective Contributions or matching contribution sections.
- Do not use the Contributions to Non-Highly Compensated Disabled Participants section as the eligible-employee rule for normal employer profit sharing contributions.
- Do not use Qualified Nonelective Contributions, Qualified Matching Contributions, QNEC, QMAC, or top-heavy minimum sections to answer normal employer profit sharing eligible employees.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the option describing who receives employer profit sharing contributions.
- If the section says Employer Profit Sharing Contributions are made to Qualifying Participants and the selected Additional Conditions only adds a last-day or service condition, this is still the all-eligible enum for this field.
- Do not treat a selected Non-Highly Compensated Disabled Participants option as an NHCE-only profit-sharing eligibility rule.
- Do not treat a selected Non-Highly Compensated row from a Qualified Nonelective Contributions section as an NHCE-only profit-sharing eligibility rule.
- If no selected normal profit-sharing row limits recipients to NHCEs, do not choose the NHCE-only enum from nearby QNEC/QMAC sections.

Value mapping:
- If selected wording applies to all qualifying/eligible participants who satisfy the age/service requirements, return value "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement".
- If the only extra condition is that Qualifying Participants must satisfy a selected last-day condition, return value "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement"; the separate allocation-conditions field carries the last-day condition.
- If selected wording limits allocations to non-Highly Compensated Employees, return value "Allocated to NHCEs only".
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the employer profit sharing eligible-employee section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing eligible-employee option text in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Profit sharing contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find employer profit sharing contribution authorization, formula, and any explicit timing language.
- Do not use matching contribution timing or matching computation period for this field.
- Do not treat formula wording like "For each Plan Year the Employer may contribute..." as a timing election by itself.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected wording about Employer Profit Sharing Contributions or nonelective/profit sharing contributions.

Value mapping:
- If selected profit sharing timing wording explicitly says annually, every payroll/pay period, or quarterly, return the matching enum.
- If profit sharing is present but the source does not state timing, return value "Not Indicated".
- Do not return null when the Employer Profit Sharing Contributions section is present and selected, but no explicit timing election is present. That is "Not Indicated".
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain profit sharing contribution formula or timing evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing timing or formula-period wording in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Profit sharing contribution as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.

- If the document authorizes discretionary nonelective or profit sharing contributions, return value "Discretionary". Do not return "No Profit Sharing" unless the document explicitly disallows profit sharing or nonelective contributions.
- Use the employer profit sharing / nonelective contribution section. Do not return "No Profit Sharing" just because a nearby safe harbor section is not selected.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Profit sharing contribution; Nonelective contribution type; Employer Profit Sharing Type.
