prompt.description: Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Applicable", "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 3% AND 50% ON NEXT 2% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 4% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 6% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "POSSIBLE SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "QACA SAFE HARBOR TIERED MATCH OF 100% ON THE FIRST 1% AND 50% ON THE NEXT 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% QACA SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Safe Harbor CODA Contributions / ADP Test Safe Harbor section as the controlling source.
- Do not use QACA automatic-enrollment, ACP safe harbor, or printed formula tables by themselves.
- A QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contribution section inside automatic-enrollment provisions is not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA applicability question.

Selection evidence:
- First inspect the page image for the selected Yes/No row in Application of Safe Harbor CODA.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- A selected No row is a real answer and controls the field; return Not Applicable, not null or Not Indicated.
- Formula choices printed below a selected No are inactive boilerplate.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means this field is "Not Applicable".
- If a page only contains QACA, ACP, or safe-harbor formula text and does not show the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Do not return a QACA safe harbor formula unless the same source path also shows that the controlling safe-harbor applicability question selected Yes.
- If Yes is selected, then use the selected safe harbor contribution formula in the same section.
- In reconcile or QA, a source-backed No from Application of Safe Harbor CODA beats formula text that does not also prove Safe Harbor CODA was selected Yes.

Value mapping:
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA selects No, return value "Not Applicable".
- If a safe harbor matching formula or nonelective formula is selected, return the closest matching enum value.
- If the document indicates safe harbor applies but does not provide details, return value "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED".
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the safe harbor label but no selected option is visible. If the safe harbor section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor option and contribution formula text in _raw_text.
- Do not treat automatic enrollment as a safe harbor contribution.
- Do not use EACA automatic enrollment or ACP test safe harbor matching language as the answer when ADP test safe harbor is not selected.

Extract employer contributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.description: Will non-affiliated employers adopt this plan as part of a MEP arrangement?
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Use the adoption-agreement question that asks whether an employer that is not a Related Employer will be allowed to participate in the Plan.
- The controlling labels include "Employers That Are Not Related Employers" and "Will an Employer that is not a Related Employer of the Adopting Employer be allowed to participate in the Plan?"
- Do not use later Participating Employer Election signature pages as the answer. Those pages are attachments and may repeat multiple-employer boilerplate.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes or No answer in the controlling adoption-agreement question.
- If the controlling selected answer says No or says non-related employers may not participate, return "false" even if later participating-employer pages mention multiple employer plan participation.
- Use later Participating Employer Election pages only as supporting evidence when the controlling question is absent.

Selection from OCR text (page image may be unavailable):
- A selected option is shown by a filled/checked box, or by its label appearing WITHOUT the empty-box glyph (e.g., a box character) that precedes unselected options.
- The form default note ("If no option is selected, ...") indicates the fallback; a plan is single-employer (not a MEP) by default.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" only when the controlling question affirmatively selects Yes (a non-Related Employer is allowed to participate).
- Return "false" when the controlling question selects No OR when no multiple-employer election is affirmatively selected (single-employer is the default).
- Do not return null when the controlling question is present on the page; resolve to "false" unless Yes is clearly selected.

Selection evidence:

Value mapping:

- If the selected source label says Partnership, return value "Partnership".
- If the selected source text does not match one of the enum values and "Other" is selected or written in, return value "Other" and put the written-in source text in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby written-in text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

prompt.description: ADP/ACP test year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Current year testing", "Prior year testing", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the ADP Testing Method and ACP Testing Method sections.
- Relevant selected options may say Current-Year Testing Method or Prior-Year Testing Method.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected options in the ADP and ACP testing method sections.
- Ignore initial plan year ADP/ACP suboptions unless they are the selected testing method itself.

Value mapping:
- If both ADP and ACP testing methods select Current-Year Testing Method, return value "Current year testing".
- If both select Prior-Year Testing Method, return value "Prior year testing".
- If the methods differ, return the value that best represents the selected ADP method and preserve both selections in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected ADP and ACP testing method options in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Automatic enrollment exemption as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No", "Not Applicable", "Yes - specify", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the automatic enrollment sections for new employees and current employees, including any option that applies automatic enrollment only to a specified classification of employees.
- This field asks whether an exemption/classification is selected from automatic enrollment.

Selection evidence:
- Pair selected marks with the automatic-enrollment classification or exemption option.
- Treat a selected Current Employees option that applies to a general group, such as employees who are not contributing participants, as no exemption/classification unless a narrower specified employee classification is also selected.
- If the specified-classification option is unselected or its text line is blank, do not return Yes.
- Do not treat automatic enrollment being selected as an exemption.

Value mapping:
- Return value "Yes - specify" when a selected option exempts or limits automatic enrollment to a specified employee classification, and preserve that classification in _raw_text.
- Return value "No" when automatic enrollment applies and no exemption/classification option is selected.
- Return value "Not Applicable" when automatic enrollment itself is not applicable.
- Return "Other" only for selected exemption wording that does not fit the above.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain automatic-enrollment applicability/classification evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected automatic-enrollment classification or no-exemption evidence in _raw_text.

Selection evidence:

Value mapping:

- If the selected option allows rollovers only when otherwise eligible for rollover distribution, return the IRR enum.
- If the selected option allows rollovers at any time for vested money sources, return the IRT enum.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected in-plan Roth rollover option in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Money purchase plan as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:

- Enum choices JSON: ["No evidence of Money Purchase Plan assets", "Money Purchase Plan was merged into this plan and these assets are being maintained as a frozen source", "Money Purchase is a active contribution source", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Search for money purchase plan, money purchase pension assets, merged money purchase plan, frozen money purchase source, or active money purchase contribution source.
- Do not infer money purchase assets from generic rollover, transfer, merger, or predecessor-service language without the money purchase label.
- If the workflow/request provides document-level images or full-document context, use that full context to decide whether any money-purchase-plan evidence exists.

Selection evidence:
- Use explicit selected options or written text about money purchase plan assets.

Value mapping:
- "No evidence of Money Purchase Plan assets" is a real enum value, not the same as null.
- If document-level context is available and no money purchase plan assets or contribution source are indicated anywhere, return value "No evidence of Money Purchase Plan assets".
- During final reconcile or QA, if no page-level candidate in the document contains explicit money purchase plan evidence, return value "No evidence of Money Purchase Plan assets".
- If only a single page is available and it has no money-purchase source/account context, return null.
- Pages covering deemed IRA deposits, SIMPLE 401(k), in-plan Roth rollover, prior-money-source setup, transfer, rollover, or plan-source account elections count as source/account context for this field.
- If a page contains plan-source/account sections where money-purchase assets would normally be stated, and no money-purchase wording is present there, return value "No evidence of Money Purchase Plan assets" for that page.
- If the document says a money purchase plan was merged and assets are frozen, return the matching merged/frozen enum.
- If the document says money purchase is an active contribution source, return the active contribution source enum.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request has no source/account context for this field.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected money-purchase wording or searched source/account section heading in _raw_text.

Extract miscellaneous plan provisions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.description: Joint and Survivor annuity flag. Determined by whether the source plan includes annuity provisions and/or protected in-service distributions.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["IX - Annuities do not apply and protected in-service distributions do not apply", "WX - Annuities do not apply and protected in-service distributions do apply", "IB - Annuities apply and protected in-service distributions do not apply", "WB - Annuities apply and protected in-service distributions do apply"].

Label discovery:
- Determine two facts: whether annuity forms apply, and whether protected in-service distributions apply.
- Use the Form of Distribution / Annuity Contracts section and the Retirement Equity Act Safe Harbor section for annuity evidence.
- Use in-service distribution / protected benefit language only for protected in-service distribution evidence.

Selection evidence:
- Pair selected Yes/No marks with the Annuity Contracts row and Retirement Equity Act Safe Harbor option.
- The Qualifying Longevity Annuity Contract (QLAC) section is not the same as the Annuity Contracts election. Do not use a QLAC Yes/No selection to decide whether annuities apply.
- Do not infer that annuities apply merely because the document prints Qualified Joint and Survivor Annuity boilerplate or survivor-annuity percentage text.
- Do not infer protected in-service distributions from generic in-service withdrawal availability unless the text says the distribution is protected under the prior plan or Code section 411(d)(6).
- Ordinary lump sum, partial payment, installment, or annuity-contract distribution options are not protected in-service distribution evidence.
- If annuity evidence is selected and no explicit protected in-service distribution evidence appears in the relevant source pages, treat protected in-service distributions as not applying.

Value mapping:
- If annuities do not apply and protected in-service distributions do not apply, return value "IX - Annuities do not apply and protected in-service distributions do not apply".
- If annuities do not apply and protected in-service distributions do apply, return value "WX - Annuities do not apply and protected in-service distributions do apply".
- If annuities apply and protected in-service distributions do not apply, return value "IB - Annuities apply and protected in-service distributions do not apply".
- If both apply, return value "WB - Annuities apply and protected in-service distributions do apply".
- Return null when the current page/request lacks enough annuity/protected-distribution evidence to decide.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected annuity and protected-distribution evidence in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Status of the plan or adoption agreement.
identifiers: Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["New Plan", "Amendment & Restatement"].

Label discovery:
- Find the option group or form area that asks whether the plan/adoption agreement is being newly established or whether an existing plan is being amended, restated, updated, or replaced.
- Use source labels that mean plan status, including labels like: New plan; Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date; Plan status.
- A relevant label does not have to match the identifiers exactly. It is relevant when its meaning is about plan status: first-time plan setup, initial adoption, new plan effective date, existing plan, amendment, restatement, amendment effective date, or restatement date.

Selection evidence:
- Identify the selected value by pairing each checkbox, tick mark, filled box, or other selection mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it.
- Nearby option text is not evidence by itself. The checkbox or selection mark confirms that option when selected and rejects that option when unselected.
- Do not treat an unselected checkbox label as the answer, even when that label contains an enum value or identifier.

Value mapping:
- If the selected label says the plan is new, newly established, or an initial adoption, return value "New Plan".
- If the selected label says the plan is existing, amended, restated, an amendment, a restatement, or an "Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date", return value "Amendment & Restatement".

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby date text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

prompt.description: Plan year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Calendar Year", "Fiscal Year", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Use only the option group headed Plan Year Means. In ADP forms, this is often headed Part G. Plan Year Means and may appear immediately after Part F. Limitation Year Means.
- Do not use Limitation Year Means, tax year, valuation year, or unrelated Plan Year references.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the row inside the Plan Year Means option group.
- A checkmark, filled box, or X immediately before or after an option label selects that option row when the page image confirms the row alignment.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat the following option row as selected when it is visually aligned with that mark.
- When a standalone mark appears between two Plan Year Means option labels, attach it to the following option row, not the preceding row or default row.
- The selected mark may appear on the option-label line while the value text continues on the next line; read the whole selected row.
- Ignore unselected rows, even when they appear first or contain enum words.
- Use a default note only when every row in the Plan Year Means option group is visibly unselected. Do not choose the first/default row when a later row has a visible selected mark.
- If Plan Year Means has a visible selected row, return that value instead of null.

Value mapping:
- If the selected Plan Year Means option is the calendar year, return value "Calendar Year".
- If the selected option is the employer tax year or a fiscal 12-month period, return value "Fiscal Year" unless the selected wording is neither calendar nor fiscal.
- If the selected wording is a 52/53 week period or other custom period, return value "Other" and preserve the wording in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Plan Year Means section.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected Plan Year Means option in _raw_text.

Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["401(k) Plan", "Profit Sharing Plan", "Money Purchase Pension Plan"].

Label discovery:
- Use the plan title, adoption agreement title, or Name of Plan field.
- This field asks for the primary plan type, not every phrase in the plan name.

Selection evidence:
- If the title includes "401(k) Profit Sharing Plan", treat the 401(k) part as the primary plan type.
- Do not choose "Profit Sharing Plan" merely because "Profit Sharing" appears in the standard 401(k) profit sharing plan title.

Value mapping:
- If the source title or plan name includes 401(k), return value "401(k) Plan".
- If the source title identifies only profit sharing and does not include 401(k), return value "Profit Sharing Plan".
- If the source title identifies a money purchase pension plan, return value "Money Purchase Pension Plan".

Source evidence:
- Put the exact plan title or Name of Plan text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

prompt.description: Valuation date as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every day (Daily Valuation)", "Last day of each Plan Year", "Last day of each Plan Year half (Semi-Annual)", "Last day of each Plan Year quarter", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the valuation-date section or table row that says when the plan value is determined.
- In ADP adoption agreements, this may appear as Part K, Valuation Date.
- Relevant labels may say valuation date, plan valuation date, allocation date, daily, quarter, semi-annual, annual, or other timing language.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkbox, tick mark, filled box, or marked option in the valuation-date option group.
- Treat the named valuation-date option group as the controlling source. If it is visible and a row is selected, return the selected value instead of returning null.
- If OCR separates the mark from the option text, use the page image to pair the mark with the aligned valuation-date row.
- When a standalone mark appears between valuation-date rows, attach it to the following row if page-image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Use default notes only when every valuation-date option row is visibly unselected. Do not return null when the valuation-date section is visible and a row is selected.
- Do not use selected rows from nearby required beginning date, retirement age, life insurance, or participant-direction sections.
- Ignore unselected timing labels in the same option group.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option says Daily, every day, or daily valuation, return value "Every day (Daily Valuation)".
- If the selected option says the last day of each Plan Year, return value "Last day of each Plan Year".
- If the selected option says semi-annual or each Plan Year half, return value "Last day of each Plan Year half (Semi-Annual)".
- If the selected option says quarterly or each Plan Year quarter, return value "Last day of each Plan Year quarter".
- If the selected source wording is a different valuation timing, return value "Other" and preserve the written source wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby timing text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer a valuation frequency from unrelated allocation, payroll, or contribution timing text.

prompt.description: 100% vesting at early retirement as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the Fully Vested Under Certain Circumstances section and the row for "The Employee satisfies the conditions for Early Retirement Age (if applicable)."
- If needed, check the Retirement Age section to see whether Early Retirement Age is applicable.
- Do not use the Normal Retirement Age row, Required Beginning Date, Valuation Date, or other selected retirement-related rows for this field.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected Yes/No checkbox with the Early Retirement Age row.
- Ignore Yes selections for death or disability; those are separate rows.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" only when the Early Retirement Age row is selected Yes.
- Return "false" when the Early Retirement Age row is selected No, or when the Retirement Age section selects "An Early Retirement Age is not applicable under the Plan."
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the early-retirement vesting row or the early-retirement applicability section.

Source evidence:
- Use the exact Early Retirement Age row and selected Yes/No mark as evidence.

prompt.description: Vesting schedule for Safe Harbor contributions.
identifiers: QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "100% immediate (QACA match)", "100% immediate (QACA NEC)", "2 Year Cliff for QACA match", "2 Year Cliff for QACA NEC", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether ADP Test Safe Harbor or QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are selected.
- Then find the vesting rule for the selected safe harbor contribution.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected safe harbor contribution options and selected vesting schedule options together.
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA / ADP Test Safe Harbor selects No, that selected No is enough evidence to return "N/A" for safe harbor vesting.
- A selected No in the Safe Harbor CODA applicability section is a real answer for this field, not missing evidence. Do not return null in that case.
- When the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question is visible, inspect the page image for the selected Yes or No row before looking at formula or vesting text.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- If the page image shows a selected mark on the No row in the Application of Safe Harbor CODA or ADP Test Safe Harbor applicability section, return "N/A" even if OCR does not include a checkbox symbol.
- When the standalone mark is between Yes and No rows, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means the safe-harbor vesting answer is "N/A".
- Do not require a separate vesting schedule when the controlling safe-harbor applicability section selects No. The selected No is the vesting answer for this field.
- Do not suppress the selected No just because this field is named as a vesting schedule. The applicability No is the source evidence that safe-harbor vesting is not applicable.
- If the document-level safe_harbor_type is "Not Applicable" or the source selected No for Application of Safe Harbor CODA, return "N/A" even when QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting schedule text appears elsewhere.
- In reconcile or QA, a candidate with formula or vesting-table text is lower priority than a visible selected No in the controlling safe-harbor applicability question.
- Inspect the page image for the selected No mark when OCR text drops checkbox symbols.
- If the current page/request has only QACA automatic-enrollment or formula text and does not show the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question, return null for this field.
- If a page contains only safe-harbor formula or vesting-table text and not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Ignore EACA automatic enrollment, QACA automatic-enrollment text, QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting tables, and ACP-test-only matching language when ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable.

Value mapping:
- If Safe Harbor CODA provisions or ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are explicitly not selected/applicable, return value "N/A".
- For this enum field, an explicit selected No for safe harbor applicability should be returned as value "N/A", not null.
- If selected safe harbor contributions vest immediately, return the matching immediate enum.
- If selected QACA safe harbor contributions use a two-year cliff, return the matching two-year cliff enum.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain safe harbor applicability or safe harbor vesting evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor applicability and vesting text in _raw_text.
- If ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable, do not populate QACA safe harbor vesting from automatic-enrollment or ACP-test-only matching language.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_vesting resolves to 'Other'.

- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

prompt.description: Vesting computation period as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Plan year/1000 hours", "Plan Year / 1000 hours calculated with an equivalency method", "Employment year/Elapsed time", "Employment year/1000 hours calculated with monthly equivalency", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether all applicable employer contribution vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only inspect Measuring Period for Vesting and Service Required for Vesting if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedule options and selected measuring-period options together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, the measuring-period rules are not needed for these fields.
- Do not use elapsed-time, Plan Year, or hours rules from Measuring Period for Vesting / Service Required for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediately vested.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If a non-immediate schedule applies and the selected measuring period is Plan Year with 1000 hours, return value "Plan year/1000 hours".
- If an equivalency or elapsed-time method is selected for a non-immediate schedule, return the closest matching enum.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain vesting schedule or measuring-period evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and measuring-period option in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Vesting service as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["All years of service", "Exclude service before company maintained plan", "Exclude service prior to age 18", "Exclude service before company maintained plan and service prior to age 18", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether employer match and/or profit sharing vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only then inspect service-exclusion rules if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedules and selected service-exclusion rows together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested.
- Do not use generic all-years-service text from Part F Exclusion of Service for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediate.
- The Part F sentence "All of an Employee's Years of Vesting Service..." is service-exclusion boilerplate. It does not override selected 100% immediate vesting schedules.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If service before the company maintained the plan is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If service prior to age 18 is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If no selected exclusion applies and a non-immediate vesting schedule applies, return value "All years of service".

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and service-exclusion text in _raw_text.

Extract vesting and retirement ages fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.description: Hardship distributions as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Permitted", "Not Permitted", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the hardship availability or hardship distribution section.
- Use the hardship withdrawals / hardship distributions section, not in-service withdrawal or rollover sections.
- Relevant rows may cover elective deferrals, Roth elective deferrals, matching contributions, employer profit sharing contributions, or other money sources.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected Yes/No options and selected money-source rows inside the hardship distribution section.
- Ignore in-service withdrawal, rollover withdrawal, loan, or voluntary distribution sections for this field.

Value mapping:
- Return value "Permitted" when hardship withdrawals or hardship distributions are selected for any money source.
- Return value "Not Permitted" only when the current page section contains the hardship distribution label and explicitly says hardship withdrawals are not allowed.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the hardship distribution label but no selected option is visible. If the hardship distribution section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected Yes/No option and selected money-source text in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Money source restrictions for hardship distributions, if permitted. Use the closest listed source restriction when the document is explicit; otherwise capture the restriction in Other.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Permitted only from elective deferrals", "Permitted only from employee contributions", "Permitted from all contribution sources", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the hardship withdrawal availability sections for Elective Deferrals and for Matching Contributions / Employer Profit Sharing Contributions.
- This field asks for a money-source restriction, not a plain list of sources where hardship withdrawals are available.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected hardship availability rows and selected restriction text together.
- Do not treat selected available contribution sources as a restriction unless the text says hardship withdrawals are limited to only those sources.

Value mapping:
- Return value "N/A" when hardship withdrawals are not permitted, or when selected rows only say which sources are available without imposing a narrower restriction.
- Return "Permitted only from elective deferrals" or "Permitted only from employee contributions" only when the selected text explicitly limits the source that way.
- Return "Permitted from all contribution sources" only when the selected text explicitly permits all contribution sources.
- Return "Other" only for an explicit selected restriction not covered by the enum.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the hardship source section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected hardship source/restriction rows in _raw_text.

prompt.instructions:

- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.

- Return "Other" only when selected vesting text is explicit but not covered by the enum.

prompt.description: Service requirement for in-service withdrawals, if permitted.
identifiers: Years of service for in-service withdrawals
prompt.instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Later of required age or 5 years of service", "Later of required age or 6 years of service", "Later of required age or 5 years of participation", "Later of required age or 6 years of participation", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Only populate this field when in_service_withdrawals.value equals "Permitted". Otherwise return null.
- If the document permits in-service withdrawals but does not impose an additional service requirement, return value "N/A". - Age-only criteria, attainment of age 59.5, Normal Retirement Age, or a row with 0 years/0 months of service do not impose an additional service requirement. Treat those as "N/A" for this field. - During final reconcile or QA, keep "N/A" when the source evidence shows only age criteria or 0 years of service. Do not change it to "Other" unless the source imposes a nonzero service/participation period not covered by the enum.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: In-service withdrawal service requirement; Years of service for in-service withdrawals; In Service Withdrawals Service Requirement.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when in_service_withdrawals_service_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
identifiers: In Service Withdrawals Service Requirement Other Specify
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when in_service_withdrawals_service_requirement.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: In Service Withdrawals Service Requirement Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

prompt.description: Vesting requirement for in-service withdrawals, if permitted.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["None - available for vested balance", "Requires 100% vesting of employer contribution", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the in-service availability section for employer contributions.
- Relevant criteria may say "Upon reaching a Vested percentage equal to 100 percent", "After becoming 100 percent Vested", vested balance, years of participation, age, or service conditions.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected in-service withdrawal rows for employer contribution sources and their criteria row/table cell.
- If Matching Contributions or Employer Profit Sharing Contributions are checked in the row "Upon reaching a Vested percentage equal to 100 percent", that is a 100% vesting requirement.
- If Matching Contributions or Employer Profit Sharing Contributions have entries on the row "After becoming 100 percent Vested, participating in the Plan for a period of years equal to (a) and attaining age (b)", that row also imposes a 100% vesting requirement.
- Numeric entries such as "(a) 0" and "(b) 59.5" in that row still count as selected criteria for the corresponding contribution-source columns.
- Ignore elective-deferral-only in-service rules when deciding employer-contribution vesting restrictions.

Value mapping:
- If employer-contribution in-service withdrawals require becoming 100 percent vested, return value "Requires 100% vesting of employer contribution".
- If withdrawals are available from vested balances without a 100% vesting requirement, return value "None - available for vested balance".
- If selected criteria are different, return value "Other" and preserve the criteria in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected employer contribution sources and criteria wording in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when in_service_withdrawals_vesting_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
identifiers: In Service Withdrawals Vesting Requirement Other Specify
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when in_service_withdrawals_vesting_requirement.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: In Service Withdrawals Vesting Requirement Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

prompt.description: Participant loans as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Loans not permitted", "Loans permitted but number of outstanding loans not indicated", "1 outstanding loans permitted per participant", "2 outstanding loans permitted per participant", "3 or more outstanding loan permitted per participant", "4 or more outstanding loan permitted per participant", "5 or more outstanding loan permitted per participant"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- If loans are permitted but the document does not state the number of outstanding loans, return value "Loans permitted but number of outstanding loans not indicated".
- If the participant loan section selects Yes / loans are permitted but does not give a numeric outstanding-loan limit, return value "Loans permitted but number of outstanding loans not indicated".
- Return "Loans not permitted" only when the document explicitly disallows participant loans.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Participant loans; Loans.

prompt.description: Payment options as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Lump Sum and Installments in cash", "Lump Sum, Installments and partial in cash", "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Voluntary Distributions / Form of Distribution section.
- Use the distribution form / payment options section, not contribution-source or withdrawal-availability sections.

Selection evidence:
- Pair each selected Yes/No checkbox with its row: Lump Sum, Partial Payments, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts.
- Ignore row labels whose Yes option is unselected or whose No option is selected.

Value mapping:
- If Lump Sum and Installment Payments are selected Yes, and Partial Payments and Annuity Contracts are not selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum and Installments in cash".
- If Lump Sum, Partial Payments, and Installment Payments are selected Yes, and Annuity Contracts are not selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and partial in cash".
- If Lump Sum, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts are selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity".
- If Lump Sum, Partial Payments, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts are all selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity" and preserve Partial Payments in _raw_text because the enum has no exact value for all four selected options.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the distribution form section is visible but selected forms cannot be determined.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the distribution form section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected distribution form rows in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Withdrawals from rollover account as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
prompt.instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Permitted at any time", "Not Permitted", "Allowed with same restrictions as In-service withdrawals", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the withdrawals of rollover contributions section.
- Relevant labels ask whether rollover contributions may be withdrawn and when.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected option in the rollover withdrawal provision.
- Ignore rollover contribution acceptance, in-plan Roth rollover, hardship, and in-service withdrawal sections unless the rollover withdrawal provision explicitly points to the same restrictions.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option says Yes without added restrictions, or says available at any time, return value "Permitted at any time".
- If the selected option says No, return value "Not Permitted".
- If the selected option ties rollover withdrawals to the same restrictions as in-service withdrawals, return value "Allowed with same restrictions as In-service withdrawals".
- If the selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve the selected wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected rollover withdrawal option in _raw_text.

prompt.description: Free text. Only populated when withdrawals_from_rollover resolves to 'Other'.
prompt.instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when withdrawals_from_rollover.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Withdrawals From Rollover Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

Extract withdrawals and distributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f1_employer_and_plan_information Definition

Extract employer and plan information fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## employer_name

Field: employer_name
Description: Employer name as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer name, Name of Employer, Employer
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Preserve the employer name exactly as written, including ampersands, initials, punctuation, and capitalization. Do not insert spaces inside a name token unless the document shows spaces.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer name; Name of Employer; Employer.

## address_street

Field: address_street
Description: Adopting Employer's street address from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Name of Adopting Employer, Part A. Adopting Employer, Address, Street address
Special Instructions:
- Return the street address of the Adopting Employer from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section, Part A, on the "Address" line directly under "Name of Adopting Employer".
- Use ONLY the employer's own address. Do NOT use the form publisher's, recordkeeper's, document-preparer's, or vendor's address printed in page headers/footers (e.g., Ascensus, Paychex, an address near a copyright "©" line, or near a form revision number such as "#3001 (Rev. 6/2020)").
- Return just the street line (e.g., "8525 W 100th Ave"); do not include city, state, or ZIP.
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.

## address_city

Field: address_city
Description: Adopting Employer's city from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Name of Adopting Employer, City, Address City
Special Instructions:
- Return the city of the Adopting Employer from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section, Part A, on the "City   State   Zip" line directly under the employer Address.
- Use ONLY the employer's own city. Do NOT use the form publisher's, recordkeeper's, or vendor's city in page headers/footers (e.g., Ascensus's West Henrietta NY, or Paychex).
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.

## address_state

Field: address_state
Description: Adopting Employer's state from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Name of Adopting Employer, State, Address State
Special Instructions:
- Return the two-letter state of the Adopting Employer from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section, Part A, on the "City   State   Zip" line.
- Use ONLY the employer's own state. Do NOT use the form publisher's, recordkeeper's, or vendor's state in page headers/footers (e.g., Ascensus's NY).
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.

## address_zip

Field: address_zip
Description: Adopting Employer's ZIP code from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Name of Adopting Employer, Zip, Address Zip
Special Instructions:
- Return the ZIP code of the Adopting Employer from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section, Part A, on the "City   State   Zip" line.
- Use ONLY the employer's own ZIP. Do NOT use the form publisher's, recordkeeper's, or vendor's ZIP in page headers/footers (e.g., Ascensus's 14586).
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.

## telephone

Field: telephone
Description: Adopting Employer's telephone number from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Name of Adopting Employer, Telephone, Employer telephone
Special Instructions:
- Return the Adopting Employer's telephone number from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section, Part A, on the "Telephone" line.
- Use ONLY the employer's own phone. Do NOT use the form publisher's, recordkeeper's, or vendor's phone, and never a toll-free 1-800/1-888 support number printed in headers/footers (e.g., an Ascensus or Paychex support line).
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.

## tax_identification_number

Field: tax_identification_number
Description: Format: XX-XXXXXXX
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Taxpayer Identification Number, TIN, Employer identification number, Tax Identification Number
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Taxpayer Identification Number; TIN; Employer identification number; Tax Identification Number.

## employer_fiscal_year_ends

Field: employer_fiscal_year_ends
Description: Month and day the fiscal year ends. Format: MM/DD. Common value: 12/31.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Fiscal year end, Plan Sponsor fiscal year end, Employer fiscal year ends
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Fiscal year end; Plan Sponsor fiscal year end; Employer fiscal year ends.

## type_of_entity

Field: type_of_entity
Description: Type of Entity as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Type of Business, Entity type, Business entity
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Corporation", "Professional Service Corporation", "S Corporation", "Limited Liability Company", "Sole Proprietorship", "Partnership", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the form area that asks for the employer business or legal entity type.
- Relevant labels may say Type of Business, Type of Entity, Entity type, Business entity, or similar wording about what kind of legal/business entity the employer is.
- Do not use affiliated-employer, controlled-group, LLC tax-subtype, or ownership questions for this field.

Selection evidence:
- Identify the selected value by pairing each checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or other selection mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it.
- Nearby option text is not evidence by itself. The selected mark confirms the option; an unselected option label is not the answer.

Value mapping:
- If the selected source label says C Corporation or Corporation, return value "Corporation".
- If the selected source label says Professional Service Corporation, return value "Professional Service Corporation".
- If the selected source label says S Corporation, return value "S Corporation".
- If the selected source label says Limited Liability Company or LLC, return value "Limited Liability Company".
- If the selected source label says Sole Proprietorship, return value "Sole Proprietorship".
- If the selected source label says Partnership, return value "Partnership".
- If the selected source text does not match one of the enum values and "Other" is selected or written in, return value "Other" and put the written-in source text in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby written-in text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

## type_of_entity_llc_subtype

Field: type_of_entity_llc_subtype
Description: Only populated when type_of_entity is 'Limited Liability Company'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Limited Liability Company taxed as, LLC taxed as, Type Of Entity LLC Subtype
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Partnership or Sole Proprietorship", "Corporation", "S Corporation"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Only populate this field when type_of_entity.value equals "Limited Liability Company". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Limited Liability Company taxed as; LLC taxed as; Type Of Entity LLC Subtype.

## type_of_entity_other_specify

Field: type_of_entity_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when type_of_entity is 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Other entity type, Other - specify entity type, Type Of Entity Other Specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when type_of_entity.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other entity type; Other - specify entity type; Type Of Entity Other Specify.

## affiliated_employers

Field: affiliated_employers
Description: Is the employer a member of a controlled group or affiliated service group?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Affiliated Employers, Controlled group or affiliated service group, Is the employer an affiliated employer
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.

Label discovery:
- Find Employer Information, Participating Employers, Related Employers, or the question asking whether the Adopting Employer is part of a controlled group, commonly controlled trades/businesses, affiliated service group, or other related employer aggregation group.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes/No option for whether all Related Employers are considered Related Participating Employers.
- Do not use unrelated boilerplate mentions of affiliated service groups outside the selected Related Employers question.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected option says Yes/all Related Employers participate.
- Return "false" when the selected option says No/Related Employers participate only if separately listed.

Source evidence:
- Use the selected Related Employers option as the source evidence.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

## affiliated_employer_group_type

Field: affiliated_employer_group_type
Description: Only populated when affiliated_employers is true. Select all that apply.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Controlled Group, Affiliated Service Group, Affiliated employer group type
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON array string whose entries are objects with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Controlled Group", "Affiliated Service Group"].
- If an extracted entry does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Only populate this field when affiliated_employers equals "true". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Controlled Group; Affiliated Service Group; Affiliated employer group type.

## participating_employers

Field: participating_employers
Description: Will affiliated employers adopt the plan as participating employers?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Participating Employers, Affiliated employers adopting the plan
Special Instructions:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Participating Employers; Affiliated employers adopting the plan.

## multiple_employer_plan

Field: multiple_employer_plan
Description: Will non-affiliated employers adopt this plan as part of a MEP arrangement?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Multiple Employer Plan, Non-affiliated employers adopt this plan
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Use the adoption-agreement question that asks whether an employer that is not a Related Employer will be allowed to participate in the Plan.
- The controlling labels include "Employers That Are Not Related Employers" and "Will an Employer that is not a Related Employer of the Adopting Employer be allowed to participate in the Plan?"
- Do not use later Participating Employer Election signature pages as the answer. Those pages are attachments and may repeat multiple-employer boilerplate.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes or No answer in the controlling adoption-agreement question.
- If the controlling selected answer says No or says non-related employers may not participate, return "false" even if later participating-employer pages mention multiple employer plan participation.
- Use later Participating Employer Election pages only as supporting evidence when the controlling question is absent.

Selection from OCR text (page image may be unavailable):
- A selected option is shown by a filled/checked box, or by its label appearing WITHOUT the empty-box glyph (e.g., a box character) that precedes unselected options.
- The form default note ("If no option is selected, ...") indicates the fallback; a plan is single-employer (not a MEP) by default.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" only when the controlling question affirmatively selects Yes (a non-Related Employer is allowed to participate).
- Return "false" when the controlling question selects No OR when no multiple-employer election is affirmatively selected (single-employer is the default).
- Do not return null when the controlling question is present on the page; resolve to "false" unless Yes is clearly selected.

## plan_name

Field: plan_name
Description: Plan name as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Plan name, Name of Plan
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Plan name; Name of Plan.

## plan_status

Field: plan_status
Description: Status of the plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Format: enumerated value
Example Identifiers: New plan, Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date, Plan status
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["New Plan", "Amendment & Restatement"].

Label discovery:
- Find the option group or form area that asks whether the plan/adoption agreement is being newly established or whether an existing plan is being amended, restated, updated, or replaced.
- Use source labels that mean plan status, including labels like: New plan; Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date; Plan status.
- A relevant label does not have to match the identifiers exactly. It is relevant when its meaning is about plan status: first-time plan setup, initial adoption, new plan effective date, existing plan, amendment, restatement, amendment effective date, or restatement date.

Selection evidence:
- Identify the selected value by pairing each checkbox, tick mark, filled box, or other selection mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it.
- Nearby option text is not evidence by itself. The checkbox or selection mark confirms that option when selected and rejects that option when unselected.
- Do not treat an unselected checkbox label as the answer, even when that label contains an enum value or identifier.

Value mapping:
- If the selected label says the plan is new, newly established, or an initial adoption, return value "New Plan".
- If the selected label says the plan is existing, amended, restated, an amendment, a restatement, or an "Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date", return value "Amendment & Restatement".

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby date text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

## oe_date

Field: oe_date
Description: Open Enrollment / Original Effective Date of the plan. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Original effective date, Effective date, Initial effective date
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Original effective date; Effective date; Initial effective date.

## restatement_effective_date

Field: restatement_effective_date
Description: Effective date of this restatement if plan_status is 'Amendment & Restatement'. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Restatement effective date, Effective date of restatement
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Restatement effective date; Effective date of restatement.

## plan_year

Field: plan_year
Description: Plan year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Plan Year Means, Plan Year, Adopting Employer's tax year
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Calendar Year", "Fiscal Year", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Use only the option group headed Plan Year Means. In ADP forms, this is often headed Part G. Plan Year Means and may appear immediately after Part F. Limitation Year Means.
- Do not use Limitation Year Means, tax year, valuation year, or unrelated Plan Year references.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the row inside the Plan Year Means option group.
- A checkmark, filled box, or X immediately before or after an option label selects that option row when the page image confirms the row alignment.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat the following option row as selected when it is visually aligned with that mark.
- When a standalone mark appears between two Plan Year Means option labels, attach it to the following option row, not the preceding row or default row.
- The selected mark may appear on the option-label line while the value text continues on the next line; read the whole selected row.
- Ignore unselected rows, even when they appear first or contain enum words.
- Use a default note only when every row in the Plan Year Means option group is visibly unselected. Do not choose the first/default row when a later row has a visible selected mark.
- If Plan Year Means has a visible selected row, return that value instead of null.

Value mapping:
- If the selected Plan Year Means option is the calendar year, return value "Calendar Year".
- If the selected option is the employer tax year or a fiscal 12-month period, return value "Fiscal Year" unless the selected wording is neither calendar nor fiscal.
- If the selected wording is a 52/53 week period or other custom period, return value "Other" and preserve the wording in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Plan Year Means section.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected Plan Year Means option in _raw_text.

## plan_year_other_specify

Field: plan_year_other_specify

## valuation_date

Label discovery:
- Find the valuation-date section or table row that says when the plan value is determined.
- In ADP adoption agreements, this may appear as Part K, Valuation Date.
- Relevant labels may say valuation date, plan valuation date, allocation date, daily, quarter, semi-annual, annual, or other timing language.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkbox, tick mark, filled box, or marked option in the valuation-date option group.
- Treat the named valuation-date option group as the controlling source. If it is visible and a row is selected, return the selected value instead of returning null.
- If OCR separates the mark from the option text, use the page image to pair the mark with the aligned valuation-date row.
- When a standalone mark appears between valuation-date rows, attach it to the following row if page-image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Use default notes only when every valuation-date option row is visibly unselected. Do not return null when the valuation-date section is visible and a row is selected.
- Do not use selected rows from nearby required beginning date, retirement age, life insurance, or participant-direction sections.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option says Daily, every day, or daily valuation, return value "Every day (Daily Valuation)".
- If the selected option says the last day of each Plan Year, return value "Last day of each Plan Year".
- If the selected option says semi-annual or each Plan Year half, return value "Last day of each Plan Year half (Semi-Annual)".
- If the selected option says quarterly or each Plan Year quarter, return value "Last day of each Plan Year quarter".
- If the selected source wording is a different valuation timing, return value "Other" and preserve the written source wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby timing text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer a valuation frequency from unrelated allocation, payroll, or contribution timing text.

## type_of_plan

Label discovery:

Selection evidence:

- Do not choose "Profit Sharing Plan" merely because "Profit Sharing" appears in the standard 401(k) profit sharing plan title.

- If the source title identifies a money purchase pension plan, return value "Money Purchase Pension Plan".

Source evidence:

- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

# Output Contract

- Custom workflow step: `adp_f1_employer_and_plan_information`
- Workflow group: `adp_f1_employer_and_plan_information`
- Step kind: `instruct`
- Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
- Use only these output keys:
- `employer_name`

- `address_state`
