When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## contribution_limit

Field: contribution_limit
Description: Maximum deferral limit expressed as percentage of compensation.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Elective deferral limit, Maximum deferral limit, Salary deferral limit, Contribution Limit
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["1% to 100% of compensation subject to IRS limits", "1% to 92% of compensation subject to IRS limits", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- If the document's exact contribution range is not one of the listed enum ranges, return value "Other" and put the exact source range in _raw_text and contribution_limit_other_specify.
- Do not coerce a 0% minimum into a 1% minimum.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Elective deferral limit; Maximum deferral limit; Salary deferral limit; Contribution Limit.

## contribution_limit_other_specify

Field: contribution_limit_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when contribution_limit resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Contribution Limit Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when contribution_limit.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- When contribution_limit.value is "Other", preserve the exact source range, including the minimum and maximum percentages.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Contribution Limit Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## contribution_method

Field: contribution_method
Description: Method(s) by which employees may elect deferrals.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Authorization of Elective Deferrals, salary reduction agreement, dollar amount or percentage
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["% of Compensation Only", "Flat Dollar Only", "% of Compensation and Flat Dollar", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the elective deferral authorization or salary reduction agreement section that describes the form of employee deferral elections.
- Do not use contribution-limit text, catch-up contribution text, or dollar limits as the contribution method unless the selected option is explicitly about the election form.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected options that say the participant may elect a percentage of compensation, a flat dollar amount, or both.
- In Limits on Elective Deferrals, a selected row that says the deferral amount is a percentage of the participant's compensation means the contribution method is percentage of compensation only.
- Ignore unselected dollar-amount options in the same list.

Value mapping:
- Return "% of Compensation Only" when only a compensation percentage election is selected.
- Return "Flat Dollar Only" when only a dollar election is selected.
- Return "% of Compensation and Flat Dollar" when both are selected or the selected option allows either a dollar amount or percentage.
- Return "Not Indicated" when elective deferrals are allowed but the selected source only states limits or plan-administrator rules and does not state the election method.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain elective-deferral election-method evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected election-method wording in _raw_text.

## deferral_change_frequency

Field: deferral_change_frequency
Description: Deferral change frequency as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Changing Elective Deferral Amounts, modify a salary reduction agreement, deferral change frequency
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Indicated", "Daily", "Every Pay Period", "Weekly", "Monthly", "Quarterly", "Semi-Annual", "Annual", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Changing Elective Deferral Amounts section. Do not use Ceasing Elective Deferrals or Return as a Contributing Participant unless the field specifically asks about those actions.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected option under Changing Elective Deferral Amounts.
- Do not use nearby options that are unselected.

Value mapping:
- Map selected Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annually, or Annually options to the matching enum.
- If the selected option says changes occur at times established by the Plan Administrator in a uniform and nondiscriminatory manner, return value "Not Indicated" because no fixed frequency is given.
- Use "Other" only when a selected option gives a specific frequency/date rule that is not one of the enum choices.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Changing Elective Deferral Amounts section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected option text in _raw_text.

## deferral_change_frequency_other_specify

Field: deferral_change_frequency_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when deferral_change_frequency resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Deferral Change Frequency Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when deferral_change_frequency.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Deferral Change Frequency Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## roth_elective_deferrals

Field: roth_elective_deferrals
Description: Are Roth (after-tax) elective deferrals permitted?.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Roth elective deferrals, Roth contributions
Special Instructions:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Roth elective deferrals; Roth contributions.

## after_tax_voluntary_contributions

Field: after_tax_voluntary_contributions
Description: Mirror of eligibility_requirements.after_tax_contributions kept in the Employee Contributions section because the workflow surfaces the concept in both places. Values should match unless the document itself is contradictory.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Nondeductible Employee Contributions, May a Contributing Participant make Nondeductible Employee Contributions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Use the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question as the source for after-tax voluntary employee contributions.
- Do not use rollover contributions, Roth elective deferrals, top-heavy contributions, or generic employee-contribution text as evidence for this field.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected answer row in the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following option row.
- When the standalone mark appears between Yes rows and the No row, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- A selected No row controls this field even when other employee-contribution features are selected elsewhere.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected Nondeductible Employee Contributions answer permits nondeductible employee contributions.
- Return "false" when the selected answer says No/not permitted, or when the section says the No/not-permitted answer applies by default when nothing else is selected.
- During reconcile or QA, keep a source-backed "false" from selected No/not permitted unless another candidate cites selected Yes/allowed evidence from the same Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- Roth elective deferrals are separate from nondeductible employee contributions; do not convert a selected Roth option into "true" here.
- The document can permit Roth elective deferrals or rollovers while prohibiting nondeductible employee contributions; that combination is still "false" for this field.
- If eligibility_requirements.after_tax_contributions is source-backed false from the same Nondeductible Employee Contributions question, this mirror field should also be false.
- If either mirrored after-tax field cites selected No/not permitted from the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question, both mirrored fields should resolve to "false" unless the same question selects Yes/allowed elsewhere.
- Do not leave this mirror field blank when the current page/request contains a visible selected Nondeductible Employee Contributions option.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section.

Source evidence:
- Use the exact selected option label and nearby question text as evidence.

## automatic_enrollment_type

Field: automatic_enrollment_type
Description: Automatic enrollment as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferrals, EACA provisions, QACA provisions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Applicable", "QACA Automatic Enrollment", "EACA Automatic Enrollment"].

Label discovery:
- Find item 9(a), Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferrals. It asks whether the automatic Elective Deferral enrollment provisions apply.
- Treat item 9(a) as the plan-level automatic enrollment election.
- Later ACA/EACA subsections describe which employees, amounts, increases, and timing apply after item 9(a) has already selected ACA/EACA/QACA.

Selection evidence:
- Use only the selected checkbox or marked option in item 9(a) to decide this field.
- If item 9(a) is visible and one ACA, EACA, QACA, or No row has a checked/filled square, return that selected row. Do not return null from that page.
- The controlling source is item "Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferrals", especially the question "Will the automatic Elective Deferral enrollment provisions apply".
- If item 9(a) selects the Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement (EACA) row, return EACA even if a QACA subsection appears later on the following pages.
- Treat later QACA details as active only when the plan-level automatic-enrollment election selected the Qualified Automatic Contribution Arrangement (QACA) row.
- In reconcile or QA, preserve a candidate whose raw text cites item 9(a) and a selected ACA/EACA/QACA row over a later candidate that only cites QACA explanation text.
- Do not infer QACA from references to Plan Section 3.01(F), safe harbor formulas, or QACA requirements unless the QACA row in item 9(a) is visibly selected.
- Do not use item 10 Current Employees, New Employees, default percent, increase percent, or timing options to decide whether automatic enrollment applies at the plan level.
- A selected "No" inside item 10 Current Employees means current employees are not covered by that subsection. It does not override item 9(a) when item 9(a) selected ACA, EACA, or QACA.
- Ignore nearby unselected EACA/QACA labels and safe harbor contribution text.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option says Eligible Automatic Contribution Arrangement or EACA, return value "EACA Automatic Enrollment".
- If the selected option says Qualified Automatic Contribution Arrangement or QACA, return value "QACA Automatic Enrollment".
- If the selected option says No automatic enrollment or automatic elective deferrals do not apply, return value "Not Applicable".

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected option label and automatic-enrollment wording in _raw_text.
- Do not use an EACA election as evidence of a QACA safe harbor contribution.
- Do not use safe harbor contribution language as evidence for this field.


## qaca_eaca_effective_date

Field: qaca_eaca_effective_date
Description: Effective date of QACA or EACA automatic enrollment. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferrals, EACA provisions, Initial Plan Document was effective
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return the date as MM/DD/YYYY, or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the automatic-enrollment election and determine whether ACA, EACA, or QACA is selected.
- Also find Section One, Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date, especially "The Initial Plan Document was effective on".
- Look for a specific ACA/EACA/QACA effective date. If no separate date exists, use the Initial Plan Document effective date when the automatic-enrollment provisions apply from the plan start.
- Do not use the amendment/restatement effective date when the Initial Plan Document effective date is visible and there is no separate automatic-enrollment effective date.

Selection evidence:
- Automatic enrollment must be selected somewhere in the document before the final value can be populated.
- In page-level extraction, do not reject the Initial Plan Document effective date just because the automatic-enrollment election is on another page.
- During final reconcile or QA, combine the selected ACA/EACA/QACA election with the Initial Plan Document effective date even when those facts came from different page-level candidates.
- A plan date can be used only when no separate automatic-enrollment effective date is printed.
- If the request includes full document page images, use them to connect the selected EACA/ACA/QACA election to the Initial Plan Document effective date.

Value mapping:
- If a specific EACA/QACA effective date is printed, return that date.
- If selected ACA/EACA/QACA applies from the plan start and no separate date is printed, return the Initial Plan Document effective date.
- Return null when the current page/request lacks both automatic-enrollment evidence and source-date evidence.

Source evidence:
- Do not infer a date from signature dates, restatement dates, payroll timing, or unrelated contribution timing.

## default_enrollment_pct

Field: default_enrollment_pct
Description: Default deferral percentage for automatically enrolled employees. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.03 = 3%).
Type: float
Example Identifiers: Default enrollment percentage, Automatic deferral percentage
Special Instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when automatic_enrollment_type.value does not equal "Not Applicable". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Default enrollment percentage; Automatic deferral percentage.

## auto_escalation_pct

Field: auto_escalation_pct
Description: Annual auto-escalation increment. Expressed as decimal.
Type: float
Example Identifiers: Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase, by percent per payroll, automatic increase
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return the numeric decimal value for the annual auto-escalation increment, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Convert percentages to decimals.

Label discovery:
- Find the Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase subsection under ACA/EACA/QACA automatic enrollment.
- This subsection can appear under item 10 ACA and EACA after the plan-level automatic enrollment election.
- Do not use QACA safe harbor contribution limits, ACP/ADP safe harbor limits, or "six-percent of Compensation" contribution-limit text for this field.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected increase option and read the filled percent blank in that selected option.
- A selected Yes/increase row with blanks for annual percent and maximum percent is the controlling source for both the annual increase and the maximum cap.
- Filled-in numbers printed in that selected increase row are the values for the blanks. Do not treat them as placeholders and do not return null.
- If the page image shows numbers printed in the blanks of the selected increase sentence, use those numbers even when OCR splits them away from the sentence.
- The first filled percent in the selected increase sentence is the annual increase. The second filled percent is the maximum cap.
- Do not return null merely because the OCR separates the filled number from the sentence. Use the image to connect the number to the selected increase row.
- OCR may put the filled numbers on lines immediately before the selected increase sentence. If the page image shows those numbers fill that selected row, use the first number as the annual increase percent.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text omits checkbox symbols. Do not return null when the image shows the increase row selected and the option text contains the filled percent.
- Ignore unselected increase percentages.

Value mapping:
- Convert the selected annual increase percent to a decimal by dividing by 100.
- Return null when auto-escalation is not selected, automatic enrollment is not applicable, or the current page/request lacks the increase subsection.

Source evidence:
- Do not use the default automatic deferral percentage or maximum cap as the annual increase.

## maximum_auto_enrollment_pct

Field: maximum_auto_enrollment_pct
Description: Maximum deferral percentage under auto-escalation. Expressed as decimal.
Type: float
Example Identifiers: Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase, up to a maximum, maximum percent
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return the numeric decimal value for the maximum automatic enrollment or auto-escalation cap, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Convert percentages to decimals.

Label discovery:
- Find the Authorization of Automatic Elective Deferral Increase subsection under ACA/EACA/QACA automatic enrollment.
- This subsection can appear under item 10 ACA and EACA after the plan-level automatic enrollment election.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected increase option and read the maximum percent in that selected option.
- A selected Yes/increase row with blanks for annual percent and maximum percent is the controlling source for both the annual increase and the maximum cap.
- Filled-in numbers printed in that selected increase row are the values for the blanks. Do not treat them as placeholders and do not return null.
- If the page image shows numbers printed in the blanks of the selected increase sentence, use those numbers even when OCR splits them away from the sentence.
- The second filled percent in the selected increase sentence is the maximum cap. The first filled percent is the annual increase.
- Do not return null merely because the OCR separates the filled number from the sentence. Use the image to connect the number to the selected increase row.
- OCR may put the filled numbers on lines immediately before the selected increase sentence. If the page image shows those numbers fill that selected row, use the second number as the maximum percent.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text omits checkbox symbols. Do not return null when the image shows the increase row selected and the option text contains the filled maximum percent.
- Ignore maximum values in unselected options.

Value mapping:
- Convert the selected maximum percent to a decimal by dividing by 100.
- Return null when auto-escalation is not selected, automatic enrollment is not applicable, or the current page/request lacks the increase subsection.

Source evidence:
- Do not use the annual increase percent as the maximum cap.
- Do not use the six-percent QACA/safe-harbor contribution cap as the maximum automatic enrollment cap.

## escalation_timing

Field: escalation_timing
Description: Escalation timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Escalation timing, Automatic increase timing
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["First day of each succeeding plan year", "Anniversary date of when participant's automatic enrollment was implemented", "First day of the second plan year after automatic deferrals were first implemented", "Every January", "Every February", "Every March", "Every April", "Every May", "Every June", "Every July", "Every August", "Every September", "Every October", "Every November", "Every December", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Only populate this field when automatic_enrollment_type.value does not equal "Not Applicable". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Escalation timing; Automatic increase timing.

## escalation_timing_other_specify

Field: escalation_timing_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when escalation_timing resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Escalation Timing Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when escalation_timing.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Escalation Timing Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## permissible_withdrawal_window

Field: permissible_withdrawal_window
Description: Window within which a newly auto-enrolled participant may withdraw their automatic contributions..
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Permissible Withdrawals of EACA or QACA Elective Deferrals, Permissible Withdrawal Period, Withdrawal Period
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not permitted", "Permitted within 30 days of participant's automatic enrollment", "Permitted within 60 days of participant's automatic enrollment", "Permitted within 90 days of participant's automatic enrollment"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Permissible Withdrawals of EACA or QACA Elective Deferrals section.
- Use the Authorization of Permissible Withdrawals subsection only to decide whether withdrawals are permitted.
- Use the Permissible Withdrawal Period subsection to choose the number of days.
- The deciding sentence often begins "A Participant's election to make a permissible withdrawal must be made within"; use the selected period row governed by that sentence.

Selection evidence:
- Treat authorization and period as two separate option groups, even when both reuse the same numbering or row labels.
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected row inside the Permissible Withdrawal Period subsection.
- A checkmark, filled box, or X immediately before or after a period option label selects that period row when the page image confirms the row alignment.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected period option label; treat the following option row as selected when it is visually aligned with that mark.
- When the standalone mark appears between two period option labels, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- The selected mark may appear on the option-label line while the day-count text continues on the next line; read the whole selected row.
- Ignore unselected period rows, Form of Distribution options, and default notes when any period row is visibly selected.
- Use a default period only when every row in the Permissible Withdrawal Period subsection is visibly unselected. Do not choose 30 days from the default note when a later period row has a visible selected mark.

Value mapping:
- If the selected authorization option says No, return value "Not permitted".
- If the selected period is 30, 60, or 90 days following the first automatic contribution, return the matching enum.
- Return null when automatic enrollment is not applicable or the current page/request does not contain the permissible-withdrawal section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected authorization and period option text in _raw_text.

## eligible_employees

Field: eligible_employees
Description: Automatic enrollment eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: ACA and EACA, New Employees, Employees subject to automatic enrollment
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["All Employees", "Newly Eligible", "Participants not contributing", "Affirmative Election of at least Automatic Deferral amount", "No existing Affirmative Election", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the ACA/EACA/QACA subsection that asks which employees are subject to automatic enrollment.
- Relevant labels may say New Employees, employees who meet eligibility requirements on or after the Effective Date, existing participants, participants not contributing, or affirmative election.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkbox or marked option in the automatic-enrollment eligible-employees option group.
- Ignore general eligibility text outside the automatic-enrollment section unless the selected option points to it.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option applies to employees who meet eligibility requirements on or after the Effective Date, return value "Newly Eligible".
- If the selected option applies to all eligible employees, return value "All Employees".
- If the selected option applies to participants not contributing, return value "Participants not contributing".
- If the selected wording is not captured by one enum, return value "Other" and preserve the full selected wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected option and governed employee population text in _raw_text.


## eligible_employees_other_specify

Field: eligible_employees_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Eligible Employees Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when eligible_employees.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Eligible Employees Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

# Output Contract

- Custom workflow step: `adp_f4_employee_contributions_and_automatic_enrollment`
- Workflow group: `adp_f4_employee_contributions_and_automatic_enrollment`
- Step kind: `instruct`
- Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
- Use only these output keys:
- `contribution_limit`
- `contribution_limit_other_specify`
- `contribution_method`
- `deferral_change_frequency`
- `deferral_change_frequency_other_specify`
- `roth_elective_deferrals`
- `after_tax_voluntary_contributions`
- `automatic_enrollment_type`
- `qaca_eaca_effective_date`
- `default_enrollment_pct`
- `auto_escalation_pct`
- `maximum_auto_enrollment_pct`
- `escalation_timing`
- `escalation_timing_other_specify`
- `permissible_withdrawal_window`
- `eligible_employees`
- `eligible_employees_other_specify`

# Final Notes

- Use the value in `Field` as the key in your JSON response for the given field.
- If you cannot identify a field with confidence, exclude it unless that field's instructions explicitly require `null`.
- If you cannot find any fields with confidence, return an empty JSON object like this: `{}`.
- Do not add commentary, markdown fences, or explanation text.
- Only return the JSON object in your response.
prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **contribution_limit** - Maximum deferral limit expressed as percentage of compensation.
- **contribution_limit_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when contribution_limit resolves to 'Other'.
- **contribution_method** - Method(s) by which employees may elect deferrals.
- **deferral_change_frequency** - Deferral change frequency as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **deferral_change_frequency_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when deferral_change_frequency resolves to 'Other'.
- **roth_elective_deferrals** - Are Roth (after-tax) elective deferrals permitted?.
- **after_tax_voluntary_contributions** - Mirror of eligibility_requirements.after_tax_contributions kept in the Employee Contributions section because the workflow surfaces the concept in both places. Values should match unless the document itself is contradictory.
- **automatic_enrollment_type** - Automatic enrollment as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **qaca_eaca_effective_date** - Effective date of QACA or EACA automatic enrollment. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
- **default_enrollment_pct** - Default deferral percentage for automatically enrolled employees. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.03 = 3%).
- **auto_escalation_pct** - Annual auto-escalation increment. Expressed as decimal.
- **maximum_auto_enrollment_pct** - Maximum deferral percentage under auto-escalation. Expressed as decimal.
- **escalation_timing** - Escalation timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **escalation_timing_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when escalation_timing resolves to 'Other'.
- **permissible_withdrawal_window** - Window within which a newly auto-enrolled participant may withdraw their automatic contributions..
- **eligible_employees** - Automatic enrollment eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f4_employee_contributions_and_automatic_enrollment` in workflow group `adp_f4_employee_contributions_and_automatic_enrollment`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f5_employer_contributions Definition

Extract employer contributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## safe_harbor_type

Field: safe_harbor_type
Description: Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor CODA Contributions, ADP Test Safe Harbor, Safe Harbor Provisions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Applicable", "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 3% AND 50% ON NEXT 2% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 4% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 6% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "POSSIBLE SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "QACA SAFE HARBOR TIERED MATCH OF 100% ON THE FIRST 1% AND 50% ON THE NEXT 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% QACA SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Safe Harbor CODA Contributions / ADP Test Safe Harbor section as the controlling source.
- Do not use QACA automatic-enrollment, ACP safe harbor, or printed formula tables by themselves.
- A QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contribution section inside automatic-enrollment provisions is not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA applicability question.

Selection evidence:
- First inspect the page image for the selected Yes/No row in Application of Safe Harbor CODA.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- A selected No row is a real answer and controls the field; return Not Applicable, not null or Not Indicated.
- Formula choices printed below a selected No are inactive boilerplate.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means this field is "Not Applicable".
- If a page only contains QACA, ACP, or safe-harbor formula text and does not show the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Do not return a QACA safe harbor formula unless the same source path also shows that the controlling safe-harbor applicability question selected Yes.
- If Yes is selected, then use the selected safe harbor contribution formula in the same section.
- In reconcile or QA, a source-backed No from Application of Safe Harbor CODA beats formula text that does not also prove Safe Harbor CODA was selected Yes.

Value mapping:
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA selects No, return value "Not Applicable".
- If a safe harbor matching formula or nonelective formula is selected, return the closest matching enum value.
- If the document indicates safe harbor applies but does not provide details, return value "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED".
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the safe harbor label but no selected option is visible. If the safe harbor section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor option and contribution formula text in _raw_text.
- Do not treat automatic enrollment as a safe harbor contribution.
- Do not use EACA automatic enrollment or ACP test safe harbor matching language as the answer when ADP test safe harbor is not selected.


## safe_harbor_nonelective_pct

Field: safe_harbor_nonelective_pct
Description: Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
Type: float
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor nonelective percentage, Nonelective contribution percentage
Special Instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor nonelective percentage; Nonelective contribution percentage.

## safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution

Field: safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution
Description: Safe harbor contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor contribution timing, Timing of safe harbor contribution, Safe Harbor Timing Of Contribution
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated", "Not Applicable"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor contribution timing; Timing of safe harbor contribution; Safe Harbor Timing Of Contribution.

## safe_harbor_annual_true_up

Field: safe_harbor_annual_true_up
Description: Is there an annual true-up of safe harbor contributions?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor true-up, Annual true-up, Safe Harbor Annual True Up
Special Instructions:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor true-up; Annual true-up; Safe Harbor Annual True Up.

## safe_harbor_eligible_employees

Field: safe_harbor_eligible_employees
Description: Safe harbor eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor eligible employees, Employees receiving safe harbor contribution
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement", "Allocated to the NHCEs only", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor eligible employees; Employees receiving safe harbor contribution.

## safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify

Field: safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor Eligible Employees Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when safe_harbor_eligible_employees.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe Harbor Eligible Employees Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## employer_match_type

Field: employer_match_type
Description: Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Matching Contribution Formula, Discretionary Match, Fixed Match
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No Match", "Fixed Match", "Discretionary Match", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the matching contribution formula section.
- This field is for employer matching contributions, separate from safe harbor contributions and profit sharing contributions.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected option in the matching contribution formula section.
- Ignore eligibility entry dates, safe harbor contribution formulas, and profit sharing formulas.

Selection from OCR text (page image may be unavailable):
- A selected option is shown by a filled/checked box or by its label appearing without the preceding empty-box glyph.
- A discretionary match is elected by selecting the discretionary-matching option; it has NO numeric formula filled in. Do not return "No Match" merely because no fixed percentage/formula numbers appear.

Value mapping:
- If the document establishes a discretionary matching contribution (e.g., the Employer will determine a discretionary Matching Contribution formula), return value "Discretionary Match".
- If the selected formula gives a fixed match formula (specific percentages/tiers), return value "Fixed Match".
- Return value "No Match" only when matching contributions are explicitly not made / the no-match option is selected.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the matching contribution formula label but no selected formula or explicit no-match election is visible. If the matching contribution formula is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected matching contribution formula option in _raw_text.


## employer_match_formula_1

Field: employer_match_formula_1
Description: First tier of employer match formula. Free text as stated in plan document.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer match formula, Matching contribution formula, First tier match formula, Employer Match Formula 1
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer match formula; Matching contribution formula; First tier match formula; Employer Match Formula 1.

## employer_match_formula_2

Field: employer_match_formula_2
Description: Second tier of employer match formula, if applicable.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Second tier match formula, Additional matching contribution formula, Employer Match Formula 2
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Second tier match formula; Additional matching contribution formula; Employer Match Formula 2.

## employer_match_timing

Field: employer_match_timing
Description: Employer match timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Matching Contribution Computation Period, Computation Period, Contribution Computation Period
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Matching Contribution Computation Period section as the controlling source.
- Do not use compensation inclusion, contribution formula, or payroll-period language outside that section as timing evidence.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the row inside the Matching Contribution Computation Period option group.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected period option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- When the standalone mark appears between two computation-period labels, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Ignore blank squares and unselected rows, even when they appear first or contain "payroll period".
- Ignore marks in compensation tables above this section; those table cells are not timing elections.
- Use a default only when every row in the Matching Contribution Computation Period option group is visibly unselected. Do not choose payroll period from the first row when a later computation-period row has a visible selected mark.

Value mapping:
- If the selected row says the employer payroll period, return value "Every Pay Period".
- If the selected row says annually or annual, return value "Annually".
- If monthly, quarterly, or semi-annual timing is selected, return the closest enum; semi-annual should be "Other" only if the enum cannot express it.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the Matching Contribution Computation Period section is visible but no option/default can be determined.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Matching Contribution Computation Period section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected computation-period option in _raw_text.

## employer_match_true_up

Field: employer_match_true_up
Description: Is there an annual true-up of employer matching contributions?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Matching Contribution Computation Period, re-calculate the Matching Contribution, true-up
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the Matching Contribution Computation Period and any matching-contribution recalculation or true-up language.
- This field asks about an annual true-up for employer matching contributions, not the general timing label alone.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected matching contribution computation-period options together with selected matching contribution formula timing.
- Do not infer true-up from boilerplate notes that start with "If Matching Contributions are made more frequently..." unless the document also selects or states that matching contributions are actually made more frequently than the computation period.
- If the computation period is Annually and there is no separate selected option saying contributions are made more frequently than annually, do not emit a true-up value from the conditional note alone.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected matching contribution terms require annual or computation-period recalculation after more frequent contributions.
- Return "false" only when the source directly says no true-up or no recalculation.
- Return null when the source only contains conditional recalculation boilerplate, when matching contributions are not applicable, or when the current page/request lacks true-up/recalculation evidence.

Source evidence:
- Preserve the computation-period and recalculation wording used for the decision.

## employer_match_allocation_conditions

Field: employer_match_allocation_conditions
Description: Conditions employees must meet to receive the employer match.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions, Last Day Requirement, Hours of Service Requirement
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["None - All eligible participants receive Match", "Must complete 500 hours of service", "Must complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 1000 hours of service"].

Label discovery:
- Find the additional conditions for receiving matching contributions section.
- Relevant rows include last day requirement, hours of service requirement, and all-eligible/no additional condition options.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected conditions under matching contributions.
- Treat a selected additional-conditions framework plus a selected Last Day Requirement row as an actual last-day condition.
- Do not return no additional conditions merely because the No Additional Conditions row is unselected or absent.
- Read selected last-day and hours rows together, including whether the condition says AND or OR.

prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **safe_harbor_type** - Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
- **safe_harbor_nonelective_pct** - Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
- **safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution** - Safe harbor contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_annual_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of safe harbor contributions?
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees** - Safe harbor eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_match_type** - Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
- **employer_match_formula_1** - First tier of employer match formula. Free text as stated in plan document.
- **employer_match_formula_2** - Second tier of employer match formula, if applicable.
- **employer_match_timing** - Employer match timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of employer matching contributions?
- **employer_match_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive the employer match.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees** - Match eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_match_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_type** - Profit sharing contribution as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method** - Profit sharing allocation method as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_timing** - Profit sharing contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive profit sharing allocation.
- **employer_profit_sharing_eligible_employees** - Profit sharing eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f5_employer_contributions` in workflow group `adp_f5_employer_contributions`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f5_employer_contributions Definition

Extract employer contributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## safe_harbor_type

Field: safe_harbor_type
Description: Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor CODA Contributions, ADP Test Safe Harbor, Safe Harbor Provisions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Applicable", "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 3% AND 50% ON NEXT 2% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 4% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 6% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "POSSIBLE SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "QACA SAFE HARBOR TIERED MATCH OF 100% ON THE FIRST 1% AND 50% ON THE NEXT 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% QACA SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Safe Harbor CODA Contributions / ADP Test Safe Harbor section as the controlling source.
- Do not use QACA automatic-enrollment, ACP safe harbor, or printed formula tables by themselves.
- A QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contribution section inside automatic-enrollment provisions is not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA applicability question.

Selection evidence:
- First inspect the page image for the selected Yes/No row in Application of Safe Harbor CODA.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- A selected No row is a real answer and controls the field; return Not Applicable, not null or Not Indicated.
- Formula choices printed below a selected No are inactive boilerplate.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means this field is "Not Applicable".
- If a page only contains QACA, ACP, or safe-harbor formula text and does not show the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Do not return a QACA safe harbor formula unless the same source path also shows that the controlling safe-harbor applicability question selected Yes.
- If Yes is selected, then use the selected safe harbor contribution formula in the same section.
- In reconcile or QA, a source-backed No from Application of Safe Harbor CODA beats formula text that does not also prove Safe Harbor CODA was selected Yes.

Value mapping:
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA selects No, return value "Not Applicable".
- If a safe harbor matching formula or nonelective formula is selected, return the closest matching enum value.
- If the document indicates safe harbor applies but does not provide details, return value "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED".
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the safe harbor label but no selected option is visible. If the safe harbor section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor option and contribution formula text in _raw_text.
- Do not treat automatic enrollment as a safe harbor contribution.
- Do not use EACA automatic enrollment or ACP test safe harbor matching language as the answer when ADP test safe harbor is not selected.


## safe_harbor_nonelective_pct

Description: Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
Type: float
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor nonelective percentage, Nonelective contribution percentage
Special Instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor nonelective percentage; Nonelective contribution percentage.

## safe_harbor_eligible_employees

Field: safe_harbor_eligible_employees
Description: Safe harbor eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor eligible employees, Employees receiving safe harbor contribution
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.

- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor eligible employees; Employees receiving safe harbor contribution.

## safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify

Field: safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.

Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.

- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe Harbor Eligible Employees Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## employer_match_type

Field: employer_match_type
Description: Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Matching Contribution Formula, Discretionary Match, Fixed Match
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No Match", "Fixed Match", "Discretionary Match", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the matching contribution formula section.
- This field is for employer matching contributions, separate from safe harbor contributions and profit sharing contributions.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected option in the matching contribution formula section.
- Ignore eligibility entry dates, safe harbor contribution formulas, and profit sharing formulas.

Selection from OCR text (page image may be unavailable):
- A selected option is shown by a filled/checked box or by its label appearing without the preceding empty-box glyph.
- A discretionary match is elected by selecting the discretionary-matching option; it has NO numeric formula filled in. Do not return "No Match" merely because no fixed percentage/formula numbers appear.

Value mapping:
- If the document establishes a discretionary matching contribution (e.g., the Employer will determine a discretionary Matching Contribution formula), return value "Discretionary Match".
- If the selected formula gives a fixed match formula (specific percentages/tiers), return value "Fixed Match".
- Return value "No Match" only when matching contributions are explicitly not made / the no-match option is selected.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the matching contribution formula label but no selected formula or explicit no-match election is visible. If the matching contribution formula is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected matching contribution formula option in _raw_text.

Label discovery:
- Use the Matching Contribution Computation Period section as the controlling source.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the row inside the Matching Contribution Computation Period option group.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected period option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- When the standalone mark appears between two computation-period labels, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.

Value mapping:
- If the selected row says the employer payroll period, return value "Every Pay Period".
- If the selected row says annually or annual, return value "Annually".

Source evidence:

Output contract:

Label discovery:

Selection evidence:
- Use selected matching contribution computation-period options together with selected matching contribution formula timing.
- Do not infer true-up from boilerplate notes that start with "If Matching Contributions are made more frequently..." unless the document also selects or states that matching contributions are actually made more frequently than the computation period.
- If the computation period is Annually and there is no separate selected option saying contributions are made more frequently than annually, do not emit a true-up value from the conditional note alone.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected matching contribution terms require annual or computation-period recalculation after more frequent contributions.
- Return "false" only when the source directly says no true-up or no recalculation.
- Return null when the source only contains conditional recalculation boilerplate, when matching contributions are not applicable, or when the current page/request lacks true-up/recalculation evidence.

Source evidence:

Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["None - All eligible participants receive Match", "Must complete 500 hours of service", "Must complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 1000 hours of service"].

Label discovery:
- Find the additional conditions for receiving matching contributions section.
- Relevant rows include last day requirement, hours of service requirement, and all-eligible/no additional condition options.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected conditions under matching contributions.
- Treat a selected additional-conditions framework plus a selected Last Day Requirement row as an actual last-day condition.
- Do not return no additional conditions merely because the No Additional Conditions row is unselected or absent.
- Read selected last-day and hours rows together, including whether the condition says AND or OR.
- Do not treat the Service Requirement row as selected unless its own checkbox/mark is selected.
- Parenthetical text such as "(not more than 1,000) Hours of Service" or "(not more than 12) months" is a limit on the blank, not a filled-in service requirement value.
- Blank underscores are not values. Do not convert a blank line plus a maximum-hours parenthetical into a service-hours requirement.
- A selected additional-conditions framework means some additional condition applies; it does not by itself select every subcondition printed underneath.
- Ignore matching formula text that explains allocation amount rather than eligibility conditions.

Value mapping:
- If only Last Day Requirement is selected, return value "Must be employed on last day of plan year".
- If last-day and hours requirements are both selected, choose the matching AND/OR enum based on the source wording.
- If only an hours requirement is selected, return the matching hours enum.
- If no additional matching allocation condition is selected, return value "None - All eligible participants receive Match".

Source evidence:

# Output Contract

# Final Notes

- Use the value in `Field` as the key in your JSON response for the given field.
