- Use the adoption-agreement question that asks whether an employer that is not a Related Employer will be allowed to participate in the Plan.
- The controlling labels include "Employers That Are Not Related Employers" and "Will an Employer that is not a Related Employer of the Adopting Employer be allowed to participate in the Plan?"
- Do not use later Participating Employer Election signature pages as the answer. Those pages are attachments and may repeat multiple-employer boilerplate.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes or No answer in the controlling adoption-agreement question.
- If the controlling selected answer says No or says non-related employers may not participate, return "false" even if later participating-employer pages mention multiple employer plan participation.
- Use later Participating Employer Election pages only as supporting evidence when the controlling question is absent.

Selection from OCR text (page image may be unavailable):
- A selected option is shown by a filled/checked box, or by its label appearing WITHOUT the empty-box glyph (e.g., a box character) that precedes unselected options.
- The form default note ("If no option is selected, ...") indicates the fallback; a plan is single-employer (not a MEP) by default.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" only when the controlling question affirmatively selects Yes (a non-Related Employer is allowed to participate).
- Return "false" when the controlling question selects No OR when no multiple-employer election is affirmatively selected (single-employer is the default).
- Do not return null when the controlling question is present on the page; resolve to "false" unless Yes is clearly selected.

## plan_name

Field: plan_name
Description: Plan name as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Plan name, Name of Plan
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Plan name; Name of Plan.

## plan_status

Field: plan_status
Description: Status of the plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Format: enumerated value
Example Identifiers: New plan, Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date, Plan status
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["New Plan", "Amendment & Restatement"].

Label discovery:
- Find the option group or form area that asks whether the plan/adoption agreement is being newly established or whether an existing plan is being amended, restated, updated, or replaced.
- Use source labels that mean plan status, including labels like: New plan; Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date; Plan status.
- A relevant label does not have to match the identifiers exactly. It is relevant when its meaning is about plan status: first-time plan setup, initial adoption, new plan effective date, existing plan, amendment, restatement, amendment effective date, or restatement date.

Selection evidence:
- Identify the selected value by pairing each checkbox, tick mark, filled box, or other selection mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it.
- Nearby option text is not evidence by itself. The checkbox or selection mark confirms that option when selected and rejects that option when unselected.
- Do not treat an unselected checkbox label as the answer, even when that label contains an enum value or identifier.

Value mapping:
- If the selected label says the plan is new, newly established, or an initial adoption, return value "New Plan".
- If the selected label says the plan is existing, amended, restated, an amendment, a restatement, or an "Existing Plan Amendment or Restatement Date", return value "Amendment & Restatement".

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby date text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

## oe_date

Field: oe_date
Description: Open Enrollment / Original Effective Date of the plan. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Original effective date, Effective date, Initial effective date
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Original effective date; Effective date; Initial effective date.

## restatement_effective_date

Field: restatement_effective_date
Description: Effective date of this restatement if plan_status is 'Amendment & Restatement'. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Restatement effective date, Effective date of restatement
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Restatement effective date; Effective date of restatement.

## plan_year

Field: plan_year
Description: Plan year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Plan Year Means, Plan Year, Adopting Employer's tax year
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Calendar Year", "Fiscal Year", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Use only the option group headed Plan Year Means. In ADP forms, this is often headed Part G. Plan Year Means and may appear immediately after Part F. Limitation Year Means.
- Do not use Limitation Year Means, tax year, valuation year, or unrelated Plan Year references.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the row inside the Plan Year Means option group.
- A checkmark, filled box, or X immediately before or after an option label selects that option row when the page image confirms the row alignment.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat the following option row as selected when it is visually aligned with that mark.
- When a standalone mark appears between two Plan Year Means option labels, attach it to the following option row, not the preceding row or default row.
- The selected mark may appear on the option-label line while the value text continues on the next line; read the whole selected row.
- Ignore unselected rows, even when they appear first or contain enum words.
- Use a default note only when every row in the Plan Year Means option group is visibly unselected. Do not choose the first/default row when a later row has a visible selected mark.
- If Plan Year Means has a visible selected row, return that value instead of null.

Value mapping:
- If the selected Plan Year Means option is the calendar year, return value "Calendar Year".
- If the selected option is the employer tax year or a fiscal 12-month period, return value "Fiscal Year" unless the selected wording is neither calendar nor fiscal.
- If the selected wording is a 52/53 week period or other custom period, return value "Other" and preserve the wording in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Plan Year Means section.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected Plan Year Means option in _raw_text.

## plan_year_other_specify

Field: plan_year_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when plan_year resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Other plan year, Other - specify plan year, Plan Year Other Specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when plan_year.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other plan year; Other - specify plan year; Plan Year Other Specify.

## plan_year_end_date

Field: plan_year_end_date
Description: Only populated when plan_year is 'Fiscal Year' or 'Other'. Format: MM/DD.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Plan year end, Fiscal year end, Plan year ending, Plan Year End Date
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when plan_year.value is one of "Fiscal Year", "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Plan year end; Fiscal year end; Plan year ending; Plan Year End Date.

## valuation_date

Field: valuation_date
Description: Valuation date as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Valuation Date, Daily, Plan Valuation Date
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every day (Daily Valuation)", "Last day of each Plan Year", "Last day of each Plan Year half (Semi-Annual)", "Last day of each Plan Year quarter", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the valuation-date section or table row that says when the plan value is determined.
- In ADP adoption agreements, this may appear as Part K, Valuation Date.
- Relevant labels may say valuation date, plan valuation date, allocation date, daily, quarter, semi-annual, annual, or other timing language.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkbox, tick mark, filled box, or marked option in the valuation-date option group.
- Treat the named valuation-date option group as the controlling source. If it is visible and a row is selected, return the selected value instead of returning null.
- If OCR separates the mark from the option text, use the page image to pair the mark with the aligned valuation-date row.
- When a standalone mark appears between valuation-date rows, attach it to the following row if page-image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Use default notes only when every valuation-date option row is visibly unselected. Do not return null when the valuation-date section is visible and a row is selected.
- Do not use selected rows from nearby required beginning date, retirement age, life insurance, or participant-direction sections.
- Ignore unselected timing labels in the same option group.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option says Daily, every day, or daily valuation, return value "Every day (Daily Valuation)".
- If the selected option says the last day of each Plan Year, return value "Last day of each Plan Year".
- If the selected option says semi-annual or each Plan Year half, return value "Last day of each Plan Year half (Semi-Annual)".
- If the selected option says quarterly or each Plan Year quarter, return value "Last day of each Plan Year quarter".
- If the selected source wording is a different valuation timing, return value "Other" and preserve the written source wording in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected source label and nearby timing text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer a valuation frequency from unrelated allocation, payroll, or contribution timing text.


## valuation_date_other_specify

Field: valuation_date_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when valuation_date resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Other valuation date, Other - specify valuation date, Valuation Date Other Specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when valuation_date.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other valuation date; Other - specify valuation date; Valuation Date Other Specify.

## plan_number

Field: plan_number
Description: Plan number as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Plan number, Three-digit plan number
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["001", "002", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Plan number; Three-digit plan number.

## plan_number_other_specify

Field: plan_number_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when plan_number resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Other plan number, Other - specify plan number, Plan Number Other Specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when plan_number.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other plan number; Other - specify plan number; Plan Number Other Specify.

## administrators_info

Field: administrators_info
Description: Plan administrator as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Plan administrator, Administrator, Administrative committee, Administrators Info
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Employer", "Committee Appointed by Employer", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Plan administrator; Administrator; Administrative committee; Administrators Info.

## administrators_info_other_specify

Field: administrators_info_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when administrators_info resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Other plan administrator, Other - specify administrator, Administrators Info Other Specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when administrators_info.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other plan administrator; Other - specify administrator; Administrators Info Other Specify.

## type_of_plan

Field: type_of_plan
Description: Type of plan as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Type of plan, 401(k) plan, Profit sharing plan
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["401(k) Plan", "Profit Sharing Plan", "Money Purchase Pension Plan"].

Label discovery:
- Use the plan title, adoption agreement title, or Name of Plan field.
- This field asks for the primary plan type, not every phrase in the plan name.

Selection evidence:
- If the title includes "401(k) Profit Sharing Plan", treat the 401(k) part as the primary plan type.
- Do not choose "Profit Sharing Plan" merely because "Profit Sharing" appears in the standard 401(k) profit sharing plan title.

Value mapping:
- If the source title or plan name includes 401(k), return value "401(k) Plan".
- If the source title identifies only profit sharing and does not include 401(k), return value "Profit Sharing Plan".
- If the source title identifies a money purchase pension plan, return value "Money Purchase Pension Plan".

Source evidence:
- Put the exact plan title or Name of Plan text in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

# Output Contract

- Custom workflow step: `adp_f1_employer_and_plan_information`
- Workflow group: `adp_f1_employer_and_plan_information`
- Step kind: `instruct`
- Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
- Use only these output keys:
- `employer_name`
- `address_street`
- `address_city`
- `address_state`
- `address_zip`
- `telephone`
- `tax_identification_number`
- `employer_fiscal_year_ends`
- `type_of_entity`
- `type_of_entity_llc_subtype`
- `type_of_entity_other_specify`
- `affiliated_employers`
- `affiliated_employer_group_type`
- `participating_employers`
- `multiple_employer_plan`
- `plan_name`
- `plan_status`
- `oe_date`
- `restatement_effective_date`
- `plan_year`
- `plan_year_other_specify`
- `plan_year_end_date`
- `valuation_date`
- `valuation_date_other_specify`
- `plan_number`
- `plan_number_other_specify`
- `administrators_info`
- `administrators_info_other_specify`
- `type_of_plan`

# Final Notes

- Use the value in `Field` as the key in your JSON response for the given field.
- If you cannot identify a field with confidence, exclude it unless that field's instructions explicitly require `null`.
- If you cannot find any fields with confidence, return an empty JSON object like this: `{}`.
- Do not add commentary, markdown fences, or explanation text.
- Only return the JSON object in your response.
prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **employer_name** - Employer name as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **address_street** - Adopting Employer's street address from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
- **address_city** - Adopting Employer's city from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
- **address_state** - Adopting Employer's state from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
- **address_zip** - Adopting Employer's ZIP code from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
- **telephone** - Adopting Employer's telephone number from the EMPLOYER INFORMATION section (Part A).
- **tax_identification_number** - Format: XX-XXXXXXX
- **employer_fiscal_year_ends** - Month and day the fiscal year ends. Format: MM/DD. Common value: 12/31.
- **type_of_entity** - Type of Entity as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **type_of_entity_llc_subtype** - Only populated when type_of_entity is 'Limited Liability Company'.
- **type_of_entity_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when type_of_entity is 'Other'.
- **affiliated_employers** - Is the employer a member of a controlled group or affiliated service group?
- **affiliated_employer_group_type** - Only populated when affiliated_employers is true. Select all that apply.
- **participating_employers** - Will affiliated employers adopt the plan as participating employers?
- **multiple_employer_plan** - Will non-affiliated employers adopt this plan as part of a MEP arrangement?
- **plan_name** - Plan name as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **plan_status** - Status of the plan or adoption agreement.
- **oe_date** - Open Enrollment / Original Effective Date of the plan. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
- **restatement_effective_date** - Effective date of this restatement if plan_status is 'Amendment & Restatement'. Format: MM/DD/YYYY.
- **plan_year** - Plan year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **plan_year_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when plan_year resolves to 'Other'.
- **plan_year_end_date** - Only populated when plan_year is 'Fiscal Year' or 'Other'. Format: MM/DD.
- **valuation_date** - Valuation date as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **valuation_date_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when valuation_date resolves to 'Other'.
- **plan_number** - Plan number as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **plan_number_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when plan_number resolves to 'Other'.
- **administrators_info** - Plan administrator as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **administrators_info_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when administrators_info resolves to 'Other'.
- **type_of_plan** - Type of plan as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f1_employer_and_plan_information` in workflow group `adp_f1_employer_and_plan_information`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f2_eligibility_requirements Definition

Extract eligibility requirements fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## excluded_employees_all_contributions

Field: excluded_employees_all_contributions
Description: Employee classes excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees, No exclusions apply, Excluded employees
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON array string whose entries are objects with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No exclusions", "Union Employees", "Nonresident Aliens", "Highly Compensated Employees", "Leased Employees", "Residents of Puerto Rico", "Interns", "Part-time Employees", "Temporary Employees", "Seasonal Employees", "Other"].
- Example shape: "[{\"value\":\"Union Employees\",\"_raw_text\":\"selected union employee class and selected contribution-source rows\"}]".

Label discovery:
- Find the Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees section, including rows that continue across pages.
- This field asks for classes excluded from all contribution types, not classes excluded only for one contribution source.

Selection evidence:
- For each excluded employee class, pair the selected class checkbox with the selected contribution-source checkboxes beneath it.
- ADP OCR may show a selected checkbox as "4", "x", a filled box, or a checked box before the class label or before each contribution-source line.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text is unclear. On ADP adoption agreements, the selected mark often appears immediately before the class label and immediately before each contribution-source line.
- If the OCR moves selected marks into a stack at the end of the page, use the page image row alignment instead of OCR order.
- OCR may show selected marks as repeated standalone lines before or beside the contribution-source labels. Use the page image to align those marks with the source rows instead of treating the standalone marks as missing labels.
- Treat a selected class plus selected contribution-source rows as real evidence even when the source text wraps across multiple lines or pages.
- When the page shows a selected excluded class followed by selected marks on every printed contribution-source row for that class, return that class even if OCR separates the marks from the labels.
- Do not require one continuous OCR sentence. Use the visual grouping: class label first, then the contribution-source rows governed by that class.
- A selected contribution-source row can be written as a continuation line under the class. If the selected rows collectively cover all listed contribution sources, include that class.
- In this ADP section, "all contribution types" means the full set of contribution-source rows printed under the selected class in Part B. Do not require proof from later plan sections before including the selected class.
- Include a class in this field when the class row is selected and every listed contribution-source line under that class is also selected: Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals and Safe Harbor Contributions, Roth Elective Deferrals, Matching Contributions, Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, and Qualified Nonelective Contributions.
- The ADP label "Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals and Safe Harbor Contributions" counts as the pre-tax/safe-harbor contribution-source line for this field.
- Do not require the source to say "all contributions" in one sentence. The visual evidence is enough when the selected class row has all printed contribution-source rows selected beneath it.
- When several selected excluded classes each have all contribution-source rows selected, return one array containing every selected class. Do not drop the whole field because the evidence spans multiple repeated row groups.
- Return every selected employee class that applies to all listed contribution sources. Do not return null just because more than one class is selected or because one selected class maps to "Other".
- If one selected class maps to "Other", still return the standard selected classes in the same JSON array string.
- A selected merger, acquisition, asset acquisition, stock acquisition, or Code section 410(b)(6)(C) class maps to "Other"; this should not cause the whole field to be null when other selected classes are also present.
- Do not return null when the Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees section is present and the page image shows selected marks for excluded classes and all contribution-source lines.
- If at least one selected excluded class has all contribution-source rows selected, return the supported selected classes. Do not require every printed class in the section to be selected.
- Page-level extraction should emit the selected classes visible on the current page. Final reconcile can merge repeated class evidence from other pages.
- When the page image shows several selected class blocks and each selected block has all listed contribution-source rows checked, return a single JSON array string with one entry per selected class.

Value mapping:
- Map selected union-employee language to "Union Employees".
- Treat "Employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement", "unit of Employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement", and employee-representative bargaining language as union-employee language.
- Map selected nonresident-alien language to "Nonresident Aliens".
- Map selected Code section 410(b)(6)(C), asset acquisition, stock acquisition, merger, or similar transaction language to "Other" and preserve the exact source text in _raw_text unless a closer enum exists.
- Return "No exclusions" only when the section selects no exclusions apply for all contribution sources.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the excluded-employee section.

Source evidence:
- Put each selected excluded class and its selected contribution-source scope in _raw_text.

## excluded_employees_all_contributions_other_specify

Field: excluded_employees_all_contributions_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when excluded_employees_all_contributions resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Other excluded employees, Other - specify excluded employees, Excluded Employees All Contributions Other Specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when any excluded_employees_all_contributions entry value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other excluded employees; Other - specify excluded employees; Excluded Employees All Contributions Other Specify.

## separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions

Field: separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions
Description: Does the plan have different eligibility requirements for employer contributions vs. elective deferrals?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Age and Eligibility Service, contribution source eligibility, different eligibility requirements
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the eligibility section that compares age, service, and entry-date requirements across Elective Deferrals, Matching Contributions, Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, Safe Harbor/QACA Safe Harbor Contributions, and Qualified Nonelective Contributions.
- This field asks whether employer contribution eligibility is different from elective-deferral eligibility. It is not asking for the age, service, or entry-date value itself.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkboxes or marked contribution-source rows for age, service, and entry dates.
- Do not treat the presence of separate contribution-source rows as proof of different eligibility. Compare the selected values across sources.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" only when selected employer-contribution eligibility requirements differ from the selected elective-deferral requirements.
- Return "false" only when the relevant eligibility rows are visible and the selected requirements are the same across elective deferrals and employer contributions, or a direct separate-eligibility question is selected No.
- Return "false" when the visible eligibility language states one shared age, service, or entry-date rule for elective deferrals, matching contributions, employer profit sharing, safe harbor contributions, and qualified nonelective contributions, with no separate employer-contribution rule selected.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the eligibility comparison needed to decide.

Source evidence:
- Preserve the selected eligibility rows in _raw_text when the output format includes raw text.
- Do not infer from unselected options or from generic contribution-source labels.

## age_requirement

Field: age_requirement
Description: Age requirement for general plan eligibility.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Age requirement, Minimum age, Eligibility age
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Age 18", "Age 19", "Age 20", "Age 20 1/2", "Age 21", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- If the plan eligibility section marks age 18 for general eligibility, return value "Age 18". Do not use contribution-specific N/A rows to override a selected general age requirement.
- The page image may be unavailable; if an age is written into the eligibility fill-in (e.g., "attainment of age 18" or "age 18" on the age-requirement line), that written age is the selected requirement. Return "N/A" only when "no age requirement" is affirmatively selected for general eligibility, not merely because a contribution-specific N/A row appears.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Age requirement; Minimum age; Eligibility age.

## age_requirement_other_specify

Field: age_requirement_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when age_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Other age requirement, Other - specify age requirement, Age Requirement Other Specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when age_requirement.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Other age requirement; Other - specify age requirement; Age Requirement Other Specify.

## service_requirement

Field: service_requirement

Type: str
Example Identifiers: Nondeductible Employee Contributions, May a Contributing Participant make Nondeductible Employee Contributions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section. In this ADP document family, nondeductible employee contributions are the source label for after-tax voluntary employee contributions.
- Do not use rollover, Roth elective deferral, top-heavy, or generic "if applicable" references as after-tax contribution evidence.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected answer row in the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following option row.
- A selected No row controls this field and means nondeductible employee contributions are not allowed.
- A selected Yes or allowed row means nondeductible employee contributions are allowed when the row governs that same question.
- This field mirrors employee_contributions.after_tax_voluntary_contributions. During reconcile or QA, keep these two values aligned to the Nondeductible Employee Contributions election unless the source document clearly contradicts itself.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected Nondeductible Employee Contributions answer permits nondeductible employee contributions.
- Return "false" when the selected answer says No/not permitted, or when the section says the No/not-permitted answer applies by default when nothing else is selected.
- During reconcile or QA across multiple page candidates, keep "false" when the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section selected No/not permitted. Do not flip this field to "true" unless the same question has a selected answer permitting nondeductible employee contributions.
- A "true" candidate that does not cite the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question is lower priority than a "false" candidate that cites selected No/not permitted.
- If any candidate for this same source question extracted selected No/not permitted, treat later generic employee-contribution evidence as lower priority. A true value requires selected Yes/allowed evidence from the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question.
- If the same page has Rollover Contributions from IRAs selected Yes and Nondeductible Employee Contributions selected No, return "false" for this field. Rollover evidence is not after-tax contribution evidence.
- Roth elective deferrals may be true while Nondeductible Employee Contributions are false. Do not copy a Roth true value into this field.
- If either mirrored after-tax field cites selected No/not permitted from the Nondeductible Employee Contributions question, both mirrored fields should resolve to "false" unless the same question selects Yes/allowed elsewhere.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Nondeductible Employee Contributions section.

Source evidence:
- Use the exact selected option label and nearby question text as evidence.

## upto_tax_contribution_pct

Field: upto_tax_contribution_pct
Description: Maximum percentage cap for after-tax (voluntary) contributions. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.10 = 10%).. Only populated when after_tax_contributions is true.
Type: float
Example Identifiers: After-tax contribution percentage, Voluntary after-tax contribution limit
Special Instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when after_tax_contributions equals "true". Otherwise return null.
- This field is only for voluntary after-tax contribution limits. Do not use the elective deferral percentage range for this field.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: After-tax contribution percentage; Voluntary after-tax contribution limit.

## simple_401k_contributions

Field: simple_401k_contributions
Description: SIMPLE 401(k) contributions as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: SIMPLE 401(k) Provisions, Will the SIMPLE 401(k) provisions apply, SIMPLE 401(k) provisions apply
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find Part K, SIMPLE 401(k) Provisions, or a question asking whether SIMPLE 401(k) provisions of the Plan apply.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes or No answer in the SIMPLE 401(k) provisions question.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected answer says SIMPLE 401(k) provisions apply.
- Return "false" when the selected answer says No/not applicable, or when the section says the No/not-applicable answer applies by default when nothing else is selected.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the SIMPLE 401(k) provisions section.

Source evidence:
- Use the exact selected SIMPLE 401(k) option text as evidence.

# Output Contract

- Custom workflow step: `adp_f2_eligibility_requirements`
- Workflow group: `adp_f2_eligibility_requirements`
- Step kind: `instruct`
- Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
- Use only these output keys:
- `excluded_employees_all_contributions`
- `excluded_employees_all_contributions_other_specify`
- `separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions`
- `age_requirement`
- `age_requirement_other_specify`
- `service_requirement`
- `service_requirement_other_specify`
- `hours_requirement`
- `hours_requirement_other_specify`
- `entry_date`
- `entry_date_other_specify`
- `deferral_age_requirement`
- `deferral_age_requirement_other_specify`
- `deferral_service_requirement`
- `deferral_entry_date`
- `matching_age_requirement`
- `matching_service_requirement`
- `matching_entry_date`
- `nec_age_requirement`
- `nec_service_requirement`
- `nec_entry_date`
- `predecessor_service`
- `rollover_contributions`
- `after_tax_contributions`
- `upto_tax_contribution_pct`
- `simple_401k_contributions`

# Final Notes

- Use the value in `Field` as the key in your JSON response for the given field.
- If you cannot identify a field with confidence, exclude it unless that field's instructions explicitly require `null`.
- If you cannot find any fields with confidence, return an empty JSON object like this: `{}`.
- Do not add commentary, markdown fences, or explanation text.
- Only return the JSON object in your response.
prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **excluded_employees_all_contributions** - Employee classes excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
- **excluded_employees_all_contributions_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when excluded_employees_all_contributions resolves to 'Other'.
- **separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions** - Does the plan have different eligibility requirements for employer contributions vs. elective deferrals?
- **age_requirement** - Age requirement for general plan eligibility.
- **age_requirement_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when age_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
- **service_requirement** - Service requirement for general plan eligibility.
- **service_requirement_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when service_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
- **hours_requirement** - Hours of service required. Common values: N/A, 250, 500, 750, 1000.
- **hours_requirement_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when hours_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
- **entry_date** - Entry date frequency for general eligibility.
- **entry_date_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when entry_date resolves to 'Other'.
- **deferral_age_requirement** - Age requirement specific to Elective Deferrals. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **deferral_age_requirement_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when deferral_age_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
- **deferral_service_requirement** - Service requirement specific to Elective Deferrals. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **deferral_entry_date** - Entry date specific to Elective Deferrals. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **matching_age_requirement** - Age requirement specific to Employer Matching Contributions. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **matching_service_requirement** - Service requirement specific to Employer Matching Contributions. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **matching_entry_date** - Entry date specific to Employer Matching Contributions. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **nec_age_requirement** - Age requirement specific to Employer Nonelective Contributions (Profit Sharing). Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **nec_service_requirement** - Service requirement specific to Employer Nonelective Contributions. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **nec_entry_date** - Entry date specific to Employer Nonelective Contributions. Only populated when separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions is true.
- **predecessor_service** - Whether prior service with a predecessor employer is recognized.
- **rollover_contributions** - Rollover contributions as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **after_tax_contributions** - Canonical Nondeductible Employee Contributions flag carried from the Eligibility Requirements / plan setup surface. This is not Roth elective deferrals.
- **upto_tax_contribution_pct** - Maximum percentage cap for after-tax (voluntary) contributions. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.10 = 10%).. Only populated when after_tax_contributions is true.
- **simple_401k_contributions** - SIMPLE 401(k) contributions as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f2_eligibility_requirements` in workflow group `adp_f2_eligibility_requirements`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f2_eligibility_requirements Definition

Extract eligibility requirements fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## excluded_employees_all_contributions

Field: excluded_employees_all_contributions
Description: Employee classes excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees, No exclusions apply, Excluded employees
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON array string whose entries are objects with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No exclusions", "Union Employees", "Nonresident Aliens", "Highly Compensated Employees", "Leased Employees", "Residents of Puerto Rico", "Interns", "Part-time Employees", "Temporary Employees", "Seasonal Employees", "Other"].
- Example shape: "[{\"value\":\"Union Employees\",\"_raw_text\":\"selected union employee class and selected contribution-source rows\"}]".

Label discovery:
- Find the Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees section, including rows that continue across pages.
- This field asks for classes excluded from all contribution types, not classes excluded only for one contribution source.

Selection evidence:
- For each excluded employee class, pair the selected class checkbox with the selected contribution-source checkboxes beneath it.
- ADP OCR may show a selected checkbox as "4", "x", a filled box, or a checked box before the class label or before each contribution-source line.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text is unclear. On ADP adoption agreements, the selected mark often appears immediately before the class label and immediately before each contribution-source line.
- If the OCR moves selected marks into a stack at the end of the page, use the page image row alignment instead of OCR order.
- OCR may show selected marks as repeated standalone lines before or beside the contribution-source labels. Use the page image to align those marks with the source rows instead of treating the standalone marks as missing labels.
- Treat a selected class plus selected contribution-source rows as real evidence even when the source text wraps across multiple lines or pages.
- When the page shows a selected excluded class followed by selected marks on every printed contribution-source row for that class, return that class even if OCR separates the marks from the labels.
- Do not require one continuous OCR sentence. Use the visual grouping: class label first, then the contribution-source rows governed by that class.
- A selected contribution-source row can be written as a continuation line under the class. If the selected rows collectively cover all listed contribution sources, include that class.
- In this ADP section, "all contribution types" means the full set of contribution-source rows printed under the selected class in Part B. Do not require proof from later plan sections before including the selected class.
- Include a class in this field when the class row is selected and every listed contribution-source line under that class is also selected: Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals and Safe Harbor Contributions, Roth Elective Deferrals, Matching Contributions, Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, and Qualified Nonelective Contributions.
- The ADP label "Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals and Safe Harbor Contributions" counts as the pre-tax/safe-harbor contribution-source line for this field.
- Do not require the source to say "all contributions" in one sentence. The visual evidence is enough when the selected class row has all printed contribution-source rows selected beneath it.
- When several selected excluded classes each have all contribution-source rows selected, return one array containing every selected class. Do not drop the whole field because the evidence spans multiple repeated row groups.
- Return every selected employee class that applies to all listed contribution sources. Do not return null just because more than one class is selected or because one selected class maps to "Other".
- If one selected class maps to "Other", still return the standard selected classes in the same JSON array string.
- A selected merger, acquisition, asset acquisition, stock acquisition, or Code section 410(b)(6)(C) class maps to "Other"; this should not cause the whole field to be null when other selected classes are also present.
- Do not return null when the Exclusion of Certain Classes of Employees section is present and the page image shows selected marks for excluded classes and all contribution-source lines.
- If at least one selected excluded class has all contribution-source rows selected, return the supported selected classes. Do not require every printed class in the section to be selected.
- Page-level extraction should emit the selected classes visible on the current page. Final reconcile can merge repeated class evidence from other pages.
- When the page image shows several selected class blocks and each selected block has all listed contribution-source rows checked, return a single JSON array string with one entry per selected class.

Value mapping:
- Map selected union-employee language to "Union Employees".
- Treat "Employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement", "unit of Employees covered by a collective bargaining agreement", and employee-representative bargaining language as union-employee language.
- Map selected nonresident-alien language to "Nonresident Aliens".
- Map selected Code section 410(b)(6)(C), asset acquisition, stock acquisition, merger, or similar transaction language to "Other" and preserve the exact source text in _raw_text unless a closer enum exists.
- Return "No exclusions" only when the section selects no exclusions apply for all contribution sources.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the excluded-employee section.

Source evidence:
- Put each selected excluded class and its selected contribution-source scope in _raw_text.

## separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions

Field: separate_eligibility_for_employer_contributions
Description: Does the plan have different eligibility requirements for employer contributions vs. elective deferrals?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Age and Eligibility Service, contribution source eligibility, different eligibility requirements
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the eligibility section that compares age, service, and entry-date requirements across Elective Deferrals, Matching Contributions, Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, Safe Harbor/QACA Safe Harbor Contributions, and Qualified Nonelective Contributions.
- This field asks whether employer contribution eligibility is different from elective-deferral eligibility. It is not asking for the age, service, or entry-date value itself.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected checkboxes or marked contribution-source rows for age, service, and entry dates.
- Do not treat the presence of separate contribution-source rows as proof of different eligibility. Compare the selected values across sources.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" only when selected employer-contribution eligibility requirements differ from the selected elective-deferral requirements.
- Return "false" only when the relevant eligibility rows are visible and the selected requirements are the same across elective deferrals and employer contributions, or a direct separate-eligibility question is selected No.
- Return "false" when the visible eligibility language states one shared age, service, or entry-date rule for elective deferrals, matching contributions, employer profit sharing, safe harbor contributions, and qualified nonelective contributions, with no separate employer-contribution rule selected.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the eligibility comparison needed to decide.

Source evidence:
- Preserve the selected eligibility rows in _raw_text when the output format includes raw text.
- Do not infer from unselected options or from generic contribution-source labels.

## age_requirement

Field: age_requirement
Description: Age requirement for general plan eligibility.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Age requirement, Minimum age, Eligibility age
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Age 18", "Age 19", "Age 20", "Age 20 1/2", "Age 21", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- If the plan eligibility section marks age 18 for general eligibility, return value "Age 18". Do not use contribution-specific N/A rows to override a selected general age requirement.
- The page image may be unavailable; if an age is written into the eligibility fill-in (e.g., "attainment of age 18" or "age 18" on the age-requirement line), that written age is the selected requirement. Return "N/A" only when "no age requirement" is affirmatively selected for general eligibility, not merely because a contribution-specific N/A row appears.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Age requirement; Minimum age; Eligibility age.

## service_requirement

Field: service_requirement
Description: Service requirement for general plan eligibility.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Service requirement, Eligibility service, Minimum service
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Immediate", "30 Days", "60 Days", "90 Days", "1 Month", "2 Months", "3 Months", "4 Months", "5 Months", "6 Months", "1 Year", "2 Years", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Use Section Two, Part A Eligibility Requirements for general plan eligibility.
- Look for the selected service option that applies across contribution sources, such as "After completing __ consecutive Months of Eligibility Service."
- Do not use later employer-contribution allocation conditions, matching/profit-sharing last-day rules, vesting service rules, or withdrawal-service rules for this general eligibility field.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the service option and read the filled number of days, months, or years.
- Inspect the page image when OCR text shows the number but not the checkbox mark.
- If "After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service" is the selected general eligibility service option, this field is 3 Months.
- Do not return N/A when the general eligibility section contains a selected months-of-service option.

Value mapping:
- Map selected consecutive-month requirements to the matching month enum, such as "3 Months".
- Map selected no-service-required language to "Immediate" or "N/A" only when that no-service option is selected.
- If the selected wording is not covered by the enum, return value "Other" and preserve the wording in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current request lacks the Section Two general eligibility service section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected service option text and filled number in _raw_text.

## hours_requirement

Field: hours_requirement
Description: Hours of service required. Common values: N/A, 250, 500, 750, 1000.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Eligibility Service Requirement, No eligibility service required, Hours of Service
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "250 Hours", "500 Hours", "750 Hours", "1000 Hours", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Eligibility Service Requirement section for general plan eligibility.
- Do not use fallback explanatory notes about 1,000 Hours of Service unless the corresponding service option is selected.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the selected service requirement option and contribution-source rows.
- Ignore unselected service options and their explanatory default notes.
- Do not copy a month-based value from service_requirement into this field. This field is only for hours-of-service counts.

Value mapping:
- If No eligibility service required is selected for the relevant contribution sources, return value "N/A".
- If a selected option requires a specific number of hours, map it to the matching hours enum.
- If a selected service requirement is based only on months/elapsed time with no hours requirement, return value "N/A" unless the schema has a closer value.
- Never return a month value such as "3 Months" for hours_requirement. Month values belong to service_requirement, not this field.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the eligibility service requirement section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected service requirement option and contribution-source rows in _raw_text.

## entry_date

Field: entry_date
Description: Entry date frequency for general eligibility.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Entry Dates, Monthly, first day of each month
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Immediate", "Monthly", "Quarterly", "Semi-Annual", "Annual", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Entry Dates section for eligibility, not the age/service requirement section.
- Relevant labels describe when an employee enters the plan after satisfying eligibility requirements.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected entry-date option and any selected contribution-source columns governed by that option.
- Do not use age, months-of-service, or immediate eligibility language as the entry-date frequency unless the entry-date section itself says immediate.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option says Monthly or first day of each month, return value "Monthly".
- If the selected option says immediate, quarterly, semi-annual, or annual, return the matching enum value.
- If the selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve the wording in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Entry Dates section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected entry-date option and selected contribution-source text in _raw_text.

## predecessor_service

Field: predecessor_service
Description: Whether prior service with a predecessor employer is recognized.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Predecessor Employer Service, service with a predecessor employer, Predecessor Employer Service
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "Recognized - specify employers", "Same as Deferral"].

Label discovery:
- Find Part H, Predecessor Employer Service, or a section asking whether service with a predecessor employer will be credited.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the Yes or No answer in the predecessor-service question.
- Do not treat merger/acquisition exclusion text as predecessor service recognition.

Value mapping:
- If the selected answer says No, or if the section says the No answer applies by default when nothing else is selected, return value "N/A".
- If the selected answer says predecessor service is recognized using the same rule as elective deferrals, return value "Same as Deferral" only when the document says that.
- If the selected answer says predecessor service is recognized and predecessor employers are specified, return value "Recognized - specify employers" and preserve the employer names in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Predecessor Employer Service section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected predecessor-service option and any named predecessor employer text in _raw_text.
