- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Plan year/1000 hours", "Plan Year / 1000 hours calculated with an equivalency method", "Employment year/Elapsed time", "Employment year/1000 hours calculated with monthly equivalency", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether all applicable employer contribution vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only inspect Measuring Period for Vesting and Service Required for Vesting if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedule options and selected measuring-period options together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, the measuring-period rules are not needed for these fields.
- Do not use elapsed-time, Plan Year, or hours rules from Measuring Period for Vesting / Service Required for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediately vested.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If a non-immediate schedule applies and the selected measuring period is Plan Year with 1000 hours, return value "Plan year/1000 hours".
- If an equivalency or elapsed-time method is selected for a non-immediate schedule, return the closest matching enum.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain vesting schedule or measuring-period evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and measuring-period option in _raw_text.

- If only allocation to eligible participants is selected, return value "Allocated to eligible participants".
- Return value "N/A: 100% vesting" only when the document provides no forfeiture-use election and the relevant contribution sources are fully vested.

Source evidence:

prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **safe_harbor_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Safe Harbor contributions.
- **safe_harbor_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_match_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Employer Matching Contributions.
- **employer_match_vesting_schedule** - Year-by-year vesting percentages. Only populated when employer_match_vesting is 'Other' or a non-standard graded schedule.
- **employer_match_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_match_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Employer Profit Sharing / Nonelective Contributions.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting_schedule** - Year-by-year vesting percentages for profit sharing. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting requires a non-standard schedule breakdown.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **vesting_service** - Vesting service as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **vesting_period_calculation** - Vesting computation period as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **forfeiture_use** - Forfeitures as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **normal_retirement_age** - Normal retirement age as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **normal_retirement_age_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when normal_retirement_age resolves to 'Other'.
- **normal_retirement_service_requirement** - Service requirement at normal retirement age, if any.
- **early_retirement_age** - Early retirement age as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **early_retirement_age_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when early_retirement_age resolves to 'Other'.
- **early_retirement_service_requirement** - Service requirement at early retirement age, if any.
- **field_100_pct_vesting_at_early_retirement** - 100% vesting at early retirement as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_definition** - Compensation definition as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_for_initial_year** - Compensation for initial year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_for_initial_year_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_for_initial_year resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_all** - Compensation components excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
- **compensation_excluded_all_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_all resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_deferrals** - Compensation components excluded for Elective Deferral purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_deferrals_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_deferrals resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_match** - Compensation components excluded for Employer Match purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_match_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_match resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_nec** - Compensation components excluded for Nonelective/Profit Sharing purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_nec_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_nec resolves to 'Other'.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f3_vesting_compensation` in workflow group `adp_f3_vesting_compensation`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f3_vesting_compensation Definition

Extract vesting compensation fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## safe_harbor_vesting

Field: safe_harbor_vesting
Description: Vesting schedule for Safe Harbor contributions.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor CODA Contributions, Application of Safe Harbor CODA, QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "100% immediate (QACA match)", "100% immediate (QACA NEC)", "2 Year Cliff for QACA match", "2 Year Cliff for QACA NEC", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether ADP Test Safe Harbor or QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are selected.
- Then find the vesting rule for the selected safe harbor contribution.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected safe harbor contribution options and selected vesting schedule options together.
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA / ADP Test Safe Harbor selects No, that selected No is enough evidence to return "N/A" for safe harbor vesting.
- A selected No in the Safe Harbor CODA applicability section is a real answer for this field, not missing evidence. Do not return null in that case.
- When the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question is visible, inspect the page image for the selected Yes or No row before looking at formula or vesting text.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- If the page image shows a selected mark on the No row in the Application of Safe Harbor CODA or ADP Test Safe Harbor applicability section, return "N/A" even if OCR does not include a checkbox symbol.
- When the standalone mark is between Yes and No rows, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means the safe-harbor vesting answer is "N/A".
- Do not require a separate vesting schedule when the controlling safe-harbor applicability section selects No. The selected No is the vesting answer for this field.
- Do not suppress the selected No just because this field is named as a vesting schedule. The applicability No is the source evidence that safe-harbor vesting is not applicable.
- If the document-level safe_harbor_type is "Not Applicable" or the source selected No for Application of Safe Harbor CODA, return "N/A" even when QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting schedule text appears elsewhere.
- In reconcile or QA, a candidate with formula or vesting-table text is lower priority than a visible selected No in the controlling safe-harbor applicability question.
- Inspect the page image for the selected No mark when OCR text drops checkbox symbols.
- If the current page/request has only QACA automatic-enrollment or formula text and does not show the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question, return null for this field.
- If a page contains only safe-harbor formula or vesting-table text and not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Ignore EACA automatic enrollment, QACA automatic-enrollment text, QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting tables, and ACP-test-only matching language when ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable.

Value mapping:
- If Safe Harbor CODA provisions or ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are explicitly not selected/applicable, return value "N/A".
- For this enum field, an explicit selected No for safe harbor applicability should be returned as value "N/A", not null.
- If selected safe harbor contributions vest immediately, return the matching immediate enum.
- If selected QACA safe harbor contributions use a two-year cliff, return the matching two-year cliff enum.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain safe harbor applicability or safe harbor vesting evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor applicability and vesting text in _raw_text.
- If ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable, do not populate QACA safe harbor vesting from automatic-enrollment or ACP-test-only matching language.

## safe_harbor_vesting_other_specify

Field: safe_harbor_vesting_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor Vesting Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when safe_harbor_vesting.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe Harbor Vesting Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## employer_match_vesting

Field: employer_match_vesting
Description: Vesting schedule for Employer Matching Contributions.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer match vesting, Matching contribution vesting schedule
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "3 Year Cliff", "6 year graded", "5 year graded", "4 year graded", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- If employer matching contributions are marked as 100% vested or immediate vesting, return value "100% immediate". Do not extract a graded schedule unless that schedule is the selected vesting election for employer matching contributions.
- Do not return "N/A" for employer match vesting when discretionary matching contributions are authorized and their selected vesting election is immediate / 100% vested.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer match vesting; Matching contribution vesting schedule.

## employer_match_vesting_schedule

Field: employer_match_vesting_schedule
Description: Year-by-year vesting percentages. Only populated when employer_match_vesting is 'Other' or a non-standard graded schedule.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Match vesting schedule, Year-by-year match vesting percentages, Employer Match Vesting Schedule
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON array string of objects, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Object keys: years_of_service, vesting_percentage.
- Only populate this field when employer_match_vesting.value requires schedule detail, such as a custom or non-standard schedule with year-by-year percentages. Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Match vesting schedule; Year-by-year match vesting percentages; Employer Match Vesting Schedule.

## employer_match_vesting_other_specify

Field: employer_match_vesting_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when employer_match_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer Match Vesting Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when employer_match_vesting.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer Match Vesting Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## employer_profit_sharing_vesting

Field: employer_profit_sharing_vesting
Description: Vesting schedule for Employer Profit Sharing / Nonelective Contributions.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule for Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, Profit Sharing, 100%
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "3 Year Cliff", "6 year graded", "5 year graded", "4 year graded", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the vesting schedule for employer profit sharing contributions.
- Use the row or column that governs profit sharing or nonelective contributions.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected vesting option for profit sharing contributions.
- Read the selected option across the years-of-vesting-service table.
- Do not use an unselected graded schedule when the selected profit sharing option is 100% immediate.

Value mapping:
- If the selected profit sharing schedule is 100% for less than one year and every later year, return value "100% immediate".
- If a cliff or graded schedule is selected, return the matching enum.
- Return value "N/A" only when the current page section contains the profit sharing/nonelective contribution or vesting label and shows that profit sharing/nonelective contributions do not apply. If the relevant section is not on the current page section, return null.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing vesting option and table row in _raw_text.


## employer_profit_sharing_vesting_schedule

Field: employer_profit_sharing_vesting_schedule
Description: Year-by-year vesting percentages for profit sharing. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting requires a non-standard schedule breakdown.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Profit sharing vesting schedule, Year-by-year nonelective vesting percentages, Employer Profit Sharing Vesting Schedule
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON array string of objects, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Object keys: years_of_service, vesting_percentage.
- Only populate this field when employer_profit_sharing_vesting.value requires schedule detail, such as a custom or non-standard schedule with year-by-year percentages. Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Profit sharing vesting schedule; Year-by-year nonelective vesting percentages; Employer Profit Sharing Vesting Schedule.

## employer_profit_sharing_vesting_other_specify

Field: employer_profit_sharing_vesting_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str

Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when employer_profit_sharing_vesting.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer Profit Sharing Vesting Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## vesting_service

Field: vesting_service
Description: Vesting service as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule, Years of Vesting Service, 100%
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["All years of service", "Exclude service before company maintained plan", "Exclude service prior to age 18", "Exclude service before company maintained plan and service prior to age 18", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether employer match and/or profit sharing vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only then inspect service-exclusion rules if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedules and selected service-exclusion rows together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested.
- Do not use generic all-years-service text from Part F Exclusion of Service for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediate.
- The Part F sentence "All of an Employee's Years of Vesting Service..." is service-exclusion boilerplate. It does not override selected 100% immediate vesting schedules.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If service before the company maintained the plan is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If service prior to age 18 is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If no selected exclusion applies and a non-immediate vesting schedule applies, return value "All years of service".

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and service-exclusion text in _raw_text.


## vesting_period_calculation

Field: vesting_period_calculation
Description: Vesting computation period as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule, Measuring Period for Vesting, 100% immediate
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Plan year/1000 hours", "Plan Year / 1000 hours calculated with an equivalency method", "Employment year/Elapsed time", "Employment year/1000 hours calculated with monthly equivalency", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether all applicable employer contribution vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only inspect Measuring Period for Vesting and Service Required for Vesting if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedule options and selected measuring-period options together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, the measuring-period rules are not needed for these fields.
- Do not use elapsed-time, Plan Year, or hours rules from Measuring Period for Vesting / Service Required for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediately vested.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If a non-immediate schedule applies and the selected measuring period is Plan Year with 1000 hours, return value "Plan year/1000 hours".
- If an equivalency or elapsed-time method is selected for a non-immediate schedule, return the closest matching enum.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain vesting schedule or measuring-period evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and measuring-period option in _raw_text.

Special Instructions:

- Relevant labels describe whether forfeitures pay plan expenses, reduce employer contributions, or are allocated to participants.

Selection evidence:

Value mapping:

- If only reduction of employer contributions is selected, return value "Used to reduce employer contribution".
- If only allocation to eligible participants is selected, return value "Allocated to eligible participants".
- Return value "N/A: 100% vesting" only when the document provides no forfeiture-use election and the relevant contribution sources are fully vested.

Source evidence:

prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **safe_harbor_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Safe Harbor contributions.
- **safe_harbor_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_match_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Employer Matching Contributions.
- **employer_match_vesting_schedule** - Year-by-year vesting percentages. Only populated when employer_match_vesting is 'Other' or a non-standard graded schedule.
- **employer_match_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_match_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Employer Profit Sharing / Nonelective Contributions.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting_schedule** - Year-by-year vesting percentages for profit sharing. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting requires a non-standard schedule breakdown.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **vesting_service** - Vesting service as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **vesting_period_calculation** - Vesting computation period as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **forfeiture_use** - Forfeitures as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **normal_retirement_age** - Normal retirement age as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **normal_retirement_age_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when normal_retirement_age resolves to 'Other'.
- **normal_retirement_service_requirement** - Service requirement at normal retirement age, if any.
- **early_retirement_age** - Early retirement age as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **early_retirement_age_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when early_retirement_age resolves to 'Other'.
- **early_retirement_service_requirement** - Service requirement at early retirement age, if any.
- **field_100_pct_vesting_at_early_retirement** - 100% vesting at early retirement as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_definition** - Compensation definition as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_for_initial_year** - Compensation for initial year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_for_initial_year_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_for_initial_year resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_all** - Compensation components excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
- **compensation_excluded_all_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_all resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_deferrals** - Compensation components excluded for Elective Deferral purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_deferrals_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_deferrals resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_match** - Compensation components excluded for Employer Match purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_match_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_match resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_nec** - Compensation components excluded for Nonelective/Profit Sharing purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_nec_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_nec resolves to 'Other'.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f3_vesting_compensation` in workflow group `adp_f3_vesting_compensation`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f3_vesting_compensation Definition

Extract vesting compensation fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## safe_harbor_vesting

Field: safe_harbor_vesting
Description: Vesting schedule for Safe Harbor contributions.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor CODA Contributions, Application of Safe Harbor CODA, QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "100% immediate (QACA match)", "100% immediate (QACA NEC)", "2 Year Cliff for QACA match", "2 Year Cliff for QACA NEC", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether ADP Test Safe Harbor or QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are selected.
- Then find the vesting rule for the selected safe harbor contribution.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected safe harbor contribution options and selected vesting schedule options together.
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA / ADP Test Safe Harbor selects No, that selected No is enough evidence to return "N/A" for safe harbor vesting.
- A selected No in the Safe Harbor CODA applicability section is a real answer for this field, not missing evidence. Do not return null in that case.
- When the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question is visible, inspect the page image for the selected Yes or No row before looking at formula or vesting text.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- If the page image shows a selected mark on the No row in the Application of Safe Harbor CODA or ADP Test Safe Harbor applicability section, return "N/A" even if OCR does not include a checkbox symbol.
- When the standalone mark is between Yes and No rows, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means the safe-harbor vesting answer is "N/A".
- Do not require a separate vesting schedule when the controlling safe-harbor applicability section selects No. The selected No is the vesting answer for this field.
- Do not suppress the selected No just because this field is named as a vesting schedule. The applicability No is the source evidence that safe-harbor vesting is not applicable.
- If the document-level safe_harbor_type is "Not Applicable" or the source selected No for Application of Safe Harbor CODA, return "N/A" even when QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting schedule text appears elsewhere.
- In reconcile or QA, a candidate with formula or vesting-table text is lower priority than a visible selected No in the controlling safe-harbor applicability question.
- Inspect the page image for the selected No mark when OCR text drops checkbox symbols.
- If the current page/request has only QACA automatic-enrollment or formula text and does not show the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question, return null for this field.
- If a page contains only safe-harbor formula or vesting-table text and not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Ignore EACA automatic enrollment, QACA automatic-enrollment text, QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting tables, and ACP-test-only matching language when ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable.

Value mapping:
- If Safe Harbor CODA provisions or ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are explicitly not selected/applicable, return value "N/A".
- For this enum field, an explicit selected No for safe harbor applicability should be returned as value "N/A", not null.
- If selected safe harbor contributions vest immediately, return the matching immediate enum.
- If selected QACA safe harbor contributions use a two-year cliff, return the matching two-year cliff enum.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain safe harbor applicability or safe harbor vesting evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor applicability and vesting text in _raw_text.
- If ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable, do not populate QACA safe harbor vesting from automatic-enrollment or ACP-test-only matching language.

## vesting_service

Field: vesting_service
Description: Vesting service as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule, Years of Vesting Service, 100%
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["All years of service", "Exclude service before company maintained plan", "Exclude service prior to age 18", "Exclude service before company maintained plan and service prior to age 18", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether employer match and/or profit sharing vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only then inspect service-exclusion rules if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedules and selected service-exclusion rows together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested.
- Do not use generic all-years-service text from Part F Exclusion of Service for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediate.
- The Part F sentence "All of an Employee's Years of Vesting Service..." is service-exclusion boilerplate. It does not override selected 100% immediate vesting schedules.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If service before the company maintained the plan is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If service prior to age 18 is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If no selected exclusion applies and a non-immediate vesting schedule applies, return value "All years of service".

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and service-exclusion text in _raw_text.


## vesting_period_calculation

Field: vesting_period_calculation
Description: Vesting computation period as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule, Measuring Period for Vesting, 100% immediate
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Plan year/1000 hours", "Plan Year / 1000 hours calculated with an equivalency method", "Employment year/Elapsed time", "Employment year/1000 hours calculated with monthly equivalency", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether all applicable employer contribution vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only inspect Measuring Period for Vesting and Service Required for Vesting if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedule options and selected measuring-period options together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, the measuring-period rules are not needed for these fields.
- Do not use elapsed-time, Plan Year, or hours rules from Measuring Period for Vesting / Service Required for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediately vested.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If a non-immediate schedule applies and the selected measuring period is Plan Year with 1000 hours, return value "Plan year/1000 hours".
- If an equivalency or elapsed-time method is selected for a non-immediate schedule, return the closest matching enum.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain vesting schedule or measuring-period evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and measuring-period option in _raw_text.

prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **safe_harbor_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Safe Harbor contributions.
- **safe_harbor_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_match_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Employer Matching Contributions.
- **employer_match_vesting_schedule** - Year-by-year vesting percentages. Only populated when employer_match_vesting is 'Other' or a non-standard graded schedule.
- **employer_match_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_match_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting** - Vesting schedule for Employer Profit Sharing / Nonelective Contributions.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting_schedule** - Year-by-year vesting percentages for profit sharing. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting requires a non-standard schedule breakdown.
- **employer_profit_sharing_vesting_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
- **vesting_service** - Vesting service as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **vesting_period_calculation** - Vesting computation period as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **forfeiture_use** - Forfeitures as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **normal_retirement_age** - Normal retirement age as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **normal_retirement_age_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when normal_retirement_age resolves to 'Other'.
- **normal_retirement_service_requirement** - Service requirement at normal retirement age, if any.
- **early_retirement_age** - Early retirement age as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **early_retirement_age_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when early_retirement_age resolves to 'Other'.
- **early_retirement_service_requirement** - Service requirement at early retirement age, if any.
- **field_100_pct_vesting_at_early_retirement** - 100% vesting at early retirement as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_definition** - Compensation definition as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_for_initial_year** - Compensation for initial year as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **compensation_for_initial_year_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_for_initial_year resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_all** - Compensation components excluded from ALL contribution types. Select all that apply.
- **compensation_excluded_all_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_all resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_deferrals** - Compensation components excluded for Elective Deferral purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_deferrals_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_deferrals resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_match** - Compensation components excluded for Employer Match purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_match_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_match resolves to 'Other'.
- **compensation_excluded_nec** - Compensation components excluded for Nonelective/Profit Sharing purposes only.
- **compensation_excluded_nec_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when compensation_excluded_nec resolves to 'Other'.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f3_vesting_compensation` in workflow group `adp_f3_vesting_compensation`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f3_vesting_compensation Definition

Extract vesting compensation fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## safe_harbor_vesting

Field: safe_harbor_vesting
Description: Vesting schedule for Safe Harbor contributions.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor CODA Contributions, Application of Safe Harbor CODA, QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contributions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "100% immediate (QACA match)", "100% immediate (QACA NEC)", "2 Year Cliff for QACA match", "2 Year Cliff for QACA NEC", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether ADP Test Safe Harbor or QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are selected.
- Then find the vesting rule for the selected safe harbor contribution.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected safe harbor contribution options and selected vesting schedule options together.
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA / ADP Test Safe Harbor selects No, that selected No is enough evidence to return "N/A" for safe harbor vesting.
- A selected No in the Safe Harbor CODA applicability section is a real answer for this field, not missing evidence. Do not return null in that case.
- When the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question is visible, inspect the page image for the selected Yes or No row before looking at formula or vesting text.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- If the page image shows a selected mark on the No row in the Application of Safe Harbor CODA or ADP Test Safe Harbor applicability section, return "N/A" even if OCR does not include a checkbox symbol.
- When the standalone mark is between Yes and No rows, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means the safe-harbor vesting answer is "N/A".
- Do not require a separate vesting schedule when the controlling safe-harbor applicability section selects No. The selected No is the vesting answer for this field.
- Do not suppress the selected No just because this field is named as a vesting schedule. The applicability No is the source evidence that safe-harbor vesting is not applicable.
- If the document-level safe_harbor_type is "Not Applicable" or the source selected No for Application of Safe Harbor CODA, return "N/A" even when QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting schedule text appears elsewhere.
- In reconcile or QA, a candidate with formula or vesting-table text is lower priority than a visible selected No in the controlling safe-harbor applicability question.
- Inspect the page image for the selected No mark when OCR text drops checkbox symbols.
- If the current page/request has only QACA automatic-enrollment or formula text and does not show the Application of Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No question, return null for this field.
- If a page contains only safe-harbor formula or vesting-table text and not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Ignore EACA automatic enrollment, QACA automatic-enrollment text, QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor vesting tables, and ACP-test-only matching language when ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable.

Value mapping:
- If Safe Harbor CODA provisions or ADP Test Safe Harbor contributions are explicitly not selected/applicable, return value "N/A".
- For this enum field, an explicit selected No for safe harbor applicability should be returned as value "N/A", not null.
- If selected safe harbor contributions vest immediately, return the matching immediate enum.
- If selected QACA safe harbor contributions use a two-year cliff, return the matching two-year cliff enum.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain safe harbor applicability or safe harbor vesting evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor applicability and vesting text in _raw_text.
- If ADP Test Safe Harbor is not applicable, do not populate QACA safe harbor vesting from automatic-enrollment or ACP-test-only matching language.

## safe_harbor_vesting_other_specify

Field: safe_harbor_vesting_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor Vesting Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when safe_harbor_vesting.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe Harbor Vesting Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## employer_match_vesting

Field: employer_match_vesting
Description: Vesting schedule for Employer Matching Contributions.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer match vesting, Matching contribution vesting schedule
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "3 Year Cliff", "6 year graded", "5 year graded", "4 year graded", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- If employer matching contributions are marked as 100% vested or immediate vesting, return value "100% immediate". Do not extract a graded schedule unless that schedule is the selected vesting election for employer matching contributions.
- Do not return "N/A" for employer match vesting when discretionary matching contributions are authorized and their selected vesting election is immediate / 100% vested.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer match vesting; Matching contribution vesting schedule.

## employer_match_vesting_schedule

Field: employer_match_vesting_schedule
Description: Year-by-year vesting percentages. Only populated when employer_match_vesting is 'Other' or a non-standard graded schedule.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Match vesting schedule, Year-by-year match vesting percentages, Employer Match Vesting Schedule
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON array string of objects, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Object keys: years_of_service, vesting_percentage.
- Only populate this field when employer_match_vesting.value requires schedule detail, such as a custom or non-standard schedule with year-by-year percentages. Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Match vesting schedule; Year-by-year match vesting percentages; Employer Match Vesting Schedule.

## employer_match_vesting_other_specify

Field: employer_match_vesting_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when employer_match_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer Match Vesting Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when employer_match_vesting.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer Match Vesting Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## employer_profit_sharing_vesting

Field: employer_profit_sharing_vesting
Description: Vesting schedule for Employer Profit Sharing / Nonelective Contributions.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule for Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, Profit Sharing, 100%
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A", "100% immediate", "3 Year Cliff", "6 year graded", "5 year graded", "4 year graded", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the vesting schedule for employer profit sharing contributions.
- Use the row or column that governs profit sharing or nonelective contributions.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected vesting option for profit sharing contributions.
- Read the selected option across the years-of-vesting-service table.
- Do not use an unselected graded schedule when the selected profit sharing option is 100% immediate.

Value mapping:
- If the selected profit sharing schedule is 100% for less than one year and every later year, return value "100% immediate".
- If a cliff or graded schedule is selected, return the matching enum.
- Return value "N/A" only when the current page section contains the profit sharing/nonelective contribution or vesting label and shows that profit sharing/nonelective contributions do not apply. If the relevant section is not on the current page section, return null.
- If selected wording is different, return value "Other" and preserve it in _raw_text.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing vesting option and table row in _raw_text.


## employer_profit_sharing_vesting_schedule

Field: employer_profit_sharing_vesting_schedule
Description: Year-by-year vesting percentages for profit sharing. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting requires a non-standard schedule breakdown.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Profit sharing vesting schedule, Year-by-year nonelective vesting percentages, Employer Profit Sharing Vesting Schedule
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON array string of objects, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Do not return an actual JSON array. Encode the JSON array as a string value.
- Object keys: years_of_service, vesting_percentage.
- Only populate this field when employer_profit_sharing_vesting.value requires schedule detail, such as a custom or non-standard schedule with year-by-year percentages. Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Profit sharing vesting schedule; Year-by-year nonelective vesting percentages; Employer Profit Sharing Vesting Schedule.

## employer_profit_sharing_vesting_other_specify

Field: employer_profit_sharing_vesting_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_vesting resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer Profit Sharing Vesting Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when employer_profit_sharing_vesting.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer Profit Sharing Vesting Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## vesting_service

Field: vesting_service
Description: Vesting service as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule, Years of Vesting Service, 100%
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["All years of service", "Exclude service before company maintained plan", "Exclude service prior to age 18", "Exclude service before company maintained plan and service prior to age 18", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether employer match and/or profit sharing vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only then inspect service-exclusion rules if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedules and selected service-exclusion rows together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested.
- Do not use generic all-years-service text from Part F Exclusion of Service for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediate.
- The Part F sentence "All of an Employee's Years of Vesting Service..." is service-exclusion boilerplate. It does not override selected 100% immediate vesting schedules.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If service before the company maintained the plan is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If service prior to age 18 is excluded, return the matching exclusion enum.
- If no selected exclusion applies and a non-immediate vesting schedule applies, return value "All years of service".

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and service-exclusion text in _raw_text.


## vesting_period_calculation

Field: vesting_period_calculation
Description: Vesting computation period as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Vesting Schedule, Measuring Period for Vesting, 100% immediate
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Plan year/1000 hours", "Plan Year / 1000 hours calculated with an equivalency method", "Employment year/Elapsed time", "Employment year/1000 hours calculated with monthly equivalency", "N/A: 100% vesting"].

Label discovery:
- First determine whether all applicable employer contribution vesting schedules are 100% immediate.
- Only inspect Measuring Period for Vesting and Service Required for Vesting if a non-immediate vesting schedule applies.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected vesting schedule options and selected measuring-period options together.
- A selected matching or profit-sharing vesting schedule that shows 100% vested before one year is an immediate vesting schedule. If all applicable selected employer-contribution schedules are immediate, the measuring-period rules are not needed for these fields.
- Do not use elapsed-time, Plan Year, or hours rules from Measuring Period for Vesting / Service Required for Vesting when all applicable employer contribution schedules are 100% immediately vested.

Value mapping:
- If all applicable employer contributions are 100% immediately vested, return value "N/A: 100% vesting".
- If a non-immediate schedule applies and the selected measuring period is Plan Year with 1000 hours, return value "Plan year/1000 hours".
- If an equivalency or elapsed-time method is selected for a non-immediate schedule, return the closest matching enum.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain vesting schedule or measuring-period evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected vesting schedule and measuring-period option in _raw_text.

## forfeiture_use

Field: forfeiture_use
Description: Forfeitures as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Allocation of Forfeitures, Use of forfeitures, Other
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["N/A: 100% vesting", "Used to reduce employer contribution", "Allocated to eligible participants", "Not Indicated", "Used to reduce employer contribution OR Allocated to eligible Participants"].

Label discovery:
- Find the allocation or use of forfeitures section.
- Relevant labels describe whether forfeitures pay plan expenses, reduce employer contributions, or are allocated to participants.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected option in the forfeitures section.
- Do not assume N/A solely from immediate vesting if the document still has a selected forfeiture-use election.

Value mapping:
- If selected wording allows forfeitures to reduce employer contributions and/or be allocated as profit sharing to participants, return value "Used to reduce employer contribution OR Allocated to eligible Participants".
- If only reduction of employer contributions is selected, return value "Used to reduce employer contribution".
- If only allocation to eligible participants is selected, return value "Allocated to eligible participants".
- Return value "N/A: 100% vesting" only when the document provides no forfeiture-use election and the relevant contribution sources are fully vested.

Source evidence:
- Put the exact selected forfeiture-use wording in _raw_text.


## normal_retirement_age
