## employer_profit_sharing_timing

Field: employer_profit_sharing_timing
Description: Profit sharing contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer Profit Sharing Contribution, for each Plan Year, timing of contribution
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find employer profit sharing contribution authorization, formula, and any explicit timing language.
- Do not use matching contribution timing or matching computation period for this field.
- Do not treat formula wording like "For each Plan Year the Employer may contribute..." as a timing election by itself.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected wording about Employer Profit Sharing Contributions or nonelective/profit sharing contributions.

Value mapping:
- If selected profit sharing timing wording explicitly says annually, every payroll/pay period, or quarterly, return the matching enum.
- If profit sharing is present but the source does not state timing, return value "Not Indicated".
- Do not return null when the Employer Profit Sharing Contributions section is present and selected, but no explicit timing election is present. That is "Not Indicated".
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain profit sharing contribution formula or timing evidence.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing timing or formula-period wording in _raw_text.

## employer_profit_sharing_allocation_conditions

Field: employer_profit_sharing_allocation_conditions
Description: Conditions employees must meet to receive profit sharing allocation.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Additional Conditions for Receiving Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, Last Day Requirement, Service Requirement
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["None - All eligible participants receive NEC", "Must complete 500 hours of service", "Must complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 1000 hours of service"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Additional Conditions for Receiving Employer Profit Sharing Contributions section.
- Relevant rows include Last Day Requirement, Service Requirement, and No additional conditions.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected conditions under Employer Profit Sharing Contributions.
- Treat a selected additional-conditions framework plus a selected Last Day Requirement row as an actual last-day condition.
- A standalone selected mark immediately before the additional-conditions framework selects that framework; it does not select the Service Requirement subrow by itself.
- Do not return no additional conditions merely because the No Additional Conditions row is unselected or absent.
- Read selected last-day and hours rows together, including whether the condition says AND or OR.
- Do not treat the Service Requirement row as selected unless its own checkbox/mark is selected or the hours/months blank is filled in.
- Parenthetical text such as "(not more than 1,000) Hours of Service" or "(not more than 12) months" is a limit on the blank, not a filled-in service requirement value.
- Blank underscores are not values. Do not convert a blank line plus "(not more than 1,000)" into "complete 1000 hours of service."
- Blank hours text or the default note about zero hours is not selected evidence.
- A selected additional-conditions framework means some additional condition applies; it does not by itself select every subcondition printed underneath.
- If the only selected subcondition under that framework is Last Day Requirement, return only the last-day enum.
- Ignore explanatory waiver rows and allocation formula rows; they do not define the base allocation condition.

Value mapping:
- If only Last Day Requirement is selected, return value "Must be employed on last day of plan year".
- If last-day and hours requirements are both selected, choose the matching AND/OR enum based on the source wording.
- If only an hours requirement is selected, return the matching hours enum.
- If the selected row says No additional conditions, return value "None - All eligible participants receive NEC".
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain this profit sharing allocation-condition section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing allocation condition rows in _raw_text.

## employer_profit_sharing_eligible_employees

Field: employer_profit_sharing_eligible_employees
Description: Profit sharing eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Participants Eligible for Employer Profit Sharing Contributions, Qualifying Participant, All Participants
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement", "Allocated to NHCEs only"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Employer Profit Sharing Contributions allocation/eligible participants section, not Qualified Nonelective Contributions or matching contribution sections.
- Do not use the Contributions to Non-Highly Compensated Disabled Participants section as the eligible-employee rule for normal employer profit sharing contributions.
- Do not use Qualified Nonelective Contributions, Qualified Matching Contributions, QNEC, QMAC, or top-heavy minimum sections to answer normal employer profit sharing eligible employees.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the option describing who receives employer profit sharing contributions.
- If the section says Employer Profit Sharing Contributions are made to Qualifying Participants and the selected Additional Conditions only adds a last-day or service condition, this is still the all-eligible enum for this field.
- Do not treat a selected Non-Highly Compensated Disabled Participants option as an NHCE-only profit-sharing eligibility rule.
- Do not treat a selected Non-Highly Compensated row from a Qualified Nonelective Contributions section as an NHCE-only profit-sharing eligibility rule.
- If no selected normal profit-sharing row limits recipients to NHCEs, do not choose the NHCE-only enum from nearby QNEC/QMAC sections.

Value mapping:
- If selected wording applies to all qualifying/eligible participants who satisfy the age/service requirements, return value "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement".
- If the only extra condition is that Qualifying Participants must satisfy a selected last-day condition, return value "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement"; the separate allocation-conditions field carries the last-day condition.
- If selected wording limits allocations to non-Highly Compensated Employees, return value "Allocated to NHCEs only".
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the employer profit sharing eligible-employee section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected profit sharing eligible-employee option text in _raw_text.

# Output Contract

- Custom workflow step: `adp_f5_employer_contributions`
- Workflow group: `adp_f5_employer_contributions`
- Step kind: `instruct`
- Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
- Use only these output keys:
- `safe_harbor_type`
- `safe_harbor_nonelective_pct`
- `safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution`
- `safe_harbor_annual_true_up`
- `safe_harbor_eligible_employees`
- `safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify`
- `employer_match_type`
- `employer_match_formula_1`
- `employer_match_formula_2`
- `employer_match_timing`
- `employer_match_true_up`
- `employer_match_allocation_conditions`
- `employer_match_eligible_employees`
- `employer_match_eligible_employees_other_specify`
- `employer_profit_sharing_type`
- `employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method`
- `employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method_other_specify`
- `employer_profit_sharing_timing`
- `employer_profit_sharing_allocation_conditions`
- `employer_profit_sharing_eligible_employees`

# Final Notes

- Use the value in `Field` as the key in your JSON response for the given field.
- If you cannot identify a field with confidence, exclude it unless that field's instructions explicitly require `null`.
- If you cannot find any fields with confidence, return an empty JSON object like this: `{}`.
- Do not add commentary, markdown fences, or explanation text.
- Only return the JSON object in your response.
prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **safe_harbor_type** - Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
- **safe_harbor_nonelective_pct** - Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
- **safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution** - Safe harbor contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_annual_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of safe harbor contributions?
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees** - Safe harbor eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_match_type** - Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
- **employer_match_formula_1** - First tier of employer match formula. Free text as stated in plan document.
- **employer_match_formula_2** - Second tier of employer match formula, if applicable.
- **employer_match_timing** - Employer match timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of employer matching contributions?
- **employer_match_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive the employer match.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees** - Match eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_match_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_type** - Profit sharing contribution as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method** - Profit sharing allocation method as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_timing** - Profit sharing contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive profit sharing allocation.
- **employer_profit_sharing_eligible_employees** - Profit sharing eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f5_employer_contributions` in workflow group `adp_f5_employer_contributions`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f5_employer_contributions Definition

Extract employer contributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## safe_harbor_type

Field: safe_harbor_type
Description: Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor CODA Contributions, ADP Test Safe Harbor, Safe Harbor Provisions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Applicable", "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 3% AND 50% ON NEXT 2% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 4% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 6% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "POSSIBLE SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "QACA SAFE HARBOR TIERED MATCH OF 100% ON THE FIRST 1% AND 50% ON THE NEXT 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% QACA SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Safe Harbor CODA Contributions / ADP Test Safe Harbor section as the controlling source.
- Do not use QACA automatic-enrollment, ACP safe harbor, or printed formula tables by themselves.
- A QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contribution section inside automatic-enrollment provisions is not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA applicability question.

Selection evidence:
- First inspect the page image for the selected Yes/No row in Application of Safe Harbor CODA.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- A selected No row is a real answer and controls the field; return Not Applicable, not null or Not Indicated.
- Formula choices printed below a selected No are inactive boilerplate.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means this field is "Not Applicable".
- If a page only contains QACA, ACP, or safe-harbor formula text and does not show the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Do not return a QACA safe harbor formula unless the same source path also shows that the controlling safe-harbor applicability question selected Yes.
- If Yes is selected, then use the selected safe harbor contribution formula in the same section.
- In reconcile or QA, a source-backed No from Application of Safe Harbor CODA beats formula text that does not also prove Safe Harbor CODA was selected Yes.

Value mapping:
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA selects No, return value "Not Applicable".
- If a safe harbor matching formula or nonelective formula is selected, return the closest matching enum value.
- If the document indicates safe harbor applies but does not provide details, return value "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED".
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the safe harbor label but no selected option is visible. If the safe harbor section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor option and contribution formula text in _raw_text.
- Do not treat automatic enrollment as a safe harbor contribution.
- Do not use EACA automatic enrollment or ACP test safe harbor matching language as the answer when ADP test safe harbor is not selected.


## safe_harbor_nonelective_pct

Field: safe_harbor_nonelective_pct
Description: Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
Type: float
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor nonelective percentage, Nonelective contribution percentage
Special Instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor nonelective percentage; Nonelective contribution percentage.

## safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution

Field: safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution
Description: Safe harbor contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor contribution timing, Timing of safe harbor contribution, Safe Harbor Timing Of Contribution
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated", "Not Applicable"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor contribution timing; Timing of safe harbor contribution; Safe Harbor Timing Of Contribution.

## safe_harbor_annual_true_up

Field: safe_harbor_annual_true_up
Description: Is there an annual true-up of safe harbor contributions?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor true-up, Annual true-up, Safe Harbor Annual True Up
Special Instructions:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor true-up; Annual true-up; Safe Harbor Annual True Up.

## safe_harbor_eligible_employees

Field: safe_harbor_eligible_employees
Description: Safe harbor eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor eligible employees, Employees receiving safe harbor contribution
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement", "Allocated to the NHCEs only", "Other"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor eligible employees; Employees receiving safe harbor contribution.

## safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify

Field: safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify
Description: Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor Eligible Employees Other Specify, Other - specify, Other specify
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Only populate this field when safe_harbor_eligible_employees.value equals "Other". Otherwise return null.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe Harbor Eligible Employees Other Specify; Other - specify; Other specify.

## employer_match_type

Field: employer_match_type
Description: Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Matching Contribution Formula, Discretionary Match, Fixed Match
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["No Match", "Fixed Match", "Discretionary Match", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the matching contribution formula section.
- This field is for employer matching contributions, separate from safe harbor contributions and profit sharing contributions.

Selection evidence:
- Use the selected option in the matching contribution formula section.
- Ignore eligibility entry dates, safe harbor contribution formulas, and profit sharing formulas.

Selection from OCR text (page image may be unavailable):
- A selected option is shown by a filled/checked box or by its label appearing without the preceding empty-box glyph.
- A discretionary match is elected by selecting the discretionary-matching option; it has NO numeric formula filled in. Do not return "No Match" merely because no fixed percentage/formula numbers appear.

Value mapping:
- If the document establishes a discretionary matching contribution (e.g., the Employer will determine a discretionary Matching Contribution formula), return value "Discretionary Match".
- If the selected formula gives a fixed match formula (specific percentages/tiers), return value "Fixed Match".
- Return value "No Match" only when matching contributions are explicitly not made / the no-match option is selected.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the matching contribution formula label but no selected formula or explicit no-match election is visible. If the matching contribution formula is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected matching contribution formula option in _raw_text.


## employer_match_formula_1

Field: employer_match_formula_1
Description: First tier of employer match formula. Free text as stated in plan document.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Employer match formula, Matching contribution formula, First tier match formula, Employer Match Formula 1
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Employer match formula; Matching contribution formula; First tier match formula; Employer Match Formula 1.

## employer_match_formula_2

Field: employer_match_formula_2
Description: Second tier of employer match formula, if applicable.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Second tier match formula, Additional matching contribution formula, Employer Match Formula 2
Special Instructions:
- Return the value supported by the document text, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Second tier match formula; Additional matching contribution formula; Employer Match Formula 2.

## employer_match_timing

Field: employer_match_timing
Description: Employer match timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Matching Contribution Computation Period, Computation Period, Contribution Computation Period
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Matching Contribution Computation Period section as the controlling source.
- Do not use compensation inclusion, contribution formula, or payroll-period language outside that section as timing evidence.

Selection evidence:
- Inspect the page image and pair the selected checkbox/mark with the row inside the Matching Contribution Computation Period option group.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected period option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- When the standalone mark appears between two computation-period labels, attach it to the following row if the page image alignment shows it belongs to that row.
- Ignore blank squares and unselected rows, even when they appear first or contain "payroll period".
- Ignore marks in compensation tables above this section; those table cells are not timing elections.
- Use a default only when every row in the Matching Contribution Computation Period option group is visibly unselected. Do not choose payroll period from the first row when a later computation-period row has a visible selected mark.

Value mapping:
- If the selected row says the employer payroll period, return value "Every Pay Period".
- If the selected row says annually or annual, return value "Annually".
- If monthly, quarterly, or semi-annual timing is selected, return the closest enum; semi-annual should be "Other" only if the enum cannot express it.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the Matching Contribution Computation Period section is visible but no option/default can be determined.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the Matching Contribution Computation Period section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected computation-period option in _raw_text.

## employer_match_true_up

Field: employer_match_true_up
Description: Is there an annual true-up of employer matching contributions?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Matching Contribution Computation Period, re-calculate the Matching Contribution, true-up
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent/not applicable.

Label discovery:
- Find the Matching Contribution Computation Period and any matching-contribution recalculation or true-up language.
- This field asks about an annual true-up for employer matching contributions, not the general timing label alone.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected matching contribution computation-period options together with selected matching contribution formula timing.
- Do not infer true-up from boilerplate notes that start with "If Matching Contributions are made more frequently..." unless the document also selects or states that matching contributions are actually made more frequently than the computation period.
- If the computation period is Annually and there is no separate selected option saying contributions are made more frequently than annually, do not emit a true-up value from the conditional note alone.

Value mapping:
- Return "true" when the selected matching contribution terms require annual or computation-period recalculation after more frequent contributions.
- Return "false" only when the source directly says no true-up or no recalculation.
- Return null when the source only contains conditional recalculation boilerplate, when matching contributions are not applicable, or when the current page/request lacks true-up/recalculation evidence.

Source evidence:
- Preserve the computation-period and recalculation wording used for the decision.

## employer_match_allocation_conditions

Field: employer_match_allocation_conditions
Description: Conditions employees must meet to receive the employer match.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions, Last Day Requirement, Hours of Service Requirement
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["None - All eligible participants receive Match", "Must complete 500 hours of service", "Must complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year AND complete 1000 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 500 hours of service", "Must be employed on last day of plan year OR complete 1000 hours of service"].

Label discovery:
- Find the additional conditions for receiving matching contributions section.
- Relevant rows include last day requirement, hours of service requirement, and all-eligible/no additional condition options.

Selection evidence:
- Use only selected conditions under matching contributions.
- Treat a selected additional-conditions framework plus a selected Last Day Requirement row as an actual last-day condition.
- Do not return no additional conditions merely because the No Additional Conditions row is unselected or absent.
- Read selected last-day and hours rows together, including whether the condition says AND or OR.
- Do not treat the Service Requirement row as selected unless its own checkbox/mark is selected.
- Parenthetical text such as "(not more than 1,000) Hours of Service" or "(not more than 12) months" is a limit on the blank, not a filled-in service requirement value.
- Blank underscores are not values. Do not convert a blank line plus a maximum-hours parenthetical into a service-hours requirement.
- A selected additional-conditions framework means some additional condition applies; it does not by itself select every subcondition printed underneath.
- Ignore matching formula text that explains allocation amount rather than eligibility conditions.

Value mapping:
- If only Last Day Requirement is selected, return value "Must be employed on last day of plan year".
- If last-day and hours requirements are both selected, choose the matching AND/OR enum based on the source wording.
- If only an hours requirement is selected, return the matching hours enum.
- If no additional matching allocation condition is selected, return value "None - All eligible participants receive Match".

Source evidence:
- Put the selected matching allocation condition rows in _raw_text.


## employer_match_eligible_employees

Field: employer_match_eligible_employees
Description: Match eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.

- Enum choices JSON: ["Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement", "Allocated to NHCEs only", "Other"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Participants or Contributing Participants Eligible for Matching Contributions section for regular employer matching contributions.
- Also use the regular Matching Contribution Formula and Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions when they define who receives the regular match.
- Do not use Qualified Matching Contributions or ACP/QACA safe harbor matching sections for this regular employer-match field.

Selection evidence:
- Pair the selected checkbox/mark with the option that says Non-Highly Compensated Employee Participants or All Contributing Participants.
- If there is no separate selected regular-match participant-class row, use selected regular-match conditions that define which contributing participants receive the match.
- If the regular match applies to Qualifying Contributing Participants subject to a selected Last Day Requirement, use that regular-match evidence instead of later Qualified Matching Contribution eligibility rows.
- If Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions selects an additional-condition framework and the Last Day Requirement row is selected, the answer is not the plain all-eligible enum. Return "Other" because the selected rule adds a last-day condition.
- If the same page shows "Additional Conditions for Receiving Matching Contributions" with a selected Last Day Requirement, return "Other" even when another regular-match field extracted "Must be employed on last day of plan year".
- Do not choose "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement" when any selected additional condition changes who receives the regular match.

- Ignore Qualified Matching Contributions / Qualified Nonelective Contributions eligibility rows when the field asks about regular employer matching contributions.

Value mapping:
- If the selected option limits matching contributions to non-Highly Compensated Employees, return value "Allocated to NHCEs only".
- If the selected option applies to all contributing/eligible participants, return value "Allocated to all eligible employees meeting age/service requirement".
- Return "Other" when the selected regular matching eligible-employee wording is not covered by the enum, including when eligibility depends on being a Qualifying Contributing Participant and satisfying a selected Last Day Requirement.

Label discovery:
- Find the Employer Profit Sharing Contributions allocation/eligible participants section, not Qualified Nonelective Contributions or matching contribution sections.
- Do not use the Contributions to Non-Highly Compensated Disabled Participants section as the eligible-employee rule for normal employer profit sharing contributions.

- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the employer profit sharing eligible-employee section.

Source evidence:

# Output Contract

- Custom workflow step: `adp_f5_employer_contributions`
- Workflow group: `adp_f5_employer_contributions`

# Final Notes

- Use the value in `Field` as the key in your JSON response for the given field.
- If you cannot identify a field with confidence, exclude it unless that field's instructions explicitly require `null`.

prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **safe_harbor_type** - Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
- **safe_harbor_nonelective_pct** - Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
- **safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution** - Safe harbor contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_annual_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of safe harbor contributions?
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees** - Safe harbor eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_match_type** - Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
- **employer_match_formula_1** - First tier of employer match formula. Free text as stated in plan document.
- **employer_match_formula_2** - Second tier of employer match formula, if applicable.
- **employer_match_timing** - Employer match timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of employer matching contributions?
- **employer_match_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive the employer match.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees** - Match eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_match_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_type** - Profit sharing contribution as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method** - Profit sharing allocation method as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_timing** - Profit sharing contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive profit sharing allocation.
- **employer_profit_sharing_eligible_employees** - Profit sharing eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f5_employer_contributions` in workflow group `adp_f5_employer_contributions`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f5_employer_contributions Definition

Extract employer contributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## safe_harbor_type

Field: safe_harbor_type
Description: Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe Harbor CODA Contributions, ADP Test Safe Harbor, Safe Harbor Provisions
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Not Applicable", "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 3% AND 50% ON NEXT 2% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 4% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "SAFE HARBOR MATCH OF 100% ON 1ST 6% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "POSSIBLE SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "QACA SAFE HARBOR TIERED MATCH OF 100% ON THE FIRST 1% AND 50% ON THE NEXT 5% OF COMPENSATION DEFERRED", "3% QACA SAFE HARBOR NON-ELECTIVE CONTRIBUTION", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Use the Safe Harbor CODA Contributions / ADP Test Safe Harbor section as the controlling source.
- Do not use QACA automatic-enrollment, ACP safe harbor, or printed formula tables by themselves.
- A QACA ADP Test Safe Harbor Contribution section inside automatic-enrollment provisions is not the controlling Safe Harbor CODA applicability question.

Selection evidence:
- First inspect the page image for the selected Yes/No row in Application of Safe Harbor CODA.
- OCR may render the selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected Yes or No option label; use the page image alignment to pair that mark with the following row.
- A selected No row is a real answer and controls the field; return Not Applicable, not null or Not Indicated.
- Formula choices printed below a selected No are inactive boilerplate.
- Default or formula text applies only if the applicability question selected Yes or has no visible selected mark. A visible selected No means this field is "Not Applicable".
- If a page only contains QACA, ACP, or safe-harbor formula text and does not show the controlling Safe Harbor CODA Yes/No election, return null for that page.
- Do not return a QACA safe harbor formula unless the same source path also shows that the controlling safe-harbor applicability question selected Yes.
- If Yes is selected, then use the selected safe harbor contribution formula in the same section.
- In reconcile or QA, a source-backed No from Application of Safe Harbor CODA beats formula text that does not also prove Safe Harbor CODA was selected Yes.

Value mapping:
- If Application of Safe Harbor CODA selects No, return value "Not Applicable".
- If a safe harbor matching formula or nonelective formula is selected, return the closest matching enum value.
- If the document indicates safe harbor applies but does not provide details, return value "INDICATES THAT YOU MAY HAVE A SAFE HARBOR PROVISION BUT DETAILS ARE NOT PROVIDED".
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the safe harbor label but no selected option is visible. If the safe harbor section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected safe harbor option and contribution formula text in _raw_text.
- Do not treat automatic enrollment as a safe harbor contribution.
- Do not use EACA automatic enrollment or ACP test safe harbor matching language as the answer when ADP test safe harbor is not selected.


## safe_harbor_nonelective_pct

Field: safe_harbor_nonelective_pct
Description: Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
Type: float
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor nonelective percentage, Nonelective contribution percentage
Special Instructions:
- Return the numeric value, or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor nonelective percentage; Nonelective contribution percentage.

## safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution

Field: safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution
Description: Safe harbor contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor contribution timing, Timing of safe harbor contribution, Safe Harbor Timing Of Contribution
Special Instructions:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Every Pay Period", "Quarterly", "Annually", "Not Indicated", "Not Applicable"].
- If the source text does not exactly match an enum value and "Other" is available, set value to "Other" and put the written-in source value in _raw_text.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.
- Use source labels such as: Safe harbor contribution timing; Timing of safe harbor contribution; Safe Harbor Timing Of Contribution.

## safe_harbor_annual_true_up

Field: safe_harbor_annual_true_up
Description: Is there an annual true-up of safe harbor contributions?
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Safe harbor true-up, Annual true-up, Safe Harbor Annual True Up
Special Instructions:
- Return exactly "true" or "false", or null when absent.
- Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text.

# Output Contract

- Custom workflow step: `adp_f5_employer_contributions`

# Final Notes

- Use the value in `Field` as the key in your JSON response for the given field.
- If you cannot identify a field with confidence, exclude it unless that field's instructions explicitly require `null`.

- Do not add commentary, markdown fences, or explanation text.
- Only return the JSON object in your response.
prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **safe_harbor_type** - Type of ADP Test Safe Harbor contribution, if any. This is the primary discriminator for the safe harbor sub-fields below.
- **safe_harbor_nonelective_pct** - Fixed nonelective contribution percentage. Only populated for Nonelective SH types. Expressed as decimal. Minimum 0.03.
- **safe_harbor_timing_of_contribution** - Safe harbor contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_annual_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of safe harbor contributions?
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees** - Safe harbor eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **safe_harbor_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when safe_harbor_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_match_type** - Type of discretionary or fixed employer matching contribution (separate from Safe Harbor).
- **employer_match_formula_1** - First tier of employer match formula. Free text as stated in plan document.
- **employer_match_formula_2** - Second tier of employer match formula, if applicable.
- **employer_match_timing** - Employer match timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_true_up** - Is there an annual true-up of employer matching contributions?
- **employer_match_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive the employer match.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees** - Match eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_match_eligible_employees_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_match_eligible_employees resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_type** - Profit sharing contribution as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method** - Profit sharing allocation method as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when employer_profit_sharing_allocation_method resolves to 'Other'.
- **employer_profit_sharing_timing** - Profit sharing contribution timing as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **employer_profit_sharing_allocation_conditions** - Conditions employees must meet to receive profit sharing allocation.
- **employer_profit_sharing_eligible_employees** - Profit sharing eligible employees as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f5_employer_contributions` in workflow group `adp_f5_employer_contributions`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

prompt.request:
# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f6_withdrawals_and_distributions Definition

Extract withdrawals and distributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## payment_options

Field: payment_options
Description: Payment options as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Voluntary Distributions, Lump Sum, Installment Payments, Annuity Contracts
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Lump Sum and Installments in cash", "Lump Sum, Installments and partial in cash", "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Voluntary Distributions / Form of Distribution section.
- Use the distribution form / payment options section, not contribution-source or withdrawal-availability sections.

Selection evidence:
- Pair each selected Yes/No checkbox with its row: Lump Sum, Partial Payments, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts.
- Ignore row labels whose Yes option is unselected or whose No option is selected.

Value mapping:
- If Lump Sum and Installment Payments are selected Yes, and Partial Payments and Annuity Contracts are not selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum and Installments in cash".
- If Lump Sum, Partial Payments, and Installment Payments are selected Yes, and Annuity Contracts are not selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and partial in cash".
- If Lump Sum, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts are selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity".
- If Lump Sum, Partial Payments, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts are all selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity" and preserve Partial Payments in _raw_text because the enum has no exact value for all four selected options.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the distribution form section is visible but selected forms cannot be determined.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the distribution form section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected distribution form rows in _raw_text.

## in_service_withdrawals

Field: in_service_withdrawals
Description: In-service withdrawals as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.

Selection evidence:
- Use selected Yes/No options and selected money-source rows inside the in-service availability section.
- Ignore severance-from-employment, hardship, rollover, loan, or voluntary distribution sections for this field.

Value mapping:
- Return value "Permitted" when any age, vesting, or money-source row under in-service withdrawals is selected.
- Return value "Not Permitted" only when the current page section contains the in-service withdrawal label and explicitly says in-service withdrawals are not allowed for all relevant sources.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the in-service withdrawal label but no selected option is visible. If the in-service withdrawal section is not on the current page section, return null.

Source evidence:

prompt.task:
# Identity

You are a structured-data extraction assistant that extracts information from document content and returns the information in JSON format.

# Process

Your process for extracting structured information from document content is as follows:

1. You are provided with document content as text, page images, or a combination of text and page images.
  - If you are provided with text, you are provided with extracted text excerpts from the document.
  - If you are provided with page images, you are provided with images for the same document scope.
  - If you are provided with both, use the page images as context for the extracted text.
2. Analyze page images only to provide context for the extracted text excerpts.
  - If text excerpts cut off important surrounding context, use the page images to repair the cutoff.
  - Do not use image-only guesses when the value is not supported by the document content.
3. Carefully analyze the provided document content for the following configured fields:
- **payment_options** - Payment options as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **in_service_withdrawals** - In-service withdrawals as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **in_service_withdrawals_age** - Minimum age for in-service withdrawals. Only populated when in_service_withdrawals is 'Permitted'.
- **in_service_withdrawals_age_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when in_service_withdrawals_age resolves to 'Other'.
- **in_service_withdrawals_service_requirement** - Service requirement for in-service withdrawals, if permitted.
- **in_service_withdrawals_service_requirement_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when in_service_withdrawals_service_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
- **in_service_withdrawals_vesting_requirement** - Vesting requirement for in-service withdrawals, if permitted.
- **in_service_withdrawals_vesting_requirement_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when in_service_withdrawals_vesting_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
- **in_service_withdrawals_money_source_restrictions** - Money source restrictions for in-service withdrawals, if permitted.
- **in_service_withdrawals_money_source_restrictions_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when in_service_withdrawals_money_source_restrictions resolves to 'Other'.
- **hardship_distributions** - Hardship distributions as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **hardship_distributions_vesting_requirement** - Vesting requirement for hardship distributions, if permitted.
- **hardship_distributions_vesting_requirement_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when hardship_distributions_vesting_requirement resolves to 'Other'.
- **hardship_distributions_money_source_restrictions** - Money source restrictions for hardship distributions, if permitted. Use the closest listed source restriction when the document is explicit; otherwise capture the restriction in Other.
- **withdrawals_from_rollover** - Withdrawals from rollover account as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
- **withdrawals_from_rollover_other_specify** - Free text. Only populated when withdrawals_from_rollover resolves to 'Other'.
- **loans** - Participant loans as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
4. For each value you find, follow the formatting and condition instructions for that field.
  - Use the configured `Field` value as the JSON key.
  - If a value is absent or low confidence, follow the field-specific instruction. If there is no field-specific null rule, exclude that key.
  - Do not invent placeholder values such as "Not Provided", "N/A", or similar filler.
5. Construct the response for custom workflow step `adp_f6_withdrawals_and_distributions` in workflow group `adp_f6_withdrawals_and_distributions`.
  - Return one JSON object whose keys exactly match the output keys.
  - Return only keys from the configured output-key contract.
6. Return the JSON response, and only the JSON response.
  - It is critical that you respond with only JSON because I will parse your response as JSON and extraneous commentary or text will break the parser.

# Examples

<document_text>
This excerpt does not contain any configured field values.
</document_text>

<assistant_response>
{}
</assistant_response>

<document_content>
The source contains a configured value for one of the requested fields.
</document_content>

<assistant_response>
{"<configured_output_key>": "<source-supported value>"}
</assistant_response>

# Request

I am going to provide you with content from a document. I want you to analyze this content, extract the relevant information for the configured workflow group, and return it as a JSON object.

# Extraction Guidelines

Below are the relevant fields that I want you to extract from the content, including the key to use in your JSON response, the format of the JSON value, and examples or instructions for how the information may be identified. These identifiers and examples are guidance, not an exhaustive list.

- If page images are provided with extracted text excerpts, use the images as context for the extracted text. Focus your extraction on the provided document content.
- If extracted text cuts off important surrounding context, use the page image context to repair that issue.
- Extract only values supported by the document content. Do not infer values that are not supported by the source.
- Follow field-specific null, enum, condition, and formatting instructions. If a field has no specific null rule and you cannot identify it with confidence, exclude that key from the response.


# adp_f6_withdrawals_and_distributions Definition

Extract withdrawals and distributions fields from the plan document. Return null for fields whose conditions are not met. ADP adoption agreements are form documents. Many values are selected by a checkbox, tick mark, filled box, selected row, or similar visual mark. Use the page image to determine selected checkbox or selected-option state. Pair each selected mark with the label immediately next to it or governed by it. OCR can render a selected mark as a standalone line immediately before the selected option label; treat that as selecting the following option row when the page image confirms the alignment. When a standalone mark appears between two option labels, attach it to the following row, not the preceding row or first/default option. Default notes such as 'if no option is selected' apply only when the same option group has no visible selected mark. Ignore unselected labels even when they contain enum words. These custom steps run per page section. If this page section does not contain the deciding label, selected option, or table row for a field, return null/blank for that field instead of a negative enum value. Use negative values such as "Not Indicated", "N/A", "No Match", "Not Permitted", "No Profit Sharing", or false only when the current page section contains the relevant source evidence and that evidence explicitly supports the negative value. Do not infer values that are not supported by the document text. When the page image is unavailable, selection is encoded directly in the OCR text by checkbox glyphs placed immediately before each option: a checked box ☑ means that option IS selected; an empty box ☐ means it is NOT selected. Read the option label(s) immediately following a ☑ as the selected election, and ignore labels following ☐. An option whose fill-in blank contains a value (a number, percentage, or date) is selected; an option with an empty blank is not. For example, in 'Age ___ (not more than 21)' the '21' is only the maximum allowed — the elected age is the number filled into the blank (e.g., 18). Likewise 'After completing 3 consecutive Months of Eligibility Service' with a filled number is the selected service requirement.

# Field Descriptions

## payment_options

Field: payment_options
Description: Payment options as stated in the source plan or adoption agreement.
Type: str
Example Identifiers: Voluntary Distributions, Lump Sum, Installment Payments, Annuity Contracts
Special Instructions:
Output contract:
- Return a JSON object string with keys value and _raw_text, or null when absent/not applicable.
- Enum choices JSON: ["Lump Sum and Installments in cash", "Lump Sum, Installments and partial in cash", "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity", "Not Indicated"].

Label discovery:
- Find the Voluntary Distributions / Form of Distribution section.
- Use the distribution form / payment options section, not contribution-source or withdrawal-availability sections.

Selection evidence:
- Pair each selected Yes/No checkbox with its row: Lump Sum, Partial Payments, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts.
- Ignore row labels whose Yes option is unselected or whose No option is selected.

Value mapping:
- If Lump Sum and Installment Payments are selected Yes, and Partial Payments and Annuity Contracts are not selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum and Installments in cash".
- If Lump Sum, Partial Payments, and Installment Payments are selected Yes, and Annuity Contracts are not selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and partial in cash".
- If Lump Sum, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts are selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity".
- If Lump Sum, Partial Payments, Installment Payments, and Annuity Contracts are all selected Yes, return value "Lump Sum, Installments and Annuity" and preserve Partial Payments in _raw_text because the enum has no exact value for all four selected options.
- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the distribution form section is visible but selected forms cannot be determined.
- Return null when the current page/request does not contain the distribution form section.

Source evidence:
- Put the selected distribution form rows in _raw_text.

## in_service_withdrawals

Value mapping:

- Return value "Not Indicated" only when the current page section contains the in-service withdrawal label but no selected option is visible. If the in-service withdrawal section is not on the current page section, return null.
